Work Package B1.11: Captain Mock Diagnostics\r\n\r\nCreate the foundation of the future Captain diagnostics panel.\r\n\r\nFor the active mock attempt, show:\r\n\r\n* waypoint name;\r\n* waypoint version;\r\n* runtime mode;\r\n* attempt state;\r\n* platform adapter;\r\n* protocol version;\r\n* result;\r\n* guidance code;\r\n* evidence digest;\r\n* event-delivery status;\r\n* timestamps;\r\n* stale/duplicate rejection status.\r\n\r\nProvide development actions:\r\n\r\n* select mock scenario;\r\n* start attempt;\r\n* cancel attempt;\r\n* replay duplicate result;\r\n* simulate stale result;\r\n* manually approve or reject metadata where allowed.\r\n\r\nManual approval in B-1 should be clearly marked as development behavior and audited.\r\n\r\nDo not expose internal database IDs unnecessarily in the normal UI. Engineering diagnostics may show them.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 18. Work Package B1.12: Attempt State Machine\r\n\r\nImplement an explicit state machine.\r\n\r\nRequired states:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nIDLE\r\nARMED\r\nCAPTURING\r\nCURATING_FRAMES\r\nRETRIEVING\r\nMATCHING\r\nLOCALIZING\r\nEVALUATING_SEQUENCE\r\nEVALUATING_SPECIAL_RULES\r\nVERIFIED\r\nINSUFFICIENT\r\nNOT_AT_TARGET\r\nAMBIGUOUS\r\nERROR\r\nCANCELLED\r\nEVENT_DELIVERED\r\nRESULT_DISPLAYED\r\nCLOSED\r\n```\r\n\r\nIn B-1, the mock adapter simulates the intermediate states.\r\n\r\nRules:\r\n\r\n* only legal transitions are accepted;\r\n* transition history is auditable;\r\n* cancelled attempts cannot later verify;\r\n* stale attempts cannot deliver events;\r\n* verified results must pass current-stage validation;\r\n* result delivery is idempotent;\r\n* an error is not equivalent to `NOT_AT_TARGET`;\r\n* unavailable platform capability is not equivalent to a failed scan.\r\n\r\nAdd state-machine tests.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 19. Work Package B1.13: API and Service Endpoints\r\n\r\nImplement or extend server endpoints using the existing API conventions.\r\n\r\nRequired logical operations include:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nGET    /vision-waypoints\r\nPOST   /vision-waypoints\r\nGET    /vision-waypoints/:id\r\nPATCH  /vision-waypoints/:id\r\nPOST   /vision-waypoints/:id/archive\r\n\r\nPOST   /vision-waypoints/:id/versions\r\nGET    /vision-waypoints/:id/versions\r\nGET    /vision-waypoint-versions/:versionId\r\nPATCH  /vision-waypoint-versions/:versionId/draft\r\nPOST   /vision-waypoint-versions/:versionId/publish\r\nPOST   /vision-waypoint-versions/:versionId/deprecate\r\n\r\nGET    /vision-waypoints/:id/usage\r\n\r\nPOST   /story-waypoint-bindings\r\nPATCH  /story-waypoint-bindings/:id\r\nDELETE /story-waypoint-bindings/:id\r\n\r\nPOST   /verification-attempts\r\nGET    /verification-attempts/:id\r\nPOST   /verification-attempts/:id/cancel\r\nPOST   /verification-attempts/:id/mock-result\r\n```\r\n\r\nAdapt routes to existing conventions and avoid duplicating a current API style.\r\n\r\nRequirements:\r\n\r\n* runtime schema validation;\r\n* authentication;\r\n* authorization;\r\n* ownership and collaboration permissions;\r\n* immutable publication enforcement;\r\n* consistent errors;\r\n* audit logging;\r\n* pagination where relevant;\r\n* no leaking of pairing secrets or sensitive hashes.\r\n\r\nGenerate or update API documentation.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 20. Work Package B1.14: Permissions\r\n\r\nIntegrate with the projectâ€™s current account and role system.\r\n\r\nLogical permissions should include:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nvisionWaypoint.create\r\nvisionWaypoint.read\r\nvisionWaypoint.editDraft\r\nvisionWaypoint.publish\r\nvisionWaypoint.deprecate\r\nvisionWaypoint.bindToStory\r\nvisionWaypoint.viewDiagnostics\r\nvisionWaypoint.administer\r\n```\r\n\r\nA creator may edit their own permitted drafts.\r\n\r\nA Player must not edit waypoint definitions.\r\n\r\nA Captain may view active attempt diagnostics only when authorized for the Tall Tale session.\r\n\r\nA published package cannot be changed by ordinary edit permission.\r\n\r\nAdd permission tests.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 21. Work Package B1.15: Security Baseline\r\n\r\nImplement the B-1 security foundation.\r\n\r\n## Required controls\r\n\r\n* runtime input validation;\r\n* strict protocol schemas;\r\n* no unrestricted desktop-native command interface;\r\n* no arbitrary filesystem read/write commands;\r\n* no generic shell execution;\r\n* no secret tokens in logs;\r\n* no sensitive values in client-visible configuration;\r\n* secure random IDs and tokens;\r\n* expiring pairing-session design;\r\n* future localhost origin restriction represented in contracts;\r\n* package hash validation;\r\n* server authorization for publication and binding;\r\n* audit trail for manual Captain actions;\r\n* Content Security Policy compatible with web and desktop where practical.\r\n\r\nDocument threat assumptions for:\r\n\r\n* malicious webpage attempting to access a future Companion;\r\n* modified development package;\r\n* duplicate verification results;\r\n* stale verification results;\r\n* unauthorized waypoint mutation;\r\n* desktop command abuse;\r\n* service-worker cache leakage.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 22. Work Package B1.16: Diagnostics and Observability\r\n\r\nAdd structured diagnostics for the new foundation.\r\n\r\nRequired diagnostic information:\r\n\r\n* app version;\r\n* build ID;\r\n* platform;\r\n* adapter selected;\r\n* protocol version;\r\n* package-schema version;\r\n* feature flags;\r\n* waypoint ID and version where appropriate;\r\n* attempt ID;\r\n* lifecycle state;\r\n* event-delivery state;\r\n* desktop-shell version;\r\n* PWA version/cache state;\r\n* API compatibility.\r\n\r\nLogging requirements:\r\n\r\n* structured format;\r\n* correlation/request IDs;\r\n* no secrets;\r\n* no raw sensitive payload dumps;\r\n* clear separation of expected mock scenario from real error;\r\n* persisted server audit events for publication and binding changes.\r\n\r\nCreate a downloadable or copyable B-1 diagnostic summary where consistent with the existing app.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 23. Work Package B1.17: Testing\r\n\r\nImplement comprehensive automated testing.\r\n\r\n## 23.1 Domain tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* waypoint creation;\r\n* version creation;\r\n* draft editing;\r\n* actions.\r\n\r\n## 23.2 Migration tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* clean database migration;\r\n* migration over representative existing data;\r\n* foreign keys;\r\n* uniqueness;\r\n* downgrade where supported;\r\n* fixture creation.\r\n\r\n## 23.3 Protocol tests\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* all message examples;\r\n* invalid envelope;\r\n* unsupported protocol;\r\n* invalid result;\r\n* unknown error Mandatory Phase Demonstration\r\n\r\nB-1 cannot close until the following complete demonstration works.\r\n\r\n## Demonstration setup\r\n\r\nCreate fixture or development accounts and one simple Tall Tale containing a Vision Waypoint block.\r\n\r\n## Demonstration steps\r\n\r\n1. Launch the server and normal web application.\r\n2. Open Studio.\r\n3. Open the Vision Waypoint Library.\r\n4. Create a new `EXACT_LANDMARK` waypoint.\r\n5. Name it something clearly development-specific, such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nB1 Development Message Rock\r\n```\r\n\r\n6. Select `STORY_CRITICAL`.\r\n7. Save the draft.\r\n8. Publish development version 1 using a deterministic mock package.\r\n9. Confirm version 1 can no longer be edited.\r\n10. Create a new draft version from version 1.\r\n11. Confirm the draft can be edited without changing version 1.\r\n12. Open the story editor.\r\n13. Add or configure a Vision Waypoint block.\r\n14. Bind it to immutable version 1.\r\n15. Select `DEVELOPMENT_MOCK`.\r\n16. Launch the story as a Player in the browser.\r\n17. Activate the mock Inspect Surroundings interaction.\r\n18. Run the `VERIFIED` mock scenario.\r\n19. Confirm a VerificationAttempt is persisted.\r\n20. Confirm the story objective advances exactly once.\r\n21. Replay the same result.\r\n22. Confirm it is rejected as a duplicate and the story does not advance again.\r\n23. Run a new session with `INSUFFICIENT_VISUAL_EVIDENCE`.\r\n24. Confirm the story does not advance.\r\n25. Run `NOT_AT_TARGET`.\r\n26. Confirm the story does not advance.\r\n27. Run `AMBIGUOUS`.\r\n28. Confirm the story does not advance.\r\n29. Run a delayed result and move the story to a different stage before it returns.\r\n30. Confirm the result is rejected as stale.\r\n31. View the Captain diagnostics for the attempts.\r\n32. Launch the same shared application in the desktop shell.\r\n33. Repeat the verified mock flow using the DesktopPlatformAdapter.\r\n34. Confirm the desktop production build uses bundled frontend assets.\r\n35. Install or launch the PWA.\r\n36. Confirm the application shell works offline.\r\n37. Confirm no sensitive mutable API data is served from an unsafe stale cache.\r\n38. Confirm feature flags hide later unfinished phases.\r\n\r\nRecord this demonstration or provide detailed evidence with screenshots.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 26. Phase B-1 Exit Gate\r\n\r\nB-1 is complete only when all of the following are true.\r\n\r\n## Shared architecture\r\n\r\n* [ ] Web and desktop builds share the same frontend implementation.\r\n* [ ] No duplicate Player, Captain, or Studio applications were created.\r\n* [ ] Platform-specific behavior is isolated behind adapters.\r\n* [ ] The desktop production build does not depend on a remote production webpage or development server.\r\n* [ ] PWA support is installable and truthfully reports update/offline state.\r\n\r\n## Domain and persistence\r\n\r\n* [ ] Vision Waypoint identity and version entities exist.\r\n* [ ] Published versions are immutable.\r\n* [ ] Story bindings pin immutable versions.\r\n* [ ] Verification attempts are persisted.\r\n* [ ] Companion and pairing domain foundations exist.\r\n* [ ] Migrations preserve existing data and pass tests.\r\n\r\n## Protocol\r\n\r\n* [ ] Versioned protocol schemas exist.\r\n* [ ] Runtime validation exists.\r\n* [ ] Capability and error schemas exist.\r\n* [ ] Contract tests run across required runtimes.\r\n\r\n## Application behavior\r\n\r\n* [ ] Studio can create, version, publish, and inspect a development waypoint.\r\n* [ ] Story editor can bind a published waypoint version.\r\n* [ ] Player can invoke deterministic mock Inspect Surroundings.\r\n* [ ] Captain can inspect mock attempts.\r\n* [ ] Every mock result state behaves correctly.\r\n* [ ] Stale and duplicate results are safely rejected.\r\n* [ ] Verified result advances a story exactly once.\r\n\r\n## Security and privacy foundation\r\n\r\n* [ ] No unrestricted desktop command interface exists.\r\n* [ ] No secrets are logged.\r\n* [ ] Authorization exists for waypoint operations.\r\n* [ ] Package hashes and publication metadata exist.\r\n* [ ] PWA caching excludes sensitive mutable responses.\r\n* [ ] Future Companion origin restrictions are represented in contracts.\r\n\r\n## Testing\r\n\r\n* [ ] New unit tests pass.\r\n* [ ] New integration tests pass.\r\n* [ ] New end-to-end tests pass.\r\n* [ ] Desktop tests pass.\r\n* [ ] PWA tests pass.\r\n* [ ] Existing project regression suite passes.\r\n* [ ] Build and type checking pass.\r\n\r\n## Documentation\r\n\r\n* [ ] Repository audit is complete.\r\n* [ ] ADRs are complete.\r\n* [ ] Domain and protocol documentation is complete.\r\n* [ ] Desktop and PWA setup documentation is accurate.\r\n* [ ] Demonstration instructions are repeatable.\r\n* [ ] Completion report contains all required evidence.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 27. Explicitly Out of Scope for B-1\r\n\r\nDo not implement the following during this phase unless a tiny scaffold is strictly necessary for the B-1 contract:\r\n\r\n* Windows Graphics Capture;\r\n* real Sea of Thieves window selection;\r\n* global scan hotkeys;\r\n* frame buffers;\r\n* screenshot or video capture;\r\n* creator target recordings;\r\n* hard-negative recordings;\r\n* frame-quality algorithms;\r\n* 3D reconstruction;\r\n* DINOv2;\r\n* SALAD;\r\n* AnyLoc;\r\n* SuperPoint;\r\n* LightGlue;\r\n* LoFTR;\r\n* COLMAP;\r\n* OpenCV pose estimation;\r\n* ONNX Runtime models;\r\n* calibrated thresholds;\r\n* real shadow verification;\r\n* automatic story progression from AI;\r\n* public installers and code signing;\r\n* cloud vision build infrastructure;\r\n* live AR effects.\r\n\r\nThese belong to later phases.\r\n\r\nDo not opportunistically begin B-2 and leave B-1 incomplete.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 28. Prohibited Shortcuts\r\n\r\nDo not:\r\n\r\n* create only empty package folders;\r\n* create only TypeScript interfaces without runtime validation;\r\n* use an in-memory waypoint store;\r\n* use random mock results;\r\n* let shared UI import native APIs directly;\r\n* point the desktop app at the production website;\r\n* copy the frontend into a second desktop implementation;\r\n* mutate a published version;\r\n* bind a story to a floating â€œlatest versionâ€ reference;\r\n* deliver a result without checking active story state;\r\n* advance a story twice from duplicate messages;\r\n* label an error as `NOT_AT_TARGET`;\r\n* allow local feature flags to bypass server authorization;\r\n* cache authentication or sensitive mutable APIs in the service worker;\r\n* expose generic shell or filesystem commands from the desktop app;\r\n* declare the phase complete because the application builds;\r\n* postpone tests and documentation to a fictional cleanup phase.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 29. Implementation Quality Expectations\r\n\r\nUse:\r\n\r\n* strong typing;\r\n* runtime validation;\r\n* explicit state machines;\r\n* normalized database relationships;\r\n* migrations;\r\n* idempotent events;\r\n* narrow interfaces;\r\n* dependency injection or equivalent adapter selection;\r\n* accessible UI;\r\n* secure defaults;\r\n* structured logging;\r\n* reproducible builds;\r\n* useful errors;\r\n* existing project style and naming conventions.\r\n\r\nAvoid:\r\n\r\n* large god services;\r\n* platform checks scattered across components;\r\n* hidden global mutable state;\r\n* unversioned JSON blobs;\r\n* duplicated schemas;\r\n* magic strings;\r\n* silent fallback;\r\n* fake status indicators;\r\n* misleading success states.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 30. flow;\r\n* navigate Player, Captain, and Studio routes;\r\n* expose desktop capability information;\r\n* invoke the DesktopPlatformAdapter;\r\n* support development and production configuration;\r\n* package a local frontend build rather than merely pointing to the production website;\r\n* include application identity, icon placeholders, and version information;\r\n* start and close cleanly;\r\n* access.\r\n\r\n## 11.2 Desktop-native command boundary\r\n\r\nCreate narrowly scoped commands such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nget_desktop_capabilities\r\ninvoke_mock_verification\r\nget_application_version\r\nopen_application_data_directory\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not expose generic shell execution, arbitrary file reads, or unrestricted command invocation.\r\n\r\n## 11.3 Development mode\r\n\r\nDocument how to run:\r\n\r\n* web frontend only;\r\n* server;\r\n* desktop shell with shared frontend;\r\n* automated desktop tests where supported.\r\n\r\nOne command or documented coordinated command set should launch the desktop development environment.\r\n\r\n## 11.4 Production build\r\n\r\nProduce a development-quality Windows build artifact or prove the full build pipeline works in the available environment.\r\n\r\nPublic code signing and final installer hardening belong to B-6.\r\n\r\nB-1 must still verify:\r\n\r\n* application bundles;\r\n* frontend assets are local;\r\n* version information is correct;\r\n* the app does not require the development server in production mode.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 12. Work Package B1.6: Progressive Web App Foundation\r\n\r\nAdd or complete PWA support without compromising data truth or security.\r\n\r\n## 12.1 Required PWA capabilities\r\n\r\n* application manifest;\r\n* icons or project-consistent placeholders;\r\n* standalone display mode;\r\n* application name and theme metadata;\r\n* service-worker strategy;\r\n* safe update detection;\r\n* offline application shell;\r\n* fallback page for unavailable content;\r\n* installability verification.\r\n\r\n## 12.2 Caching boundaries\r\n\r\nThe service worker may cache:\r\n\r\n* hashed frontend assets;\r\n* icons;\r\n* fonts already licensed and bundled;\r\n* safe public static assets;\r\n* application shell;\r\n* explicitly downloaded story packages in later phases.\r\n\r\nIt must not indiscriminately cache:\r\n\r\n* authentication responses;\r\n* sensitive API responses;\r\n* mutable admin data;\r\n* unversioned story state;\r\n* verification attempts;\r\n* Companion secrets;\r\n* pairing tokens.\r\n\r\nDocument the chosen cache strategy in an ADR.\r\n\r\n## 12.3 Update truth\r\n\r\nDo not present â€œUpdated just nowâ€ unless an actual update occurred.\r\n\r\nThe PWA must distinguish:\r\n\r\n```text\r\napplication version\r\nlast successful server synchronization\r\nlast downloaded story-package version\r\noffline status\r\nupdate available\r\n```\r\n===== LINES 981-1228 =====\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 13. Work Package B1.7: Feature Flags\r\n\r\nImplement a centralized feature-flag system.\r\n\r\nRequired flags:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nvision_waypoints\r\ndesktop_shell\r\npwa_install\r\ncreator_capture\r\nvision_build_engine\r\nplayer_hold_to_scan\r\nshadow_verification\r\nautomatic_progression\r\nlive_external_ar\r\n```\r\n\r\nB-1 expected defaults:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nvision_waypoints = enabled in development/test\r\ndesktop_shell = enabled in desktop build\r\npwa_install = enabled\r\ncreator_capture = disabled\r\nvision_build_engine = disabled\r\nplayer_hold_to_scan = mock/development only\r\nshadow_verification = disabled\r\nautomatic_progression = disabled\r\nlive_external_ar = disabled\r\n```\r\n\r\nRequirements:\r\n\r\n* flags must be typed;\r\n* flags must have documented defaults;\r\n* server-enforced flags must not be bypassed by changing local storage;\r\n* UI must not show unavailable production functionality;\r\n* tests must verify disabled behavior;\r\n* flag state must be visible in diagnostics;\r\n* no scattered string literals.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 14. Work Package B1.8: Studio Vision Waypoint Foundation\r\n\r\nCreate the initial Studio capability needed for the B-1 vertical slice.\r\n\r\nThis is not the full B-3 authoring wizard.\r\n\r\n## 14.1 Vision Waypoint Library\r\n\r\nImplement a foundational library view containing:\r\n\r\n* waypoint name;\r\n* type;\r\n* lifecycle state;\r\n* draft version;\r\n* published versions;\r\n* verification profile;\r\n* story usage count;\r\n* sharing scope;\r\n* last modified;\r\n* create/open actions.\r\n\r\nThe view must use real persisted data.\r\n\r\n## 14.2 Minimal waypoint editor\r\n\r\nProvide enough functionality to:\r\n\r\n* create a waypoint;\r\n* edit stable metadata;\r\n* select type;\r\n* select verification profile;\r\n* create or edit the current draft configuration;\r\n* publish a development package version;\r\n* create a new draft from the published version;\r\n* verify published immutability;\r\n* inspect version history.\r\n\r\nThe full recording workflow belongs to B-3.\r\n\r\n## 14.3 Development publication\r\n\r\nB-1 may create a deterministic development package artifact such as:\r\n\r\n```json\r\n{\r\n  \"packageSchemaVersion\": 1,\r\n  \"packageType\": \"development-mock\",\r\n  \"waypointId\": \"vp_...\",\r\n  \"waypointVersion\": 1,\r\n  \"mockScenario\": \"verified\"\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe package must:\r\n\r\n* be hashed;\r\n* be immutable after publication;\r\n* validate against a schema;\r\n* be referenced by the published version.\r\n\r\nClearly label it as a mock development package.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 15. Work Package B1.9: Story Block Integration\r\n\r\nIntegrate Vision Waypoints into the existing story editor and runtime without replacing the current story architecture.\r\n\r\n## 15.1 Story editor block\r\n\r\nAdd a Vision Waypoint verification block or equivalent node.\r\n\r\nIt must allow:\r\n\r\n* selecting an existing waypoint;\r\n* selecting one immutable published version;\r\n* showing version and lifecycle state;\r\n* selecting `DEVELOPMENT_MOCK` runtime mode;\r\n* configuring success output;\r\n* configuring retry and failure text;\r\n* enabling Captain fallback metadata;\r\n* warning when a draft rather than published version is selected;\r\n* warning when a referenced version is deprecated.\r\n\r\n## 15.2 Runtime activation\r\n\r\nWhen the story reaches the block:\r\n\r\n* create an active verification context;\r\n* bind it to current story/stage;\r\n* render the mock Inspect Surroundings interaction;\r\n* invoke the selected platform adapter;\r\n* persist a VerificationAttempt;\r\n* process the result;\r\n* emit the correct story event;\r\n* advance only after a valid `VERIFIED` result;\r\n* avoid duplicate advancement.\r\n\r\n## 15.3 Idempotency\r\n\r\nThe story engine must use an idempotency key based on stable values such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nstory session\r\nstage or block\r\nwaypoint version\r\nverification attempt\r\nevent type\r\n```\r\n\r\nA duplicate result message must not:\r\n\r\n* complete the objective twice;\r\n* play irreversible effects twice;\r\n* create duplicate history entries;\r\n* unlock multiple copies of the next stage.\r\n\r\nAdd automated tests.\r\n\r\n## 15.4 Stale-stage protection\r\n\r\nBefore delivering a result:\r\n\r\n* confirm the story session still exists;\r\n* confirm the stage is still active;\r\n* confirm the waypoint version still matches;\r\n* confirm the attempt has not been cancelled or replaced.\r\n\r\nA delayed result from a previous stage must be rejected as stale.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 16. Work Package B1.10: Player Mock Interaction\r\n\r\nCreate a polished development-only version of the future hold-to-scan interface.\r\n\r\nRequirements:\r\n\r\n* visually integrated with the current Tall Tale interface;\r\n* clearly labeled as development mock in non-production diagnostics;\r\n* configurable hold control;\r\n* progress reflects deterministic mock timing;\r\n* cancellation works;\r\n* result states render distinctly;\r\n* reduced-motion behavior exists;\r\n* no fake â€œAI confidenceâ€ is shown to normal users;\r\n* screen-reader and keyboard support exist.\r\n\r\nPlayer-facing result states:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nVERIFIED\r\nINSUFFICIENT_VISUAL_EVIDENCE\r\nNOT_AT_TARGET\r\nAMBIGUOUS\r\nSYSTEM_ERROR\r\nCANCELLED\r\n```\r\n\r\nSuggested story-safe text may be seeded, but the story editor must be able to override it.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 17. Work Package B1.11: Captain Mock Diagnostics\r\n\r\nCreate the foundation of the future Captain diagnostics panel.\r\n\r\nFor the active mock attempt, show:\r\n\r\n* waypoint name;\r\n* waypoint version;\r\n* runtime mode;\r\n* attempt state;\r\n* platform adapter;\r\n* protocol version;\r\n* result;\r\n* guidance code;\r\n* evidence digest;\r\n* event-delivery status;\r\n* timestamps;\r\n* stale/duplicate rejection status.\r\n\r\nProvide development actions:\r\n\r\n* select mock scenario;\r\n* start attempt;\r\n* cancel attempt;\r\n* replay duplicate result;\r\n* simulate stale result;\r\n* manually approve or reject metadata where allowed.\r\n\r\nManual approval in B-1 should be clearly marked as development behavior and audited.\r\n\r\nDo not expose internal database IDs unnecessarily in the normal UI. Engineering diagnostics may show them.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 18. Work Package B1.12: Attempt State Machine\r\n\r\nImplement an explicit state machine.\r\n\r\n===== LINES 1229-1418 =====\r\nRequired states:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nIDLE\r\nARMED\r\nCAPTURING\r\nCURATING_FRAMES\r\nRETRIEVING\r\nMATCHING\r\nLOCALIZING\r\nEVALUATING_SEQUENCE\r\nEVALUATING_SPECIAL_RULES\r\nVERIFIED\r\nINSUFFICIENT\r\nNOT_AT_TARGET\r\nAMBIGUOUS\r\nERROR\r\nCANCELLED\r\nEVENT_DELIVERED\r\nRESULT_DISPLAYED\r\nCLOSED\r\n```\r\n\r\nIn B-1, the mock adapter simulates the intermediate states.\r\n\r\nRules:\r\n\r\n* only legal transitions are accepted;\r\n* transition history is auditable;\r\n* cancelled attempts cannot later verify;\r\n* stale attempts cannot deliver events;\r\n* verified results must pass current-stage validation;\r\n* result delivery is idempotent;\r\n* an error is not equivalent to `NOT_AT_TARGET`;\r\n* unavailable platform capability is not equivalent to a failed scan.\r\n\r\nAdd state-machine tests.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 19. Work Package B1.13: API and Service Endpoints\r\n\r\nImplement or extend server endpoints using the existing API conventions.\r\n\r\nRequired logical operations include:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nGET    /vision-waypoints\r\nPOST   /vision-waypoints\r\nGET    /vision-waypoints/:id\r\nPATCH  /vision-waypoints/:id\r\nPOST   /vision-waypoints/:id/archive\r\n\r\nPOST   /vision-waypoints/:id/versions\r\nGET    /vision-waypoints/:id/versions\r\nGET    /vision-waypoint-versions/:versionId\r\nPATCH  /vision-waypoint-versions/:versionId/draft\r\nPOST   /vision-waypoint-versions/:versionId/publish\r\nPOST   /vision-waypoint-versions/:versionId/deprecate\r\n\r\nGET    /vision-waypoints/:id/usage\r\n\r\nPOST   /story-waypoint-bindings\r\nPATCH  /story-waypoint-bindings/:id\r\nDELETE /story-waypoint-bindings/:id\r\n\r\nPOST   /verification-attempts\r\nGET    /verification-attempts/:id\r\nPOST   /verification-attempts/:id/cancel\r\nPOST   /verification-attempts/:id/mock-result\r\n```\r\n\r\nAdapt routes to existing conventions and avoid duplicating a current API style.\r\n\r\nRequirements:\r\n\r\n* runtime schema validation;\r\n* authentication;\r\n* authorization;\r\n* ownership and collaboration permissions;\r\n* immutable publication enforcement;\r\n* consistent errors;\r\n* audit logging;\r\n* pagination where relevant;\r\n* no leaking of pairing secrets or sensitive hashes.\r\n\r\nGenerate or update API documentation.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 20. Work Package B1.14: Permissions\r\n\r\nIntegrate with the projectâ€™s current account and role system.\r\n\r\nLogical permissions should include:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nvisionWaypoint.create\r\nvisionWaypoint.read\r\nvisionWaypoint.editDraft\r\nvisionWaypoint.publish\r\nvisionWaypoint.deprecate\r\nvisionWaypoint.bindToStory\r\nvisionWaypoint.viewDiagnostics\r\nvisionWaypoint.administer\r\n```\r\n\r\nA creator may edit their own permitted drafts.\r\n\r\nA Player must not edit waypoint definitions.\r\n\r\nA Captain may view active attempt diagnostics only when authorized for the Tall Tale session.\r\n\r\nA published package cannot be changed by ordinary edit permission.\r\n\r\nAdd permission tests.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 21. Work Package B1.15: Security Baseline\r\n\r\nImplement the B-1 security foundation.\r\n\r\n## Required controls\r\n\r\n* runtime input validation;\r\n* strict protocol schemas;\r\n* no unrestricted desktop-native command interface;\r\n* no arbitrary filesystem read/write commands;\r\n* no generic shell execution;\r\n* no secret tokens in logs;\r\n* no sensitive values in client-visible configuration;\r\n* secure random IDs and tokens;\r\n* expiring pairing-session design;\r\n* future localhost origin restriction represented in contracts;\r\n* package hash validation;\r\n* server authorization for publication and binding;\r\n* audit trail for manual Captain actions;\r\n* Content Security Policy compatible with web and desktop where practical.\r\n\r\nDocument threat assumptions for:\r\n\r\n* malicious webpage attempting to access a future Companion;\r\n* modified development package;\r\n* duplicate verification results;\r\n* stale verification results;\r\n* unauthorized waypoint mutation;\r\n* desktop command abuse;\r\n* service-worker cache leakage.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 22. for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ncompanion.hello\r\ncompanion.capabilities\r\ncompanion.pair.request\r\ncompanion.pair.approved\r\ncompanion.pair.rejected\r\ncompanion.status\r\n\r\ncapture.target.select.request\r\ncapture.target.selected\r\ncapture.target.unavailable\r\n\r\ncapture.creator.start\r\ncapture.creator.progress\r\ncapture.creator.complete\r\ncapture.creator.cancel\r\ncapture.creator.failed\r\n\r\nruntime.scan.start\r\nruntime.scan.progress\r\nruntime.scan.cancel\r\nruntime.scan.result\r\nruntime.scan.failed\r\n\r\nbuild.request\r\nbuild.progress\r\nbuild.complete\r\nbuild.failed\r\n\r\nvision.pause\r\nvision.resume\r\n\r\ndiagnostic.bundle.request\r\ndiagnostic.bundle.available\r\n```\r\n\r\nB-1 only needs to execute mock runtime-scan messages, but all core families should have initial schemas.\r\n\r\n## 9.3 Capability schema\r\n\r\nSupport capability reporting Mandatory Phase Demonstration\r\n\r\nB-1 cannot close until the following complete demonstration works.\r\n\r\n## Demonstration setup\r\n\r\nCreate fixture or development accounts and one simple Tall Tale containing a Vision Waypoint block.\r\n\r\n## Demonstration steps\r\n\r\n1. Launch the server and normal web application.\r\n2. Open Studio.\r\n3. Open the Vision Waypoint Library.\r\n4. Create a new `EXACT_LANDMARK` waypoint.\r\n5. Name it something clearly development-specific, such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nB1 Development Message Rock\r\n```\r\n\r\n6. Select `STORY_CRITICAL`.\r\n7. Save the draft.\r\n8. Confirm the draft can be edited without changing version 1.\r\n12. Open the story editor.\r\n13. Add or configure a Vision Waypoint block.\r\n14. Bind it to immutable version 1.\r\n15. Select `DEVELOPMENT_MOCK`.\r\n16. Launch the story as a Player in the browser.\r\n17. Activate the mock Inspect Surroundings interaction.\r\n18. Run the `VERIFIED` mock scenario.\r\n19. Confirm a VerificationAttempt is persisted.\r\n20. Confirm the story objective advances exactly once.\r\n21. Replay the same result.\r\n22. Confirm it is rejected as a duplicate and the story does not advance again.\r\n23. Run a new session with `INSUFFICIENT_VISUAL_EVIDENCE`.\r\n24. Confirm the story does not advance.\r\n25. Run `NOT_AT_TARGET`.\r\n26. Confirm the story does not advance.\r\n27. Run `AMBIGUOUS`.\r\n28. Confirm the story does not advance.\r\n29. Run a delayed result and move the story to a different stage before it returns.\r\n30. Confirm the result is rejected as stale.\r\n31. View the Captain diagnostics for the attempts.\r\n32. Launch the same shared application in the desktop shell.\r\n33. Repeat the verified mock flow using the DesktopPlatformAdapter.\r\n34. Confirm the desktop production build uses bundled frontend assets.\r\n35. Install or launch the PWA.\r\n36. Confirm the application shell works offline.\r\n37. Confirm no sensitive mutable API data is served from an unsafe stale cache.\r\n38. Confirm feature flags hide later unfinished phases.\r\n\r\nRecord this demonstration or provide detailed evidence with screenshots.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 26. version entities exist.\r\n* [ ] Published versions are immutable.\r\n* [ ] Story bindings pin immutable versions.\r\n* [ ] Verification attempts are persisted.\r\n* [ ] Companion and pairing domain 7/18/2026 12:33:13 AM vitest.setup.ts               \r\n# Repository instructions\n\n## Shared development documentation\n\n`Development_Docs` at the repository root is the canonical shared location for development requirements, project history, architecture, decisions, implementation plans, and related authored documentation.\n\nBefore beginning work that depends on those materials:\n\n1. Confirm the repository, active branch, upstream, and complete working-tree state.\n2. Preserve any local modifications, then run `git fetch --prune origin`.\n3. Determine whether the branch is behind, ahead, or diverged with `git rev-list --left-right --count \"HEAD...@{upstream}\"`.\n4. Pull with `--ff-only`, or rebase when the repository's branch policy permits it, only when the operation is demonstrably safe. Never discard local work to synchronize.\n5. If the same document changed on two computers, preserve both sets of work and surface conflicts. Perform a normal textual merge only when clearly correct. Keep clearly differentiated variants of unmergeable binary documents when appropriate, and never push unresolved conflict markers.\n\nNever use `git reset --hard`, `git clean -fd`, `git checkout -- .`, `git restore .`, `git push --force`, or `git push --force-with-lease` as synchronization shortcuts.\n\nInspect `Development_Docs` before work when project history, requirements, architecture, decisions, or plans are relevant. Update a relevant document when an implementation decision makes it inaccurate, but do not modify unrelated documents merely because they were read. Preserve authored content and native formatting, avoid normalization churn, and do not use the directory for routine terminal output or generated test/build artifacts. Never upload secrets, credentials, private configuration, production data, or unapproved sensitive company information.\n\n## Mandatory task finalization\n\nFor every Codex task performed in this repository, conversation and development-document synchronization is a required finalization gate. Do not delete or weaken these steps:\n\n1. Complete and test the requested project work.\n2. Inspect the complete working tree and classify task changes, `Development_Docs` changes, `Codex_Chats` changes, unrelated pre-existing work, and generated files. Never stage the whole repository by default.\n3. Run `python scripts/sync_codex_chats.py --dry-run`. Review conversation classifications and the structured `development_docs` status, including eligible, excluded, suspicious, large, conflicted, renamed, and deleted paths.\n4. Run `python scripts/sync_codex_chats.py` to ingest every currently accessible project-associated Codex session and any newer export in `Codex_Chats/imports/`, and to commit eligible `Development_Docs` changes through the same path-scoped workflow.\n5. Run `python scripts/sync_codex_chats.py --validate`, inspect the exact synchronization-owned Git diff, and re-check the complete repository status.\n6. Confirm each command's exit status and structured report. Never claim a conversation was archived unless a source was read and a transcript was written successfully. Never claim documentation was synchronized unless the reported commit and push state prove it.\n7. If synchronization content changed, confirm the scoped commit and permitted push succeeded and verify the remote SHA. If nothing changed, confirm no empty commit was created. Report unrelated remaining changes accurately.\n8. Include concise `Chat archive:` and `Development docs:` result lines in the final response.\n\nThe synchronizer is deliberately not a `post-commit` hook because many prompts finish without a code commit. The Codex host currently exposes no repository task-completion hook that can be versioned here, so this instruction is authoritative. A task's final assistant message can only be captured on the next synchronization because it does not exist until after the pre-finalization run; the next task must update that same stable conversation rather than creating a duplicate.\n\n`Development_Docs` remains a normal Git-tracked directory. Do not copy it into `Codex_Chats`, archive it as transcripts, or maintain a duplicate mirror. The synchronizer may commit only explicit eligible paths; it must leave ignored, suspicious, oversized, conflicted, and unrelated files untouched. Do not commit raw ChatGPT exports or bypass a secret warning, unsafe Git state, ambiguous classification, source failure, integrity failure, branch divergence, or protected-branch policy.\n\r\n# Codex handoff\n\n- Canonical repository: `Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT`\n- Integrated branches: `feature/game-master-command-center` and `feature/cinematic-animation-rebuild`\n- Integration method: normal merge commits on `main`, preserving both source histories\n- Status: expanded Command Center, complete Player Companion, cinematic animation architecture, and Tall Tale Studio share one integrated tree\n- Command Center routes: `/quartermaster` plus `/chapters`, `/hints`, `/voyage`, `/artifacts`, `/quests`, `/journal`, `/events`, `/player-view`, `/recovery`, `/audit`, `/diagnostics`\n- Authentication: GM bcrypt/database session and CSRF; player sessions cannot call admin APIs\n- Commands: prepare/release/solve/complete chapter, prepare/release hint, reveal map, award artifact, discover/advance quest, release journal entry, pause/resume, undo, reconciliation\n- Migration: Phase 2 `20260716223000_player_companion_shell`, then Phase 3 `20260716233000_game_master_command_center`; connector-parity MySQL SQL exists and is wired through `npm run db:migrate:mysql:command-center`\n- Development data: Phase 2 multi-state presets plus Phase 3 prepared-action and audit fixtures; normal launcher restarts preserve campaign state, while `npm run db:preset -- <preset>` is the explicit reset path\n- Startup: `npm run dev:full`; validation: `npm run validate`; stop: `npm run Codex, human\r\ndevelopers, designers, testers, and future contributors MUST use it as the primary\r\nsource of truth. Implementation details may improve, but product behavior , safety\r\nboundaries, data ownership, workflow expectations, verification philosophy , and re-\r\nlease gates MUST NOT be silently changed.\r\nThis specification consolidates the complete design developed for:\r\nâ€¢ passive cinematic scene transformations;\r\nâ€¢ active screen-based location verification;\r\nâ€¢ highly accurate multi-model visual localization;\r\nâ€¢ reusable Studio-authored Vision Waypoints;\r\nâ€¢ a local Capture Companion that observes only the selected game window;\r\nâ€¢ versioned waypoint packages that can be built once and reused without custom coding;\r\nâ€¢ player-friendly verification that does not expose computer-vision machinery;\r\nâ€¢ Captain controls, diagnostics, shadow testing, and manual fallback;\r\nâ€¢ privacy , anti-cheat, reliability , calibration, publishing, and long-term maintainability .\r\nThe governing intent is to prevent implementation drift. A future developer must not re-\r\nplace this architecture with a one-image classifier merely because it is easier , nor embed\r\none-off scripts inside individual Tall Tales, nor require creators to manipulate training fold-\r\ners, command lines, or Python notebooks. The product is a creator tool , not a collection\r\nof clever prototypes held together by Codex prompts and optimism.\r\nNormative keywords\r\nThe words MUST, MUST NOT , SHOULD, SHOULD NOT , and MAY are normative:\r\nâ€¢ MUST / MUST NOT : mandatory for conformance.\r\nâ€¢ SHOULD / SHOULD NOT : expected unless a documented architectural decision ex-\r\nplains the exception.\r\nâ€¢ MAY: optional behavior that must not conflict with mandatory requirements.\r\ni\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 3 =====\r\n\r\nVISION WAYPOINT & EXTERNAL AR GOVERNING SPECIFICATION v1.0\r\nChange control\r\nAny material departure from this specification requires an Architecture Decision Record,\r\nor ADR, containing:\r\n1. the requirement being changed;\r\n2. the reason the existing requirement is inadequate;\r\n3. alternatives considered;\r\n4. effects on creator workflow , player experience, privacy , performance, safety , and com-\r\npatibility;\r\n5. migration and rollback plans;\r\n6. explicit approval before merging.\r\nA library substitution that preserves all governing behavior does not require a product-\r\nspecification rewrite, but it SHOULD receive a technical ADR. For example, replacing\r\nSALAD with a better place-recognition model is acceptable if the hard-negative, ambiguity ,\r\ncalibration, and package contracts remain intact. Replacing hierarchical verification with\r\na single opaque confidence score is not acceptable.\r\nii\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 4 =====\r\n\r\nDocument Control\r\nField Governing value\r\nProject Tall Tale Studio / Date Nights\r\nCapability Vision Waypoints and external augmented\r\nreality\r\nBaseline Version 1.0\r\nOwner Project Captain / Studio product owner\r\nPrimary implementer Codex-assisted development under human\r\nreview\r\nCurrent phase Passive presentation complete; guided\r\nactive verification next\r\nPrimary platform Windows creator/player Companion plus\r\nbrowser-based Studio and Player surfaces\r\nInitial game integration Sea of Thieves, read-only visual\r\nobservation only\r\nReliability posture False-positive-averse, ambiguity-rejecting,\r\nguided-retry design\r\nPrivacy posture Local capture and inference by default; no\r\nretained footage by default\r\nModification boundary No game injection, memory reading, file\r\nmodification, automation, or in-game\r\noverlay\r\niii\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 5 =====\r\n\r\nHow to Read This Specification\r\nReaders implementing one piece at a time should use the following order:\r\n1. It will also establish a shared application | architecture so the current website can be delivered as a normal web application, an installable Progressive W eb App, and | a full Windows desktop application without becoming three divergent products. | The complete Phase B result will allow a creator to: | 1. open the Studio; | 2. create a reusable Vision W aypoint;\r\n17: VISION WAYPOINT PHASE B IMPLEMENTATION ROADMAP v1.0 | Phase Name Primary outcome | B-5 Player, Story, and Captain Integration Hold-to-scan gameplay, Phase A | reveal triggering, shadow mode, | diagnostics, overrides, and offline | continuity | B-6 Hardening and Public Creator | Release\r\n18: Chapter 1 | Phase B Product Boundary | 1.1 Definition of Phase B complete | Phase B is complete when the product supports all of the following in a stable, documented, and repeatable manner: | â€¢ reusable Vision W aypoints created within Studio; | â€¢ a native Companion capable of selecting and capturing the Sea of Thieves window without touching the game process; | â€¢ creator target, boundary, environmental-variation, and hard-negative recordings; | â€¢ player-initiated multi-frame hold-to-scan verification;\r\n19: VISION WAYPOINT PHASE B IMPLEMENTATION ROADMAP v1.0 | 1.3 Phase B completion demonstration | The final demonstration MUST show one complete T all T ale path in which: | 1. a Vision W aypoint is created entirely through Studio; | 2. the waypoint is built and tested against positives and hard negatives; | 3. a published immutable version is attached to a story block; | 4. a player launches the T all T ale in the Windows desktop application or website paired to the Companion; | 5. the player holds Inspect Surroundings at the correct location;\r\n20: Chapter 2 | Governing Delivery Principles | 2.1 Vertical value in every phase | Every phase MUST end with a demonstrable product slice. A phase may contain architecture work, but it may not close | with only diagrams, placeholder APIs, static UI, or unexercised schemas. | 2.2 Shared product, not parallel rewrites | The website, PW A, and desktop application MUST share the same UI, domain logic, story engine, data contracts, and | design system wherever the platform permits. Platform-specific behavior MUST be isolated behind adapters.\r\n21: VISION WAYPOINT PHASE B IMPLEMENTATION ROADMAP v1.0 | 2.8 Phase exit gates are binary | A phase is either complete or not complete. A green interface does not compensate for missing backend behavior. A | passing unit test does not compensate for a broken end-to-end demonstration. Exit evidence MUST include automated | tests, human-visible behavior, and updated documentation. | 4\r\n22: Chapter 3 | Overall System Architecture | 3.1 Logical components | Shared Backend Services | | | +------------------+------------------+ | | | | Browser / PWA Windows Desktop App\r\n23: VISION WAYPOINT PHASE B IMPLEMENTATION ROADMAP v1.0 | 3.3 Recommended repository shape | The exact current repository layout may be migrated incrementally, but the final architecture SHOULD resemble: | apps/ | web/ | desktop/ | server/ | vision-worker/\r\n24: Chapter 4 | Website, PWA, and Desktop Strategy | 4.1 The product shall use three delivery surfaces | 4.1.1 Browser website | The website remains the universal access surface for: | â€¢ account creation and authentication; | â€¢ Player, Captain, and Studio access from any supported browser; | â€¢ invitation and collaboration flows;\r\n25: VISION WAYPOINT PHASE B IMPLEMENTATION ROADMAP v1.0 | â€¢ controlled cinematic presentation in a dedicated window. | 4.2 One codebase, multiple shells | The desktop application MUST NOT become an independent rewrite. paired local device identity and capabilities;\r\nâ€¢ CompanionPairingSession - short-lived pairing authorization.\r\n7.6.2 Identity rules\r\nâ€¢ A waypoint has a stable ID independent of its name.\r\nâ€¢ A waypoint version has its own immutable ID.\r\nâ€¢ Recordings and tests are attached to a specific draft version.\r\nâ€¢ A published story references a specific published waypoint version.\r\nâ€¢ Renaming a waypoint does not change its identity.\r\nâ€¢ Deleting a draft cannot invalidate a published story.\r\nâ€¢ Archived waypoints remain resolvable when referenced by history.\r\n7.6.3 Version rules\r\nA waypoint SHALL support:\r\nStable Waypoint\r\nVersion 1 - ## Tool Result: call_KJU2vfCA2jUKpL1ZKDHIYyXV

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 1.9 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 1.6 seconds\nOutput:\n===== PAGE 13 =====\r\n\r\nChapter 1\r\nExecutive Summary\r\nThe Tall Tale companion will support story moments in which the real Sea of Thieves view\r\ntransitions into a modified, cinematic, or interactive interpretation. A player may reach\r\na rock and see a faint message appear carved into it, hold an ordinary mermaid gem and\r\nwatch it transform into the storyâ€™s true artifact, discover a hidden symbol, or trigger a\r\njournal reveal only after reaching and inspecting the correct place.\r\nPhase A provides the passive presentation framework: the story system can trigger pre-\r\npared images, videos, audio, Three.js scenes, or transitions after a manual Captain action\r\nor another trusted event. Phase B adds player-initiated automatic verification. The player\r\nholds an Inspect Surroundings control for several seconds while slowly scanning the\r\nscene. A local Companion captures a short burst from the selected Sea of Thieves window ,\r\ndiscards unusable frames, and proves the location through multiple independent layers:\r\n1. current story-stage restriction;\r\n2. capture-quality validation;\r\n3. global visual place retrieval;\r\n4. comparison against known similar wrong places;\r\n5. local feature matching;\r\n6. geometric verification;\r\n7. camera-pose localization within a small 3D reconstruction;\r\n8. spatial distribution of evidence;\r\n9. consistency across several frames;\r\n10. checkpoint-specific rules.\r\nThe verifier MUST never unlock from one screenshot, one model, or one weighted confi-\r\ndence score. A wrong result can destroy the story , so ambiguity is rejected. At the same\r\ntime, legitimate users must not be punished by brittle exact-image matching. The system\r\ntherefore localizes a new camera view inside a reconstructed target scene and provides\r\nguided retries when evidence is incomplete.\r\nMost importantly , the entire system must be reusable. A creator must be able to build a\r\nVision Waypoint inside the Studio through a guided wizard. The creator records the cor-\r\nrect location, walks the valid player region, records confusing wrong locations, confirms\r\nimportant visual regions, runs positive and negative tests, and publishes a versioned pack-\r\nage. The Studio and Companion perform frame extraction, mapping, feature generation,\r\ncalibration, testing, and packaging automatically . Codex builds the tool once; creators use\r\nit repeatedly .\r\n1\r\n\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 14 =====\r\n\r\nChapter 2\r\nProduct Vision and Core Objectives\r\n2.1 Product vision\r\nThe player should feel that the Tall Tale companion is not merely waiting for a button\r\npress. It should appear to perceive the journey , recognize meaningful places and objects,\r\nand reveal a hidden narrative layer at exactly the right moment.\r\nThe creator should feel as though they are using a polished game editor . Creating a visually\r\nverified story event should be comparable to creating a checkpoint, adding a cinematic, or\r\nplacing a trigger in a modern level editor . It must not feel like training a machine-learning\r\nmodel.\r\n2.2 Primary objectives\r\nThe system MUST:\r\nâ€¢ enable automatic verification of story-critical game locations and visual conditions;\r\nâ€¢ minimize false positives strongly enough that a wrong location does not prematurely\r\ntrigger a reveal;\r\nâ€¢ avoid exact-view brittleness by supporting reasonable variation in player position, angle,\r\ndistance, field of view , resolution, lighting, weather , and graphical settings;\r\nâ€¢ guide legitimate players toward successful verification instead of returning generic fail-\r\nures;\r\nâ€¢ allow nontechnical creators to author , test, reuse, version, and share Vision Waypoints\r\nin the Studio;\r\nâ€¢ keep the Sea of Thieves process untouched;\r\nâ€¢ perform runtime processing locally by default;\r\nâ€¢ attach verification results to a general event-driven story engine rather than hard-code\r\ncinematics into the verifier;\r\nâ€¢ preserve Captain oversight and manual recovery;\r\nâ€¢ allow gradual promotion from development to shadow mode, Captain-confirmed mode,\r\nand fully automatic progression;\r\nâ€¢ support future live external-AR effects without requiring the core authoring system to\r\nbe rebuilt.\r\n2\r\n\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 15 =====\r\n\r\nVISION WAYPOINT & EXTERNAL AR GOVERNING SPECIFICATION v1.0\r\n2.3 Quality hierarchy\r\nWhen tradeoffs occur , the governing priority is:\r\n1. prevent false story progression;\r\n2. preserve player trust and privacy;\r\n3. provide a successful guided retry for legitimate players;\r\n4. keep creator authoring simple;\r\n5. maintain explainability and diagnostics;\r\n6. optimize performance;\r\n7. add spectacle.\r\nA beautiful ten-second reveal is valuable. A beautiful ten-second reveal triggered by the\r\nwrong palm tree is accidental comedy and therefore not a release criterion.\r\n3\r\n\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 16 =====\r\n\r\nChapter 3\r\nGoverning Principles\r\n3.1 The verifier proves; it does not guess\r\nThe system MUST use mandatory evidence gates. A high score from one stage cannot\r\ncompensate for a failed geometric, pose, negative-margin, or temporal gate.\r\n3.2 Current story context narrows the problem\r\nThe system MUST inspect only the currently active waypoint and its explicit confusers. It\r\nis not required to classify the entire Sea of Thieves world continuously .\r\n3.3 Multiâ€frame evidence is the default\r\nPhase B MUST use a short guided scan, not a single screenshot. Multiple frames provide\r\nview diversity , obstruction recovery , motion evidence, and sequence consistency .\r\n3.4 Geometry outranks appearance\r\nGlobal similarity may retrieve candidates, but geometric evidence and camera localization\r\nMUST determine whether the physical scene is consistent with the target.\r\n3.5 Hard negatives are firstâ€class data\r\nCreators MUST record similar wrong places for high-reliability waypoints. The system\r\nMUST compare target confidence against known confusers and refuse close ties.\r\n3.6 Ambiguity is a valid result\r\nThe internal outcome set is:\r\nâ€¢ VERIFIED;\r\nâ€¢ INSUFFICIENT_VISUAL_EVIDENCE;\r\nâ€¢ NOT_AT_TARGET.\r\n4\r\n\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 17 =====\r\n\r\nVISION WAYPOINT & EXTERNAL AR GOVERNING SPECIFICATION v1.0\r\nThe verifier MUST NOT force every attempt into success or failure when the evidence is\r\nambiguous.\r\n3.7 Story logic is separate from recognition logic\r\nA Vision Waypoint proves a condition. The story graph decides what happens next. The\r\nsame waypoint may trigger different outcomes in different Tall Tales.\r\n3.8 The creator sees guidance, not computerâ€vision jargon\r\nThe default Studio experience MUST use language such as â€œrecord more of the right side,â€\r\nâ€œcapture another similar rock,â€ and â€œmove farther away .â€ Raw model thresholds belong in\r\nengineering diagnostics.\r\n3.9 Published behavior is versioned\r\nA published Tall Tale MUST reference an immutable waypoint version. Updating a library\r\nwaypoint MUST NOT silently alter a published story .\r\n3.10 Safety boundaries are architectural, not optional settings\r\nThe Companion MUST NOT inject into the game, read process memory , alter game files,\r\nsimulate input, or render an in-game overlay . These are prohibited implementation paths,\r\nnot merely disabled defaults.\r\n5\r\n\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 18 =====\r\n\r\nChapter 4\r\nScope and Nonâ€Goals\r\n4.1 In scope\r\nThis specification covers:\r\nâ€¢ passive image and video reveals;\r\nâ€¢ view-matched pre-rendered reveals;\r\nâ€¢ external augmented-reality presentation in the companion surface;\r\nâ€¢ creator-authored visual location verification;\r\nâ€¢ exact landmarks, area arrival, viewpoints, object inspection, held-item conditions, and\r\nsequences;\r\nâ€¢ local screen capture of a user-selected game window;\r\nâ€¢ local and optional cloud-assisted waypoint building;\r\nâ€¢ global place recognition, hard-negative comparison, feature matching, 3D reconstruc-\r\ntion, camera-pose localization, temporal consistency , and checkpoint-specific rules;\r\nâ€¢ Studio UX, Companion UX, Player UX, Captain controls, diagnostics, packages,\r\nschemas, testing, publishing, and lifecycle management.\r\n4.2 Explicitly out of scope for the governing baseline\r\nThe baseline MUST NOT depend on:\r\nâ€¢ game memory inspection;\r\nâ€¢ modification of Sea of Thieves files or rendering pipeline;\r\nâ€¢ code injection, DLL hooks, ReShade, or an in-game overlay;\r\nâ€¢ automated player movement, mouse control, key simulation, or gameplay automation;\r\nâ€¢ competitive intelligence, enemy detection, loot highlighting, fog removal, or navigation\r\nassistance unrelated to the authored story;\r\nâ€¢ continuous global recognition of every island and object;\r\nâ€¢ mandatory custom model training per waypoint;\r\nâ€¢ mandatory cloud upload of gameplay footage;\r\nâ€¢ public marketplace moderation infrastructure in the first implementation phase.\r\nThese exclusions protect safety , product focus, and the playerâ€™s account. Humans have\r\nalready invented enough ways for software to become unexpectedly bannable.\r\n6\r\n\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 19 =====\r\n\r\nChapter 5\r\nT erminology\r\nTerm Governing definition\r\nVision Waypoint A reusable, versioned Studio asset that\r\nproves a visual location, viewpoint, object,\r\nitem, area, or sequence condition.\r\nCheckpoint A runtime instance of a Vision Waypoint\r\nattached to a story stage.\r\nTarget recording Creator footage captured at the correct\r\nlocation and acceptable viewpoints.\r\nHard negative A deliberately recorded wrong place or\r\ncondition that closely resembles the target.\r\nBorderline positive A valid but difficult viewpoint near the\r\nedge of acceptance.\r\nAccepted pose volume The approved 3D camera positions and\r\nviewing orientations from which\r\nverification may succeed.\r\nStable region A fixed visual area suitable for matching,\r\nsuch as rock cracks or architecture.\r\nIgnored region Dynamic or unreliable imagery such as sky ,\r\nwater , particles, players, HUD, or foliage.\r\nGlobal descriptor A compact representation used to retrieve\r\nvisually similar reference views.\r\nLocal feature A distinctive point or semi-dense\r\ncorrespondence used to verify detailed\r\ngeometry .\r\nInlier A feature match consistent with the\r\nestimated geometric transformation.\r\nReprojection error The disagreement between observed image\r\npoints and points predicted by the\r\nestimated camera geometry .\r\nSparse 3D map A Structure-from-Motion reconstruction of\r\nstable visual landmarks and reference\r\ncamera poses.\r\n7\r\n\r\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

A Creator MUST be able to complete\r\nthe normal workflow without command-line tools, source code, model terminology , or di-\r\nrect filesystem manipulation.\r\n6.2 Captain\r\nThe Captain operates a live Tall Tale, monitors exceptional verification attempts, receives\r\noptional notifications, and may approve, reject, or manually trigger an event. The Captain\r\nmay also be the Creator , but the roles remain separate in the product model.\r\n6.3 Player\r\nThe Player sees only story-appropriate instructions and feedback. The Player SHOULD\r\nNOT see match counts, thresholds, coordinates, or model names.\r\n6.4 Studio modes\r\n6.4.1 Guided mode\r\nDefault mode for ordinary creators. The system chooses the recognition strategy and\r\nthresholds, provides capture instructions, and blocks unsafe publication.\r\n6.4.2 Detailed mode\r\nAllows region masking, pose-volume editing, required landmarks, strictness controls, test-\r\ncase management, and deeper reliability review .\r\n6.4.3 Engineering mode\r\nAllows model selection, feature visualizations, match inliers, pose estimates, score distri-\r\nbutions, calibration curves, and runtime profiling. This mode exists because some people\r\nsee an â€œ Advanced Diagnosticsâ€ disclosure and regard it as a personal invitation.\r\n9\r\n\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 22 =====\r\n\r\nVISION WAYPOINT & EXTERNAL AR GOVERNING SPECIFICATION v1.0\r\n6.5 Runtime modes\r\nMode Behavior\r\nManual Captain triggers the presentation without\r\nautomatic vision.\r\nShadow Vision evaluates attempts but cannot\r\nprogress the story .\r\nCaptain-confirmed Vision proposes a result; Captain approves\r\nprogression.\r\nAutomatic Certified verification progresses the story\r\ndirectly .\r\nDisabled Waypoint is retained in the story graph but\r\nnot currently available.\r\n10\r\n\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 23 =====\r\n\r\nChapter 7\r\nSystem Architecture\r\nFigure 7.1: System context and data flow\r\n7.1 Major components\r\n7.1.1 T all T ale Studio\r\nThe browser-based Studio owns:\r\nâ€¢ waypoint library and metadata;\r\nâ€¢ creation wizard;\r\nâ€¢ target and negative recording orchestration;\r\nâ€¢ coverage visualization;\r\nâ€¢ accepted-pose editor;\r\nâ€¢ stable-region and ignore-region editing;\r\nâ€¢ reliability dashboards;\r\nâ€¢ test management;\r\nâ€¢ versioning and publishing;\r\nâ€¢ story-block attachment;\r\nâ€¢ creator permissions and sharing scope.\r\n7.1.2 Local Vision Companion\r\nThe local Companion owns:\r\nâ€¢ window selection and secure screen capture;\r\nâ€¢ pairing with the Studio and Player web surfaces;\r\nâ€¢ authoring recording sessions;\r\nâ€¢ local frame buffering;\r\nâ€¢ quality checks and live capture guidance;\r\nâ€¢ local runtime inference;\r\n11\r\n\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 24 =====\r\n\r\nVISION WAYPOINT & EXTERNAL AR GOVERNING SPECIFICATION v1.0\r\nâ€¢ hardware capability detection;\r\nâ€¢ local package cache;\r\nâ€¢ privacy controls and capture indicators;\r\nâ€¢ diagnostic evidence collection when authorized.\r\n7.1.3 Vision Build Engine\r\nThe Build Engine owns:\r\nâ€¢ frame extraction;\r\nâ€¢ blur and exposure rejection;\r\nâ€¢ near-duplicate removal;\r\nâ€¢ visual diversity analysis;\r\nâ€¢ descriptor and feature generation;\r\nâ€¢ sparse scene reconstruction;\r\nâ€¢ accepted-region estimation;\r\nâ€¢ negative indexing;\r\nâ€¢ threshold calibration;\r\nâ€¢ automated offline testing;\r\nâ€¢ package assembly and signing;\r\nâ€¢ build reports and actionable remediation.\r\nThe Build Engine MAY run locally or in a cloud-assisted environment. The contracts and\r\noutputs MUST remain identical.\r\n7.1.4 Vision Waypoint Package\r\nA package is the immutable runtime artifact containing:\r\nâ€¢ manifest and schema version;\r\nâ€¢ thumbnail and creator-facing metadata;\r\nâ€¢ selected reference descriptors;\r\nâ€¢ local features and 3D map data;\r\nâ€¢ stable and ignored region masks;\r\nâ€¢ hard-negative index;\r\nâ€¢ accepted pose volume;\r\nâ€¢ verification rules and thresholds;\r\nâ€¢ player guidance strings;\r\nâ€¢ reliability certification metadata;\r\nâ€¢ package integrity information.\r\nRaw creator recordings MUST NOT be required at runtime.\r\n7.1.5 Player web experience\r\nThe Player surface owns:\r\nâ€¢ story instructions;\r\nâ€¢ scan initiation and progress;\r\nâ€¢ feedback messages;\r\nâ€¢ event-driven presentation;\r\n12\r\n\r\n\r\n===== PAGE 25 =====\r\n\r\nVISION WAYPOINT & EXTERNAL AR GOVERNING SPECIFICATION v1.0\r\nâ€¢ journal and history updates;\r\nâ€¢ fallback messaging when the Companion is absent or disabled.\r\n7.1.6 Captain controls\r\nThe Captain surface owns:\r\nâ€¢ verification attempt queue;\r\nâ€¢ current checkpoint status;\r\nâ€¢ evidence summary;\r\nâ€¢ manual approve, reject, and trigger actions;\r\nâ€¢ mode promotion and demotion where permitted;\r\nâ€¢ privacy-conscious diagnostics;\r\nâ€¢ live recovery .\r\n13\r\n\r\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

\"3.15.0\",\n    \"lottie-web\": \"5.13.0\",\n    \"motion\": \"12.42.2\",\n    \"next\": \"16.2.10\",\n    \"page-flip\": \"2.0.7\",\n    \"pino\": \"10.1.0\",\n    \"qrcode\": \"1.5.4\",\n \"@tailwindcss/postcss\": \"^4\",\n    \"@testing-library/jest-dom\": \"6.9.1\",\n    \"@testing-library/react\": \"16.3.0\",\n    \"@types/node\": \"^20\",\n    \"@types/qrcode\": \"1.5.5\",\n    \"@types/react\": \"^19\",\n    \"@types/react-dom\": \"^19\",\n    \"eslint\": \"^9\",\n    \"eslint-config-next\": \"16.2.10\",\n    \"jsdom\": \"27.2.0\",\n    \"prettier\": \"3.6.2\",\n    \"prisma\": \"6.19.3\",\n    \"tailwindcss\": \"^4\",\n    \"tsx\": \"4.20.6\",\n    \"typescript\": \"^5\",\n    \"vitest\": \"4.1.10\"\n  },\n  \"overrides\": {\n    \"next\": {\n      \"postcss\": \"8.5.10\"\n    }\n  }\n}\n\r\nimport type { NextConfig } from \"next\";\n\nconst nextConfig: NextConfig = {\n  /* config options here */\n};\n\nexport default nextConfig;\n\r\n{\n  \"compilerOptions\": {\n    \"target\": \"ES2017\",\n    \"lib\": [\"dom\", \"dom.iterable\", \"esnext\"],\n    \"allowJs\": true,\n    \"skipLibCheck\": true,\n    \"strict\": true,\n    \"noEmit\":
