## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-18T04:48:00.495Z

The audit is now written before any code change. Its key implementation choice is additive integration: keep the shared Next.js product, reuse the existing authoritative verification/progression seam, and package that same app in a locally bundled Electron shell because this server-rendered Prisma application cannot be safely reduced to static files. I’m recording the ten required ADRs before changing runtime code.

## Tool Call: exec
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-18T04:49:02.558Z

const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\ADRs\\0001-shared-web-pwa-desktop-application.md\n+# ADR 0001: Shared web, PWA, and desktop application\n+\n+Status: accepted for Phase B-1\n+\n+## Context\n+\n+Player, Captain, and Studio already share one Next.js App Router application, domain model, design system, API, and story engine. Phase B requires browser, PWA, and Windows delivery without divergent products.\n+\n+## Decision\n+\n+Keep the current Next.js application as the canonical product. The PWA registers against it, and the desktop shell packages and launches its production standalone output. No Player, Captain, Studio, authentication, or story implementation is copied into a second frontend.\n+\n+## Alternatives considered\n+\n+- Split the repository into several applications immediately: rejected because it would move stable code without improving the B-1 vertical slice.\n+- Build a separate desktop React application: rejected because it would drift and violate the governing shared-product rule.\n+\n+## Consequences\n+\n+All surfaces inherit the same routes and UI changes. Desktop packaging includes the application server runtime as well as frontend assets.\n+\n+## Migration implications\n+\n+Shared Vision modules are added under `src/vision`; a future monorepo extraction may preserve these boundaries without changing contracts.\n+\n+## Security implications\n+\n+Desktop content remains local, while authentication, authorization, CSRF, and server validation remain unchanged.\n+\n+## Compatibility implications\n+\n+Existing browser routes and deployments continue to work. Desktop-specific behavior is available only through the adapter and allowlisted preload bridge.\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\ADRs\\0002-electron-desktop-shell.md\n+# ADR 0002: Electron desktop shell\n+\n+Status: accepted for Phase B-1\n+\n+## Context\n+\n+Tauri 2 is preferred when a frontend can be bundled as static assets or paired with a justified sidecar. This repository uses dynamic Next.js server components, route handlers, Prisma, database-backed sessions, and filesystem-backed assets. The audited Windows host has Node but no Rust toolchain. Tauri would still require a bundled Node server sidecar.\n+\n+## Decision\n+\n+Use Electron as a narrow Windows shell. It launches the packaged Next standalone server on loopback, renders that local application, uses one-instance locking, disables renderer Node integration, enables context isolation and sandboxing, and exposes only fixed IPC commands through a preload bridge.\n+\n+## Alternatives considered\n+\n+- Tauri 2 plus Node sidecar: rejected for B-1 because it adds Rust and Node native packaging while retaining the same server requirement.\n+- Remote production URL wrapper: prohibited because it is not a local production bundle.\n+- Static export: rejected because it cannot preserve the existing authenticated dynamic application.\n+\n+## Consequences\n+\n+The artifact is larger than Tauri but uses one existing runtime family and packages the actual application. Electron-builder becomes a development dependency.\n+\n+## Migration implications\n+\n+The `VisionPlatformAdapter` contract permits a future shell substitution. No domain or UI type depends on Electron.\n+\n+## Security implications\n+\n+No generic shell, filesystem, process, or arbitrary IPC command is exposed. Navigation and window creation are restricted, and production loads only the owned loopback server.\n+\n+## Compatibility implications\n+\n+The development shell uses the normal Next development URL; production uses bundled standalone output and does not require a separately installed development server.\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\ADRs\\0003-vision-platform-adapters.md\n+# ADR 0003: Vision platform adapters\n+\n+Status: accepted for Phase B-1\n+\n+## Context\n+\n+The shared UI must work in browser, PWA, and desktop environments while future native capture remains outside B-1.\n+\n+## Decision\n+\n+Define one `VisionPlatformAdapter` contract and select `MockVisionPlatformAdapter`, `WebCompanionPlatformAdapter`, or `DesktopPlatformAdapter` through a factory. Components depend only on the contract. Desktop bridge calls and future localhost communication stay inside adapters.\n+\n+## Alternatives considered\n+\n+- Platform checks inside React components: rejected because capability behavior would scatter.\n+- A single adapter with optional methods: rejected because unavailable and incompatible states would become ambiguous.\n+\n+## Consequences\n+\n+Capability reporting and error behavior are explicit and testable. B-2 can replace an adapter without redesigning UI or story logic.\n+\n+## Migration implications\n+\n+Existing helper APIs remain compatible and may later back a Companion adapter.\n+\n+## Security implications\n+\n+Native APIs are never imported by shared UI. Capability truth prevents a browser from pretending native capture exists.\n+\n+## Compatibility implications\n+\n+All adapters consume the same versioned protocol and return the same governed results.\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\ADRs\\0004-waypoint-version-immutability.md\n+# ADR 0004: Vision Waypoint version immutability\n+\n+Status: accepted for Phase B-1\n+\n+## Context\n+\n+One reusable waypoint may serve many stories. Editing a published version in place would silently change active and historical playthrough behavior.\n+\n+## Decision\n+\n+Separate stable `VisionWaypoint` identity from numbered `VisionWaypointVersion` records. Only unpublished draft versions are editable. Publication creates a one-to-one immutable publication record, deterministic package artifact and SHA-256 hash. New work derives a draft with `parentVersionId`. Story bindings pin the exact published version.\n+\n+## Alternatives considered\n+\n+- Store the latest configuration on the stable waypoint: rejected because stories would float.\n+- Copy configuration into every story only: rejected because reuse, usage lookup, and governed package identity would be lost.\n+\n+## Consequences\n+\n+Edits require explicit new drafts and story migration. Deprecated versions remain resolvable.\n+\n+## Migration implications\n+\n+The schema is additive and does not modify existing Tall Tale publication rows.\n+\n+## Security implications\n+\n+Publication and binding require server authorization. Artifact hashes detect modified development packages.\n+\n+## Compatibility implications\n+\n+Package schema and compatibility metadata are versioned independently of database migrations.\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\ADRs\\0005-zod-vision-protocol.md\n+# ADR 0005: Zod protocol serialization and validation\n+\n+Status: accepted for Phase B-1\n+\n+## Context\n+\n+Vision messages will cross web, desktop, Companion, worker, and server boundaries. TypeScript-only interfaces provide no runtime protection.\n+\n+## Decision\n+\n+Use strict Zod schemas as the canonical protocol implementation. A versioned common envelope, discriminated message types, capabilities, governed errors, package manifests, and examples are shared by all B-1 runtimes. JSON remains the wire format.\n+\n+## Alternatives considered\n+\n+- Informal interfaces: rejected because invalid runtime messages would pass unchecked.\n+- A second schema technology: rejected because Zod is already a core repository dependency and route convention.\n+\n+## Consequences\n+\n+Schemas provide inferred TypeScript types and runtime parsing. Contract snapshots guard drift.\n+\n+## Migration implications\n+\n+Protocol major/minor compatibility is separate from application/database versioning.\n+\n+## Security implications\n+\n+Unknown types, versions, enum values, and unrecognized fields fail closed at boundaries.\n+\n+## Compatibility implications\n+\n+Examples are plain JSON and can be validated by future non-TypeScript implementations against generated JSON Schema/OpenAPI documentation.\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\ADRs\\0006-pwa-cache-boundaries.md\n+# ADR 0006: PWA cache boundaries\n+\n+Status: accepted for Phase B-1\n+\n+## Context\n+\n+An installable offline shell is required, but stale authenticated or mutable data would misrepresent story truth and leak sensitive state.\n+\n+## Decision\n+\n+Use a versioned service-worker cache with an explicit static allowlist. Cache the offline document, manifest, icons, same-origin hashed `/_next/static/*` assets, and approved public assets. Treat `/api/*`, authentication, Player/Captain/Studio data, attempts, pairing, and mutable navigation as network-only. Navigations use network-first with offline fallback.\n+\n+## Alternatives considered\n+\n+- Cache-first for all GET requests: rejected as unsafe.\n+- No service worker: rejected because it cannot satisfy installability and offline-shell requirements.\n+\n+## Consequences\n+\n+The shell can open offline while protected live content truthfully reports unavailable. Cache version and update state are exposed in diagnostics.\n+\n+## Migration implications\n+\n+Later downloaded story packages require a separate versioned cache and explicit user action.\n+\n+## Security implications\n+\n+Sensitive mutable responses never enter Cache Storage. Logout does not rely on cache eviction for secrecy.\n+\n+## Compatibility implications\n+\n+Unsupported browsers continue as normal web applications without false install status.\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\ADRs\\0007-deterministic-mock-verification.md\n+# ADR 0007: Deterministic mock verification\n+\n+Status: accepted for Phase B-1\n+\n+## Context\n+\n+B-1 authorizes mocks only for capture and visual inference. Persistence, adapter invocation, protocol validation, lifecycle, stale checks, and event delivery must be real.\n+\n+## Decision\n+\n+Implement named deterministic scenarios with fixed result/guidance mappings and a stable SHA-256 evidence digest. The adapter sends real protocol envelopes, the server persists attempts/transitions, and accepted results enter the existing progression engine. Duplicate and stale scenarios exercise the real rejection paths.\n+\n+## Alternatives considered\n+\n+- Random confidence/results: rejected because tests and demonstrations would be irreproducible.\n+- In-memory fake store: prohibited because it bypasses persistence and audit truth.\n+\n+## Consequences\n+\n+Every scenario is repeatable. The expected mock outcome is labeled separately from internal failures.\n+\n+## Migration implications\n+\n+The mock adapter can be disabled or removed after later real adapters pass the same contract suite.\n+\n+## Security implications\n+\n+Mock execution is server-flagged and production-disabled by default. It grants no generic progression command.\n+\n+## Compatibility implications\n+\n+Results use the same protocol, attempt state, and story event contracts as future inference.\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\ADRs\\0008-typed-feature-flags.md\n+# ADR 0008: Typed feature flags\n+\n+Status: accepted for Phase B-1\n+\n+## Context\n+\n+Later Phase B capabilities must remain hidden and unauthorized while B-1 mock surfaces are available in development and test.\n+\n+## Decision\n+\n+Define all Vision flags in one typed registry with documented environment defaults. Server modules enforce feature availability and permissions. A serializable public subset controls presentation and adapter selection but cannot grant server access.\n+\n+## Alternatives considered\n+\n+- Local-storage flags: rejected because they are user-modifiable and unaudited.\n+- Scattered environment checks: rejected because defaults and diagnostics would drift.\n+\n+## Consequences\n+\n+Flags appear in diagnostics and have enabled/disabled tests. Production defaults keep unfinished capture, build, shadow, automatic, and live AR features off.\n+\n+## Migration implications\n+\n+Flags can later move to a persistent deployment service while preserving names and types.\n+\n+## Security implications\n+\n+Changing client state cannot bypass server authorization or production mock restrictions.\n+\n+## Compatibility implications\n+\n+Unknown environment values fail to documented safe defaults.\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\ADRs\\0009-verification-event-idempotency.md\n+# ADR 0009: Verification event idempotency and stale protection\n+\n+Status: accepted for Phase B-1\n+\n+## Context\n+\n+Retries, reconnects, delayed results, and duplicate delivery must not advance a story twice or complete a stale stage.\n+\n+## Decision\n+\n+Give every attempt a stable unique idempotency key derived from session, block, waypoint version, attempt, and event type. Immediately before delivery, recheck the active session, block, pinned story version, waypoint version, request state, and cancellation state. Route accepted results through existing unique `TaleVerificationEvent` and `TaleSessionEvent` keys. Persist duplicate/stale rejection metadata on the attempt.\n+\n+## Alternatives considered\n+\n+- Client-side deduplication: rejected because clients can retry, reconnect, or be duplicated.\n+- Marking the attempt complete before story delivery: rejected because partial failure would be ambiguous.\n+\n+## Consequences\n+\n+Duplicate replay returns the prior outcome, creates no second progression history, and remains observable. Stale results close without delivery.\n+\n+## Migration implications\n+\n+Existing story idempotency remains unchanged; Vision attempts add another durable correlation layer.\n+\n+## Security implications\n+\n+Replayed or cross-stage results cannot unlock content.\n+\n+## Compatibility implications\n+\n+Future adapters must preserve attempt and message identity across retries.\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\ADRs\\0010-vision-database-entities.md\n+# ADR 0010: Vision database entity design\n+\n+Status: accepted for Phase B-1\n+\n+## Context\n+\n+The governing model requires reusable identities, immutable versions, future authoring/build foundations, story bindings, attempts, evidence, devices, and pairing without model-specific coupling.\n+\n+## Decision\n+\n+Add normalized Prisma entities for waypoint identity/version/publication, draft capture/recording/region/pose/negative/build/artifact/certification/test foundations, story bindings, attempts and ordered transitions, evidence bundles, custom verification profiles, Companion devices, and expiring pairing sessions. JSON columns store only schema-versioned configuration or extensible metadata; identity and referential rules remain relational.\n+\n+## Alternatives considered\n+\n+- One JSON waypoint table: rejected because relations, immutability, usage, and lifecycle constraints would be weak.\n+- Store artifacts in database blobs: rejected; records hold hashes, sizes, and storage references.\n+\n+## Consequences\n+\n+The schema is larger in B-1 but avoids breaking redesign in B-2 through B-4. Services operationalize only the B-1 subset.\n+\n+## Migration implications\n+\n+SQLite and MySQL receive matching additive migrations, indexes, foreign keys, and uniqueness constraints. Existing records are untouched. Rollback is application rollback plus matched database backup restore because repository policy uses forward-only production SQL.\n+\n+## Security implications\n+\n+Pairing stores hashes only; raw frame payloads are not stored by default; ownership and collaboration checks remain server-side.\n+\n+## Compatibility implications\n+\n+Model/library versions live in build/certification metadata, not story blocks or stable waypoint records.\n*** End Patch";
text(await tools.apply_patch(patch));

[id], onDelete: Cascade)\n+\n+  @@index([attemptId, createdAt])\n+  @@index([expiresAt, deletedAt])\n+}\n+\n+model CompanionDevice {\n+  id              String    @id @default(cuid())\n+  ownerId pairingSessions PairingSession[]\n+\n+  @@index([ownerId, revokedAt, lastSeenAt])\n+}\n+\n+model PairingSession {\n+  id                String    @id @default(cuid())\n+  ownerId           String\n+  companionDeviceId String\n+  pairingCodeHash   String    @unique\n+  sessionSecretHash String    @unique\n+  allowedOrigin     String\n+  expiresAt         DateTime\n+  pairedAt          DateTime?\n+  revokedAt         DateTime?\n+  createdAt         DateTime  @default(now())\n+  owner             GameMasterUser @relation(\"PairingSessionOwner\", fields: [ownerId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n+  companionDevice   CompanionDevice @relation(fields: [companionDeviceId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n+\n+  @@index([ownerId, expiresAt, revokedAt])\n+  @@index([companionDeviceId, expiresAt])\n+}\n*** End Patch";
text(await tools.apply_patch(patch));

Cascade)\n+  pairingSessions PairingSession[]\n+\n+  @@index([ownerId, revokedAt, lastSeenAt])\n+}\n+\n+model PairingSession {\n+  id                String    @id @default(cuid())\n+  ownerId DateTime?\n+  revokedAt         DateTime?\n+  createdAt         DateTime  @default(now())\n+  owner             GameMasterUser @relation(\"PairingSessionOwner\", fields: [ownerId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n+  companionDevice   CompanionDevice @relation(fields: [companionDeviceId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)\n+\n+  @@index([ownerId, expiresAt, revokedAt])\n+  @@index([companionDeviceId, expiresAt])\n+}\n*** End Patch";
text(await tools.apply_patch(patch));

32) => randomBytes(bytes).toString(\"base64url\");\r\nexport function safeEqual(a: string, b: string) {\r\n  const left = Buffer.from(a);\r\n  const right = Buffer.from(b);\r\n  return left.length === right.length && timingSafeEqual(left, right);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function setPlayerSession(playerAccessId: string) {\r\n  const jar = await cookies();\r\n  jar.set(PLAYER_COOKIE, playerAccessId, {\r\n    httpOnly: true,\r\n    sameSite: \"strict\",\r\n    secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\",\r\n    path: \"/\",\r\n    maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 30,\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function requirePlayer(campaignSlug: string) {\r\n  const jar = await cookies();\r\n  const id = jar.get(PLAYER_COOKIE)?.value;\r\n  if (!id) return null;\r\n  return db.playerAccess.findFirst({\r\n    where: { id, campaign: { slug: campaignSlug }, OR: [{ expiresAt: null }, { expiresAt: { gt: new Date() } }] },\r\n    include: { campaign: true },\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function createGmSession(userId: string) {\r\n  const id = makeToken();\r\n  const csrfToken = makeToken(24);\r\n  await db.gameMasterSession.create({\r\n    data: { id: hashToken(id), userId, csrfToken, expiresAt: new Date(Date.now() + sessionAge) },\r\n  });\r\n  const jar = await cookies();\r\n  jar.set(GM_COOKIE, id, {\r\n    httpOnly: true,\r\n    sameSite: \"strict\",\r\n    secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === \"production\",\r\n    path: \"/\",\r\n    maxAge: sessionAge / 1000,\r\n  });\r\n  return csrfToken;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function requireGm() {\r\n  const raw = (await cookies()).get(GM_COOKIE)?.value;\r\n  if (!raw) return null;\r\n  return db.gameMasterSession.findFirst({\r\n    where: { id: hashToken(raw), expiresAt: { gt: new Date() } },\r\n    include: { user: true },\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport type GmCapability = \"CAPTAIN\" | \"CREATE_TALES\" | \"PUBLISH_TALES\" | \"MANAGE_ASSETS\";\r\n\r\nconst roleCapabilities: Record<string, GmCapability[]> = {\r\n  CAPTAIN: [\"CAPTAIN\"],\r\n  CREATOR: [\"CREATE_TALES\", \"MANAGE_ASSETS\"],\r\n  PUBLISHER: [\"CREATE_TALES\", \"PUBLISH_TALES\", \"MANAGE_ASSETS\"],\r\n  CAPTAIN_CREATOR: [\"CAPTAIN\", \"CREATE_TALES\", \"PUBLISH_TALES\", \"MANAGE_ASSETS\"],\r\n};\r\n\r\nexport function gmCan(user: { role: string; capabilities: string }, capability: GmCapability) {\r\n  let explicit: string[] = [];\r\n  try {\r\n    const parsed = JSON.parse(user.capabilities);\r\n    if (Array.isArray(parsed)) explicit = parsed.filter((item): item is string => typeof item === \"string\");\r\n  } catch {}\r\n  return roleCapabilities[user.role]?.includes(capability) || explicit.includes(capability);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function requireGmCapability(capability: GmCapability) {\r\n  const session = await requireGm();\r\n  return session && gmCan(session.user, capability) ? session : null;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function verifyCsrf(session: { csrfToken: string }) {\r\n  const provided = (await headers()).get(\"x-csrf-token\") ?? \"\";\r\n  return safeEqual(provided, session.csrfToken);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function clearGmSession() {\r\n  const jar = await cookies();\r\n  const raw = jar.get(GM_COOKIE)?.value;\r\n  if (raw) await db.gameMasterSession.deleteMany({ where: { id: hashToken(raw) } });\r\n  jar.delete(GM_COOKIE);\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

return { deleted: true };\n+  });\n+}\n+\n+export async function syncVisionBindingsForDraft(\n+  tx: Prisma.TransactionClient,\n+  storyId: string,\n+  blocks: Array<{ id: string; type: string; configuration: Record<string, unknown> }>,\n+  actorId: string,\n+) {\n+  for (const block of blocks.filter(\n+    (candidate) =>\n+      candidate.type === \"visionWaypoint\" || candidate.configuration.verificationProvider === \"visionLocation\",\n+  )) {\n+    const versionId = String(block.configuration.waypointVersionId ?? \"\");\n+    if (!versionId) continue;\n+    const version = await tx.visionWaypointVersion.findUnique({ where: { id: versionId }, include: { waypoint: true } });\n+    if (!version?.publishedAt)\n+      throw new VisionDomainError(\n+ new VisionDomainError(\"WAYPOINT_PERMISSION_DENIED\", \"The selected waypoint is not owned by this creator.\");\n+    const runtimeMode = waypointRuntimeModeSchema.parse(block.configuration.runtimeMode ?? \"DEVELOPMENT_MOCK\");\n+    if (runtimeMode !== \"DEVELOPMENT_MOCK\")\n+      throw new VisionDomainError(\"RUNTIME_MODE_DISABLED\", \"B-1 permits only DEVELOPMENT_MOCK story blocks.\");\n+    const scanInteraction = {\n+      mode: block.configuration.scanMode ?? \"HOLD\",\n+      holdDurationMs: retryMessages = jsonObject.parse({\n+      insufficient: block.configuration.insufficientMessage ?? \"Move slowly and inspect the surroundings again.\",\n+      ambiguous: block.configuration.ambiguousMessage ?? \"Show more of the surrounding landmarks.\",\n+    });\n+    const failureMessages = jsonObject.parse({\n+      notAtTarget: block.configuration.notAtTargetMessage ?? \"The compass remains silent here.\",\n+      systemError: block.configuration.systemErrorMessage ?? \"The vision helper is unavailable. Ask the Captain.\",\n+    });\n+    const binding = await tx.storyWaypointBinding.create({\n+      data: {\n+        storyId,\n+        blockId: block.id,\n+        waypointId: version.waypointId,\n+        waypointVersionId: version.id,\n+        runtimeMode,\n+        scanInteraction: JSON.stringify(scanInteraction),\n+        successEvent: String(block.configuration.successEvent ?? \"vision.verification_succeeded\"),\n+        retryMessageConfiguration: JSON.stringify(retryMessages),\n+        failureMessageConfiguration: JSON.stringify(failureMessages),\n+        captainFallbackPolicy: JSON.stringify(fallback),\n+        offlineBehavior: String(block.configuration.offlineBehavior ?? \"CAPTAIN_FALLBACK\"),\n+      },\n+    });\n+    await audit(tx, {\n+      actorId,\n+      action: \"VISION_WAYPOINT_BINDING_SYNCHRONIZED\",\n+      resourceType: \"STORY_WAYPOINT_BINDING\",\n+      resourceId: binding.id,\n+      metadata: { storyId, blockId: block.id, waypointVersionId: version.id },\n+    });\n+  }\n+}\n*** End Patch";

Date().toISOString(),\n+      result: resultSubmissionValue(outcome.result),\n+      evidence: {\n+        evidenceDigest,\n+        waypointId: attempt.waypointId,\n+        waypointVersionId: attempt.waypointVersionId,\n+        mockScenario: scenario,\n+        protocolVersion: VISION_PROTOCOL_VERSION,\n+      },\n+    };\n+    const first = await submitVerification(submission, { sourceType: actor.sourceType, sourceId: actor.sourceId });\n+    delivered = first.accepted;\n+    if (scenario === \"duplicate_result_delivery\") {\n+      const duplicate = await submitVerification(submission, { sourceType: actor.sourceType, sourceId: actor.sourceId });\n+      duplicateRejected = duplicate.duplicate;\n+    }\n+  } catch (cause) {\n+    if (cause instanceof VerificationRejectedError && [\"wrongBlock\", \"staleRequest\", \"staleObservation\"].includes(cause.reason)) {\n+      const stale = await markStale(attemptId, cause.reason === \"wrongBlock\" ? \"STORY_STAGE_CHANGED\" : \"ATTEMPT_STALE\");\n+      return publicAttempt(stale);\n+    }\n+    throw cause;\n+  }\n+\n+  if (outcome.result === \"VERIFIED\" && delivered)\n+    await transitionVerificationAttempt(attemptId, \"EVENT_DELIVERED\", { storyEvent: \"verificationSatisfied\" });\n+  const updated = await db.verificationAttempt.update({\n+    where: { id: attemptId },\n+    data: {\n+      eventDeliveryStatus: outcome.result === \"VERIFIED\" ? \"DELIVERED\" : \"RESULT_RECORDED\",\n+      duplicateResultRejected: duplicateRejected,\n+    },\n+    include: {\n+      transitions: { orderBy: { sequence: \"asc\" } },\n+      waypoint: { select: { name: true } },\n+      waypointVersion: { select: { versionNumber: true, lifecycleStatus: true } },\n+    },\n+  });\n+  logger.info(\n+    {\n+      area: \"vision-mock\",\n+      attemptId,\n+      scenario,\n+      result: outcome.result,\n+      eventDeliveryStatus: updated.eventDeliveryStatus,\n+      duplicateResultRejected: updated.duplicateResultRejected,\n+    },\n+    \"Deterministic mock verification completed\",\n+  );\n+  return publicAttempt(updated);\n+}\n+\n+export async function recordCaptainAttemptAction(\n+  attemptId: string,\n+  actorId: string,\n+  action: \"APPROVED\" | \"REJECTED\",\n+  reason: string,\n+) {\n+  const cleanReason = z.string().trim().min(3).max(500).parse(reason);\n+  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\n+    const attempt = await tx.verificationAttempt.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: attemptId } });\n+    const updated = await tx.verificationAttempt.update({ where: { id: attemptId }, data: { captainAction: action } });\n+    await tx.platformAuditEvent.create({\n+      data: {\n+        actorType: \"CAPTAIN\",\n+        actorId,\n+        action: `VISION_ATTEMPT_MANUAL_${action}`,\n+        resourceType: \"VERIFICATION_ATTEMPT\",\n+        resourceId: attempt.id,\n+        correlationId: randomUUID(),\n+        metadata: JSON.stringify({ action, reason: cleanReason.slice(0, 200), sessionId: attempt.sessionId }),\n+      },\n+    });\n+    return publicAttempt(updated);\n+  });\n+}\n*** End Patch";

Record<string, BlockDefinition>;\n\nexport type BlockType = keyof typeof blockRegistry;\nexport const blockTypeIds = Object.keys(blockRegistry) as BlockType[];\n\nexport function getBlockDefinition(type: string): BlockDefinition | undefined {\n  return blockRegistry[type as BlockType];\n}\n\nexport function serializeBlockRegistry() {\n  return blockTypeIds.map((type) => {\n    const { validationSchema: _schema, ...definition } = blockRegistry[type];\n    void _schema;\n    return definition;\n  });\n}\n\nexport function providerForBlock(type: string, configuration: JsonObject): VerificationProviderType | null {\n  if (type === \"captainApproval\") return \"captainManual\";\n  if (type === \"textAnswer\" || type === \"riddle\") return \"textAnswer\";\n  if (type === \"wait\") return \"timer\";\n  const configured = String(configuration.verificationProvider ?? configuration.completionMode ?? \"playerConfirmation\");\n  if (configured === \"automatic\") return null;\n  if (\n    [\n      \"captainManual\",\n      \"playerConfirmation\",\n      \"textAnswer\",\n      \"timer\",\n      \"visionLocation\",\n      \"visionObject\",\n      \"externalWebhook\",\n    ].includes(configured)\n  )\n    return configured as VerificationProviderType;\n  return \"playerConfirmation\";\n}\n\r\n---VALIDATION---\r\nimport { db } from \"@/lib/db\";\nimport { getBlockDefinition } from \"@/tall-tale/block-registry\";\nimport { getStudioTale, slugSchema } from \"@/tall-tale/studio-service\";\nimport type { DraftValidationResult, ValidationIssue } from \"@/tall-tale/types\";\n\nconst futureProviders = new Set([\"visionLocation\", \"visionObject\", \"externalWebhook\"]);\n\nexport async function validateTaleDraft(taleId: string): Promise<DraftValidationResult & { autosaveVersion: number }> {\n  const studio = await getStudioTale(taleId);\n  const errors: ValidationIssue[] = [];\n  const warnings: ValidationIssue[] = [];\n  const error = (issue: Omit<ValidationIssue, \"severity\">) => errors.push({ ...issue, severity: \"error\" });\n  const warn = (issue: Omit<ValidationIssue, \"severity\">) => warnings.push({ ...issue, severity: \"warning\" });\n\n  if (!studio.tale.title.trim()) error({ code: \"TALE_TITLE\", message: \"The Tall Tale needs a title.\", field: \"title\" });\n  const slug = slugSchema.safeParse(studio.tale.slug);\n  if (!slug.success)\n    error({\n      code: \"TALE_SLUG\",\n      message: slug.error.issues[0]?.message ?? \"The tale address is invalid.\",\n      field: \"slug\",\n    });\n  if (!studio.draft.chapters.length) error({ code: \"NO_CHAPTERS\", message: \"Add at least one chapter.\" });\n  if (!studio.tale.coverAssetId)\n    warn({ code: \"NO_COVER\", message: \"Add cover artwork before featuring this tale.\", field: \"coverAssetId\" });\n  if (!studio.tale.estimatedDuration)\n    warn({\n      code: \"NO_DURATION\",\n      message: \"Add an estimated duration for the player catalog.\",\n      field: \"estimatedDuration\",\n    });\n\n  const assets = new Map(studio.assets.map((asset) => [asset.id, asset]));\n  const allBlocks = studio.draft.chapters.flatMap((chapter) =>\n    chapter.blocks.map((block) => ({ ...block, chapterId: chapter.id })),\n  );\n  const blockIds = new Set(allBlocks.map((block) => block.id));\n  const outgoing = new Map<string, string[]>();\n  const referencedAssetIds = new Set<string>();\n  let taleCompleteCount = 0;\n\n  for (const chapter of studio.draft.chapters) {\n    if (!chapter.blocks.length) {\n      error({ code: \"EMPTY_CHAPTER\", message: `â€œ${chapter.title}â€ needs an entry block.`, chapterId: chapter.id });\n      continue;\n    }\n    if (!chapter.estimatedDuration)\n      warn({\n        code: \"CHAPTER_DURATION\",\n        message: `â€œ${chapter.title}â€ has no estimated duration.`,\n        chapterId: chapter.id,\n      });\n    for (const block of chapter.blocks) {\n      const definition = getBlockDefinition(block.blockType);\n      if (!definition) {\n        error({\n          code: \"UNKNOWN_BLOCK\",\n          message: `â€œ${block.title}â€ uses unknown block type ${block.blockType}.`,\n          chapterId: chapter.id,\n          blockId: block.id,\n        });\n        continue;\n      }\n      const parsed = definition.validationSchema.safeParse(block.configuration);\n      if (!parsed.success) {\n        for (const issue of parsed.error.issues)\n          error({\n            code: \"BLOCK_FIELD\",\n            message: `${block.title}: ${issue.message}`,\n            chapterId: chapter.id,\n            blockId: block.id,\n            field: issue.path.join(\".\"),\n          });\n      }\n      if (block.blockType === \"taleComplete\") taleCompleteCount += 1;\n      const provider = String(block.configuration.verificationProvider ?? block.configuration.completionMode ?? \"\");\n      if (futureProviders.has(provider))\n        error({\n          code: \"FUTURE_PROVIDER_ACTIVE\",\n          message: `${block.title} selects ${provider}, which is stored for future use but unavailable as an active Phase 1 provider.`,\n          chapterId: chapter.id,\n          blockId: block.id,\n          field: \"verificationProvider\",\n        });\n      if ([\"riddle\", \"textAnswer\"].includes(block.blockType)) {\n        const answers = block.configuration.acceptedAnswers;\n        if (!Array.isArray(answers) || !answers.some((answer) => typeof answer === \"string\" && answer.trim()))\n          error({\n            code: \"NO_ANSWERS\",\n            message: `${block.title} needs at least one accepted answer.`,\n            chapterId: chapter.id,\n            blockId: block.id,\n            field: \"acceptedAnswers\",\n          });\n      }\n      for (const [field, mediaTypes] of Object.entries(definition.assetFields)) {\n        const assetId = block.configuration[field];\n        if (!assetId || typeof assetId !== \"string\") continue;\n        const selected = assets.get(assetId);\n        if (!selected) {\n          error({\n            code: \"MISSING_ASSET\",\n            message: `${block.title} references an unavailable asset.`,\n            chapterId: chapter.id,\n            blockId: block.id,\n            assetId,\n            field,\n          });\n          continue;\n        }\n        referencedAssetIds.add(assetId);\n        if (mediaTypes.length && !mediaTypes.includes(selected.mediaType))\n          error({\n            code: \"ASSET_TYPE\",\n            message: `${selected.displayName} is not compatible with ${definition.fields.find((item) => item.key === field)?.label ?? field}.`,\n            chapterId: chapter.id,\n            blockId: block.id,\n            assetId,\n            field,\n          });\n        if (selected.roles.includes(\"CAPTAIN_ONLY_REFERENCE\"))\n          error({\n            code: \"PRIVATE_ASSET\",\n            message: `${selected.displayName} is Captain-only and cannot be assigned to player content.`,\n            chapterId: chapter.id,\n            blockId: block.id,\n            assetId,\n            field,\n          });\n        if (!selected.variants.some((variant) => variant.processingState === \"READY\"))\n          error({\n            code: \"ASSET_PROCESSING\",\n            message: `${selected.displayName} has no ready player variant.`,\n            chapterId: chapter.id,\n            blockId: block.id,\n            assetId,\n            field,\n          });\n        if (selected.fileSize > 20 * 1024 * 1024)\n          warn({\n            code: \"LARGE_ASSET\",\n            message: `${selected.displayName} is larger than 20 MB and may load slowly on mobile connections.`,\n            chapterId: chapter.id,\n            blockId: block.id,\n            assetId,\n            field,\n          });\n      }\n      if (block.blockType === \"image\" && !String(block.configuration.altText ?? \"\").trim())\n        warn({\n          code: \"MISSING_ALT\",\n          message: `${block.title} needs useful alternative text.`,\n          chapterId: chapter.id,\n          blockId: block.id,\n          field: \"altText\",\n        });\n      if (block.blockType === \"imageTransformation\") {\n        const alignment = block.configuration.alignment;\n        if (!alignment || typeof alignment !== \"object\")\n          warn({\n            code: \"NO_ALIGNMENT\",\n            message: `${block.title} has no saved mobile/alignment treatment.`,\n            chapterId: chapter.id,\n            blockId: block.id,\n            field: \"alignment\",\n          });\n      }\n      const targets = block.connections.map((connection) => connection.targetBlockId);\n      outgoing.set(block.id, targets);\n      for (const target of targets)\n        if (!blockIds.has(target))\n          error({\n            code: \"BROKEN_CONNECTION\",\n            message: `${block.title} points to a story block that no longer exists.`,\n            chapterId: chapter.id,\n            blockId: block.id,\n          });\n      if (\n        block.isEnabled &&\n        !targets.length &&\n        ![\"chapterComplete\", \"taleComplete\"].includes(block.blockType) &&\n        block !== chapter.blocks.at(-1)\n      )\n        error({\n          code: \"MISSING_DEFAULT_CONNECTION\",\n          message: `${block.title} has no default continuation.`,\n          chapterId: chapter.id,\n          blockId: block.id,\n        });\n      if (block.blockType === \"choice\") {\n        const choices = block.configuration.choices;\n        if (!Array.isArray(choices) || choices.length < 2)\n          error({\n            code: \"CHOICE_COUNT\",\n            message: `${block.title} needs at least two choices.`,\n            chapterId: chapter.id,\n            blockId: block.id,\n            field: \"choices\",\n          });\n        for (const choice of Array.isArray(choices) ? choices : []) {\n          const target =\n            choice && typeof choice === \"object\" ? String((choice as Record<string, unknown>).targetBlockId ?? \"\") : \"\";\n          if (!blockIds.has(target))\n            error({\n              code: \"CHOICE_TARGET\",\n              message: `${block.title} has a missing choice target.`,\n              chapterId: chapter.id,\n              blockId: block.id,\n              field: \"choices\",\n            });\n        }\n      }\n      if (block.blockType === \"condition\") {\n        for (const field of [\"successTargetBlockId\", \"failureTargetBlockId\"] as const)\n          if (!blockIds.has(String(block.configuration[field] ?? \"\")))\n            error({\n              code: \"CONDITION_TARGET\",\n              message: `${block.title} has a missing ${field === \"successTargetBlockId\" ? \"success\" : \"failure\"} target.`,\n              chapterId: chapter.id,\n              blockId: block.id,\n              field,\n            });\n      }\n    }\n  }\n  if (!taleCompleteCount)\n    error({ code: \"NO_TALE_COMPLETE\", message: \"Add a Tale Complete block so the runtime has a safe endpoint.\" });\n  if (taleCompleteCount > 1)\n    warn({\n      code: \"MULTIPLE_TALE_COMPLETE\",\n      message: \"The tale has multiple completion endpoints. Confirm each branch is intentional.\",\n    });\n\n  const entry = allBlocks[0]?.id;\n  if (entry) {\n    const visited = new Set<string>();\n    const queue = [entry];\n    while (queue.length) {\n      const current = queue.shift()!;\n      if (visited.has(current)) continue;\n      visited.add(current);\n      for (const target of outgoing.get(current) ?? []) if (!visited.has(target)) queue.push(target);\n    }\n    for (const block of allBlocks)\n      if (block.isEnabled && !visited.has(block.id)) {\n        const chapter = studio.draft.chapters.find((item) => item.id === block.chapterId);\n        const issue = {\n          code: \"UNREACHABLE_BLOCK\",\n          message: `${block.title} cannot be reached through the saved story graph.`,\n          chapterId: block.chapterId,\n          blockId: block.id,\n        };\n        if (chapter?.isOptional) warn(issue);\n        else error(issue);\n      }\n\n    const completionIds = new Set(\n      allBlocks.filter((block) => block.blockType === \"taleComplete\").map((block) => block.id),\n    );\n    const canComplete = new Set(completionIds);\n    let changed = true;\n    while (changed) {\n      changed = false;\n      for (const block of allBlocks) {\n        if (canComplete.has(block.id)) continue;\n        if ((outgoing.get(block.id) ?? []).some((target) => canComplete.has(target))) {\n          canComplete.add(block.id);\n          changed = true;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    for (const block of allBlocks)\n      if (block.isEnabled && visited.has(block.id) && !canComplete.has(block.id))\n        error({\n          code: \"NO_COMPLETION_PATH\",\n          message: `${block.title} enters a branch or loop with no path to Tale Complete.`,\n          chapterId: block.chapterId,\n          blockId: block.id,\n        });\n  }\n\n  for (const asset of studio.assets)\n    if (!referencedAssetIds.has(asset.id) && asset.id !== studio.tale.coverAssetId)\n      warn({\n        code: \"UNUSED_ASSET\",\n        message: `${asset.displayName} is not used by the current draft.`,\n        assetId: asset.id,\n      });\n\n  const checkedAt = new Date();\n  const result: DraftValidationResult = {\n    valid: errors.length === 0,\n    errors,\n    warnings,\n    checkedAt: checkedAt.toISOString(),\n  };\n  await db.taleDraft.update({\n    where: { id: studio.draft.id },\n    data: {\n      validationState: result.valid ? \"VALID\" : \"INVALID\",\n      validationSummary: JSON.stringify(result),\n      lastValidatedAt: checkedAt,\n    },\n  });\n  return { ...result, autosaveVersion: studio.draft.autosaveVersion };\n}\n\r\n---PROGRESSION VISION---\r\n21:const futureProviders = new Set([\"visionLocation\", \"visionObject\", \"externalWebhook\"]);\n33:    \"visionLocation\",\n109:  if (futureProviders.has(providerType))\n", "type": "input_text"}]

requirePlayerIdentity();\r\n  return Boolean(session && provided && safeEqual(session.csrfToken, provided));\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function clearPlayerIdentitySession() {\r\n  const jar = await cookies();\r\n  const token = jar.get(PLAYER_IDENTITY_COOKIE)?.value;\r\n  if (token)\r\n    await db.playerIdentitySession.updateMany({\r\n      where: { tokenHash: hashToken(token), revokedAt: null },\r\n      data: { revokedAt: new Date() },\r\n    });\r\n  jar.delete(PLAYER_IDENTITY_COOKIE);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function setPendingInvitationToken(token: string) {\r\n  const csrfToken = makeToken(24);\r\n  (await cookies()).set(PENDING_INVITATION_COOKIE, `${token}.${csrfToken}`, cookieOptions(30 * 60));\r\n  return csrfToken;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function readPendingInvitationToken() {\r\n  const value = (await cookies()).get(PENDING_INVITATION_COOKIE)?.value;\r\n  if (!value) return null;\r\n  const separator = value.lastIndexOf(\".\");\r\n  return separator > 0 ? value.slice(0, separator) : null;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function readPendingInvitationCsrf() {\r\n  const value = (await cookies()).get(PENDING_INVITATION_COOKIE)?.value;\r\n  if (!value) return null;\r\n  const separator = value.lastIndexOf(\".\");\r\n  return separator > 0 ? value.slice(separator + 1) : null;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function verifyPendingInvitationCsrf(provided: string | null) {\r\n  const expected = await readPendingInvitationCsrf();\r\n  return Boolean(expected && provided && safeEqual(expected, provided));\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function clearPendingInvitationToken() {\r\n  (await cookies()).delete(PENDING_INVITATION_COOKIE);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function playerCanAccessPlaythrough(playthroughId: string, playerId?: string) {\r\n  const identity = playerId ? null : await requirePlayerIdentity();\r\n  const resolvedPlayerId = playerId ?? identity?.playerProfileId;\r\n  if (!resolvedPlayerId) return false;\r\n  return Boolean(\r\n    await db.playthroughMembership.findFirst({\r\n      where: {\r\n        playthroughId,\r\n        playerProfileId: resolvedPlayerId,\r\n        status: { in: [\"INVITED\", \"ACCEPTED\", \"READY\", \"ACTIVE_MEMBER\", \"COMPLETED_MEMBER\"] },\r\n      },\r\n      select: { id: true },\r\n    }),\r\n  );\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function authorizeTaleSessionPlayer(playthroughId: string) {\r\n  const identity = await requirePlayerIdentity();\r\n  if (identity && (await playerCanAccessPlaythrough(playthroughId, identity.playerProfileId)))\r\n    return {\r\n      kind: \"identity\" as const,\r\n      playerId: identity.playerProfileId,\r\n      identitySessionId: identity.id,\r\n      csrfToken: identity.csrfToken,\r\n    };\r\n  const legacy = await readTaleSessionCookie(playthroughId);\r\n  if (!legacy) return null;\r\n  const session = await db.taleSession.findFirst({\r\n    where: { id: playthroughId, accessTokenHash: hashToken(legacy.token) },\r\n    select: { id: true },\r\n  });\r\n  return session ? { kind: \"legacy\" as const, token: legacy.token } : null;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function pendingInvitationMatches(invitationId: string) {\r\n  const credential = await readPendingInvitationToken();\r\n  if (!credential) return false;\r\n  const where = credential.startsWith(\"code:\")\r\n    ? { shortCodeHash: hashToken(credential.slice(5)) }\r\n    : { tokenHash: hashToken(credential) };\r\n  return Boolean(await db.invitation.findFirst({ where: { id: invitationId, ...where }, select: { id: true } }));\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

null,\r\n      eventType: input.eventType,\r\n      sourceType: input.sourceType,\r\n      sourceId: input.sourceId ?? null,\r\n      idempotencyKey: input.idempotencyKey,\r\n      payload: JSON.stringify(input.payload ?? 0);\r\n  return tx.taleVerificationRequest.create({\r\n    data: {\r\n      sessionId,\r\n      blockId: block.id,\r\n      providerType,\r\n      expiresAt: providerType === \"timer\" && duration > 0 ? null, lastHeartbeatAt: new Date() },\r\n  });\r\n  if (session.publishedVersionId)\r\n    await tx.revealState.upsert({\r\n      where: {\r\n        playthroughId_contentType_contentKey: {\r\n          playthroughId: session.id,\r\n          contentType: \"BLOCK\",\r\n          contentKey: block.id,\r\n        },\r\n      },\r\n      update: {},\r\n\"use client\";\n\nimport Link from \"next/link\";\nimport { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useState } from \"react\";\nimport { PublishedBlockView } from \"@/components/tales/PublishedBlockView\";\nimport type { JsonObject } from \"@/tall-tale/types\";\n\ntype State = {\n  csrfToken?: string;\n  session: {\n    id: string;\n    status: string;\n    previewMode: boolean;\n    versionId: string;\n    currentSequence: number;\n    completedAt: string | null;\n  };\n  tale: { title: string; slug: string };\n  chapter: { title: string; orderIndex: number } | null;\n  block: {\n    id: string;\n    blockType: string;\n    title: string;\n    configuration: JsonObject;\n    connections: Array<{ targetBlockId: string; connectionType: string; label?: string | null }>;\n  } | null;\n  pendingVerification: { id: string; providerType: string; expiresAt: string | null } | null;\n  assets: Array<{ id: string; displayName: string; url: string }>;\n};\n\nexport function PlayerRuntime({ sessionId }: { sessionId: string }) {\n  const [state, setState] = useState<State | null>(null);\n  const [answer, setAnswer] = useState(\"\");\n  const [busy, setBusy] = useState(false);\n  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n  const [connected, setConnected] = useState(false);\n  const [now, setNow] = useState(0);\n  const load = useCallback(async () => {\n    const response = await fetch(`/api/play/sessions/${sessionId}`, { cache: \"no-store\" });\n    const body = (await response.json()) as State & { error?: string };\n    if (!response.ok) setError(body.error ?? \"The voyage state could not be read.\");\n    else {\n      setState(body);\n      setError(\"\");\n    }\n  }, [sessionId]);\n  useEffect(() => {\n    queueMicrotask(() => {\n      setNow(Date.now());\n      void load();\n    });\n    const source = new EventSource(`/api/play/sessions/${sessionId}/events`);\n    source.onopen = () => setConnected(true);\n    source.onerror = () => setConnected(false);\n    source.addEventListener(\"progression\", () => void load());\n    const timer = setInterval(() => setNow(Date.now()), 500);\n    return () => {\n      source.close();\n      clearInterval(timer);\n    };\n  }, [load, sessionId]);\n  const waitRemaining = useMemo(\n    () => Math.max(0, Date.parse(state?.pendingVerification?.expiresAt ?? \"\") - now),\n    [now, state],\n  );\n  async function act(action: \"continue\" | \"confirm\" | \"answer\" | \"choice\" | \"timer\", extra: JsonObject = {}) {\n    setBusy(true);\n    setError(\"\");\n    const response = await fetch(`/api/play/sessions/${sessionId}`, {\n      method: \"POST\",\n      headers: {\n        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n        ...(state?.csrfToken ? { \"x-csrf-token\": state.csrfToken } : {}),\n      },\n      body: JSON.stringify({ action, idempotencyKey: crypto.randomUUID(), answer, ...extra }),\n    });\n    const body = (await response.json()) as { state?: State; accepted?: boolean; error?: string };\n    if (!response.ok) setError(body.error ?? \"The story could not advance.\");\n    else if (body.state) {\n      setState({ ...body.state, csrfToken: body.state.csrfToken ?? state?.csrfToken });\n      if (body.accepted) setAnswer(\"\");\n    }\n    setBusy(false);\n  }\n  if (!state)\n    return (\n      <main className=\"player-runtime loading\">\n        <p role=\"status\">{error || \"Finding the current mark on the chartâ€¦\"}</p>\n      </main>\n    );\n  const choices =\n    state.block?.blockType === \"choice\" && Array.isArray(state.block.configuration.choices)\n      ? (state.block.configuration.choices as Array<Record<string, unknown>>)\n      : [];\n  return (\n    <main className={`player-runtime ${state.session.previewMode ? \"preview-mode\" : \"\"}`}>\n      <header>\n        <div>\n          <p className=\"eyebrow\">\n            {state.session.previewMode ? \"Preview Mode â€” no real progress\" : (state.chapter?.title ?? \"Tall Tale\")}\n          </p>\n          <h1>{state.tale.title}</h1>\n        </div>\n        <div className={`runtime-connection ${connected ? \"connected\" : \"reconnecting\"}`}>\n          <i />\n          {connected ? \"Captain channel connected\" : \"Reconnecting to canonical state\"}\n        </div>\n      </header>\n      <section className=\"runtime-stage\">\n        {state.block ? (\n          <PublishedBlockView block={state.block} assets={state.assets} />\n        ) : (\n          <article className=\"runtime-block unknown-block\">\n            <h2>No safe story block is available.</h2>\n          </article>\n        )}\n        <div className=\"runtime-actions\" aria-live=\"polite\">\n          {state.session.status === \"COMPLETED\" ? (\n            <>\n              <strong>This Tall Tale is complete.</strong>\n              <Link className=\"brass-button\" href={`/play/${state.tale.slug}/history`}>\n                View voyage history\n              </Link>\n              <Link href=\"/tales\">Choose another tale</Link>\n            </>\n          ) : state.pendingVerification?.providerType === \"captainManual\" ? (\n            <div className=\"awaiting-captain\">\n              <span>â—·</span>\n              <strong>{String(state.block?.configuration.waitingText ?? \"Awaiting the Captain's approvalâ€¦\")}</strong>\n              <p>The player view will update automatically when the Captain responds.</p>\n            </div>\n          ) : state.pendingVerification?.providerType === \"textAnswer\" ? (\n            <form\n              onSubmit={(event) => {\n                event.preventDefault();\n                void act(\"answer\");\n              }}\n            >\n              <label>\n                <span>Your answer</span>\n                <input value={answer} onChange={(event) => setAnswer(event.target.value)} autoComplete=\"off\" required />\n              </label>\n              <button className=\"brass-button\" disabled={busy}>\n                {busy ? \"Testing the answerâ€¦\" : \"Submit answer\"}\n              </button>\n            </form>\n          ) : state.pendingVerification?.providerType === \"timer\" ? (\n            <button className=\"brass-button\" disabled={busy || waitRemaining > 0} onClick={() => void act(\"timer\")}>\n              {waitRemaining > 0 ? `Continue in ${Math.ceil(waitRemaining / 1000)}s` : \"Continue\"}\n            </button>\n          ) : choices.length ? (\n            <div className=\"choice-actions\">\n              <p>{String(state.block?.configuration.prompt ?? \"Choose a course.\")}</p>\n              {choices.map((choice) => (\n                <button\n                  key={String(choice.id ?? choice.label)}\n                  disabled={busy || !choice.targetBlockId}\n                  onClick={() => void act(\"choice\", { targetBlockId: choice.targetBlockId })}\n                >\n                  <strong>{String(choice.label ?? \"Choice\")}</strong>\n                  {Boolean(choice.description) && <span>{String(choice.description)}</span>}\n                </button>\n              ))}\n            </div>\n          ) : (\n            <button\n              className=\"brass-button\"\n              disabled={busy || state.block?.blockType === \"condition\" || state.block?.blockType === \"setVariable\"}\n              onClick={() => void act(\"continue\")}\n            >\n              {busy\n                ? \"Writing progressâ€¦\"\n                : String(\n                    state.block?.configuration.buttonLabel ?? state.block?.configuration.primaryLabel ?? \"Continue\",\n                  )}\n            </button>\n          )}\n          {error && (\n            <p className=\"runtime-error\" role=\"alert\">\n              {error}\n            </p>\n          )}\n        </div>\n      </section>\n      <footer>\n        <span>\n          Version {state.session.versionId.startsWith(\"draft:\") ? \"draft preview\" : state.session.versionId.slice(0, 8)}\n        </span>\n        <span>Event sequence {state.session.currentSequence}</span>\n      </footer>\n    </main>\n  );\n}\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

{\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const binding = await tx.storyWaypointBinding.findFirstOrThrow({\r\n      where: { id: bindingId, story: { creatorId: actorId } },\r\n    });\r\n    await tx.storyWaypointBinding.delete({ where: { id: binding.id } });\r\n    await audit(tx, {\r\n      actorId,\r\n      action: \"VISION_WAYPOINT_BINDING_REMOVED\",\r\n      resourceType: \"STORY_WAYPOINT_BINDING\",\r\n      resourceId: binding.id,\r\n      metadata: { storyId: binding.storyId, blockId: binding.blockId, waypointVersionId: binding.waypointVersionId },\r\n    });\r\n    return { deleted: true };\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function syncVisionBindingsForDraft(\r\n  tx: Prisma.TransactionClient,\r\n  storyId: string,\r\n  blocks: Array<{ id: string; type: string; configuration: Record<string, unknown> }>,\r\n  actorId: string,\r\n) {\r\n  for (const block of blocks.filter(\r\n    (candidate) =>\r\n      candidate.type === \"visionWaypoint\" || candidate.configuration.verificationProvider === \"visionLocation\",\r\n  )) {\r\n    const versionId = String(block.configuration.waypointVersionId ?? \"\");\r\n    if (!versionId) continue;\r\n    const version = await tx.visionWaypointVersion.findUnique({ where: { id: versionId }, include: { waypoint: true } });\r\n    if (!version?.publishedAt)\r\n      throw new VisionDomainError(\r\n        \"PUBLISHED_VERSION_REQUIRED\",\r\n        `Vision block ${block.id} must reference an immutable published waypoint version.`,\r\n      );\r\n    if (version.waypoint.ownerId !== actorId)\r\n      throw new VisionDomainError(\"WAYPOINT_PERMISSION_DENIED\", \"The selected waypoint is not owned by this creator.\");\r\n    const runtimeMode = waypointRuntimeModeSchema.parse(block.configuration.runtimeMode ?? \"DEVELOPMENT_MOCK\");\r\n    if (runtimeMode !== \"DEVELOPMENT_MOCK\")\r\n      throw new VisionDomainError(\"RUNTIME_MODE_DISABLED\", \"B-1 permits only DEVELOPMENT_MOCK story blocks.\");\r\n    const scanInteraction = {\r\n      mode: block.configuration.scanMode ?? \"HOLD\",\r\n      holdDurationMs: Number(block.configuration.holdDurationMs ?? 5_000),\r\n      progressAnnouncementIntervalMs: Number(block.configuration.progressAnnouncementIntervalMs ?? 1_000),\r\n    };\r\n    const fallback = {\r\n      enabled: Boolean(block.configuration.captainFallbackEnabled ?? true),\r\n      allowManualApprove: true,\r\n      allowManualReject: true,\r\n      requireReason: true,\r\n    };\r\n    const retryMessages = jsonObject.parse({\r\n      insufficient: block.configuration.insufficientMessage ?? \"Move slowly and inspect the surroundings again.\",\r\n      ambiguous: block.configuration.ambiguousMessage ?? \"Show more of the surrounding landmarks.\",\r\n    });\r\n    const failureMessages = jsonObject.parse({\r\n      notAtTarget: block.configuration.notAtTargetMessage ?? \"The compass remains silent here.\",\r\n      systemError: block.configuration.systemErrorMessage ?? \"The vision helper is unavailable. Ask the Captain.\",\r\n    });\r\n    const binding = await tx.storyWaypointBinding.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        storyId,\r\n        blockId: block.id,\r\n        waypointId: version.waypointId,\r\n        waypointVersionId: version.id,\r\n        runtimeMode,\r\n        scanInteraction: JSON.stringify(scanInteraction),\r\n        successEvent: String(block.configuration.successEvent ?? \"vision.verification_succeeded\"),\r\n        retryMessageConfiguration: JSON.stringify(retryMessages),\r\n        failureMessageConfiguration: JSON.stringify(failureMessages),\r\n        captainFallbackPolicy: JSON.stringify(fallback),\r\n        offlineBehavior: String(block.configuration.offlineBehavior ?? \"CAPTAIN_FALLBACK\"),\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    await audit(tx, {\r\n      actorId,\r\n      action: \"VISION_WAYPOINT_BINDING_SYNCHRONIZED\",\r\n      resourceType: \"STORY_WAYPOINT_BINDING\",\r\n      resourceId: binding.id,\r\n      metadata: { storyId, blockId: block.id, waypointVersionId: version.id },\r\n    });\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

\"@/tall-tale/studio-service\";\nimport { PublishValidationError } from \"@/tall-tale/publishing\";\nimport { VerificationRejectedError } from \"@/tall-tale/progression\";\nimport { logger } from \"@/lib/logger\";\nimport { InvitationUnavailableError } from \"@/platform/invitations\";\n\nexport function apiError(cause: unknown) {\n  logger.warn(\n    {\n      area: \"tall-tale-api\",\n      errorType: cause instanceof Error ? cause.constructor.name : typeof cause,\n      errorCode:\n        cause instanceof DraftConflictError\n          ? \"DRAFT_CONFLICT\"\n          : cause instanceof InvitationUnavailableError\n                ? cause.code\n                : \"REQUEST_FAILED\",\n    },\n    \"Tall Tale request rejected\",\n  );\n  if (cause instanceof DraftConflictError)\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: cause.message, code: \"DRAFT_CONFLICT\", currentVersion: cause.currentVersion },\n      { status: 409 },\n    );\n  if (cause instanceof PublishValidationError)\n    return NextResponse.json(\n      { error: cause.message, code: \"VALIDATION_FAILED\", validation: cause.validation },\n      { status: 422 },\n    );\n  if (cause instanceof VerificationRejectedError)\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: cause.message, code: cause.reason }, { status: 409 });\n  if (cause instanceof InvitationUnavailableError) {\n    const status =\n      cause.code === \"ACCOUNT_REQUIRED\" ? 401 : cause.code === \"CONFLICT\" ? 409 : cause.code === \"INVALID\" ? 400 : 410;\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: cause.message, code: cause.code }, { status });\n  }\n  const message = cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"The request could not be completed.\";\n  if (message.includes(\"Unique constraint\"))\n    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"That name or address is already in use.\" }, { status: 409 });\n  return NextResponse.json({ error: message }, { status: 400 });\n}\n\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function transitionVerificationAttempt(\r\n  attemptId: string,\r\n  toState: VerificationAttemptState,\r\n  metadata: Record<string, unknown> = {},\r\n) {\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const attempt = await tx.verificationAttempt.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: attemptId } });\r\n    return appendTransition(tx, attempt, toState, metadata);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction publicAttempt<T extends {\r\n  id: string;\r\n  storyId: string;\r\n  stageId: string;\r\n  playerId: string | null;\r\n  sessionId: string | null;\r\n  verificationRequestId: string | null;\r\n  waypointId: string;\r\n  waypointVersionId: string;\r\n  attemptState: string;\r\n  result: string | null;\r\n  guidanceCode: string | null;\r\n  evidenceDigest: string | null;\r\n  platform: string;\r\n  adapterType: string;\r\n  eventDeliveryStatus: string;\r\n  captainAction: string | null;\r\n  mockScenario: string | null;\r\n  protocolVersion: string;\r\n  protocolMessageId: string | null;\r\n  idempotencyKey: string;\r\n  staleResultRejected: boolean;\r\n  duplicateResultRejected: boolean;\r\n  createdAt: Date;\r\n  updatedAt: Date;\r\n  startedAt: Date | null;\r\n  completedAt: Date | null;\r\n  closedAt: Date | null;\r\n  transitions?: Array<{ sequence: number; fromState: string | null; toState: string; metadata: string; createdAt: Date }>;\r\n  waypoint?: { name: string };\r\n  waypointVersion?: { versionNumber: number; lifecycleStatus: string };\r\n}>(attempt: T) {\r\n  return {\r\n    ...attempt,\r\n    createdAt: attempt.createdAt.toISOString(),\r\n    updatedAt: attempt.updatedAt.toISOString(),\r\n    startedAt: attempt.startedAt?.toISOString() ?? null,\r\n    completedAt: attempt.completedAt?.toISOString() ?? null,\r\n    closedAt: attempt.closedAt?.toISOString() ?? null,\r\n    transitions: attempt.transitions?.map((transition) => ({\r\n      ...transition,\r\n      metadata: parseJson(transition.metadata),\r\n      createdAt: transition.createdAt.toISOString(),\r\n    })),\r\n  };\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function createVerificationAttempt(unchecked: unknown, actor: { playerId?: string; actorId?: string }) {\r\n  const input = createAttemptSchema.parse(unchecked);\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const session = await tx.taleSession.findUnique({\r\n      where: { id: input.sessionId },\r\n      include: { version: true, tale: true },\r\n    });\r\n    if (!session || !session.publishedVersionId || !session.version)\r\n      throw new VisionDomainError(\"SESSION_NOT_VERSIONED\", \"A published Tall Tale session is required.\");\r\n    if (session.currentBlockId !== input.blockId)\r\n      throw new VisionDomainError(\"STORY_STAGE_CHANGED\", \"The story is no longer at this Vision Waypoint block.\");\r\n    const binding = await tx.storyWaypointBinding.findFirst({\r\n      where: {\r\n        storyId: session.taleId,\r\n        blockId: input.blockId,\r\n        waypointVersionId: input.waypointVersionId,\r\n        runtimeMode: \"DEVELOPMENT_MOCK\",\r\n      },\r\n      include: { waypointVersion: { include: { publication: true } } },\r\n    });\r\n    if (!binding?.waypointVersion.publishedAt || !binding.waypointVersion.publication)\r\n      throw new VisionDomainError(\"PUBLISHED_VERSION_REQUIRED\", \"The active story binding is not publishable Vision data.\");\r\n    const request = await tx.taleVerificationRequest.findFirst({\r\n      where: { sessionId: session.id, blockId: input.blockId, providerType: \"visionLocation\", status: \"PENDING\" },\r\n      orderBy: { requestedAt: \"desc\" },\r\n    });\r\n    if (!request)\r\n      throw new VisionDomainError(\"VERIFICATION_REQUEST_REQUIRED\", \"The story has no active Vision verification request.\");\r\n    const id = randomUUID();\r\n    const messageId = randomUUID();\r\n    const idempotencyKey = `vision:${session.id}:${input.blockId}:${input.waypointVersionId}:${id}:result`;\r\n    const envelope = createVisionEnvelope({\r\n      messageType: \"runtime.scan.start\",\r\n      messageId,\r\n      sessionId: session.id,\r\n      sender: {\r\n        type: input.platform === \"DESKTOP\" ? \"desktop\" : \"web\",\r\n        instanceId: input.platform === \"DESKTOP\" ? \"desktop-b1-instance\" : \"web-b1-instance\",\r\n      },\r\n      payload: {\r\n        attemptId: id,\r\n        storyId: session.taleId,\r\n        stageId: input.blockId,\r\n        waypointId: binding.waypointId,\r\n        waypointVersionId: binding.waypointVersionId,\r\n        mockScenario: input.scenario,\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    const attempt = await tx.verificationAttempt.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        id,\r\n        storyId: session.taleId,\r\n        stageId: input.blockId,\r\n        playerId: actor.playerId ?? null,\r\n        sessionId: session.id,\r\n        verificationRequestId: request.id,\r\n        waypointId: binding.waypointId,\r\n        waypointVersionId: binding.waypointVersionId,\r\n        platform: input.platform,\r\n        adapterType: input.adapterType,\r\n        mockScenario: input.scenario,\r\n        protocolVersion: VISION_PROTOCOL_VERSION,\r\n        protocolMessageId: envelope.messageId,\r\n        idempotencyKey,\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    await tx.verificationAttemptTransition.create({\r\n      data: { attemptId: attempt.id, sequence: 0, fromState: null, toState: \"IDLE\", metadata: \"{}\" },\r\n    });\r\n    const armed = await appendTransition(tx, attempt, \"ARMED\", { protocolMessageId: envelope.messageId });\r\n    await tx.platformAuditEvent.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        actorType: actor.playerId ? \"PLAYER\" : \"CAPTAIN\",\r\n        actorId: actor.playerId ?? actor.actorId ?? null,\r\n        action: \"VISION_VERIFICATION_ATTEMPT_CREATED\",\r\n        resourceType: \"VERIFICATION_ATTEMPT\",\r\n        resourceId: attempt.id,\r\n        correlationId: envelope.messageId,\r\n        metadata: JSON.stringify({\r\n          storyId: session.taleId,\r\n          stageId: input.blockId,\r\n          waypointVersionId: binding.waypointVersionId,\r\n          platform: input.platform,\r\n          adapterType: input.adapterType,\r\n          mockScenario: input.scenario,\r\n        }),\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    return publicAttempt(armed);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function getVerificationAttempt(attemptId: string) {\r\n  const attempt = await db.verificationAttempt.findUniqueOrThrow({\r\n    where: { id: attemptId },\r\n    include: {\r\n      transitions: { orderBy: { sequence: \"asc\" } },\r\n      waypoint: { select: { name: true } },\r\n      waypointVersion: { select: { versionNumber: true, lifecycleStatus: true } },\r\n    },\r\n  });\r\n  return publicAttempt(attempt);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function listVerificationAttempts(input: { sessionId: string; limit?: number }) {\r\n  const attempts = await db.verificationAttempt.findMany({\r\n    where: { sessionId: input.sessionId },\r\n    orderBy: { createdAt: \"desc\" },\r\n    take: Math.min(Math.max(input.limit ?? 20, 1), 100),\r\n    include: {\r\n      transitions: { orderBy: { sequence: \"asc\" } },\r\n      waypoint: { select: { name: true } },\r\n      waypointVersion: { select: { versionNumber: true, lifecycleStatus: true } },\r\n    },\r\n  });\r\n  return attempts.map(publicAttempt);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function cancelVerificationAttempt(attemptId: string, actorId?: string) {\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const attempt = await tx.verificationAttempt.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: attemptId } });\r\n    const state = verificationAttemptStateSchema.parse(attempt.attemptState);\r\n    if ([\"VERIFIED\", \"INSUFFICIENT\", \"NOT_AT_TARGET\", \"AMBIGUOUS\", \"ERROR\", \"CANCELLED\", \"CLOSED\"].includes(state))\r\n      throw new VisionDomainError(\"ATTEMPT_ALREADY_TERMINAL\", \"This attempt can no longer be cancelled.\");\r\n    const cancelled = await appendTransition(tx, attempt, \"CANCELLED\", { actorId });\r\n    return publicAttempt(\r\n      await tx.verificationAttempt.update({\r\n        where: { id: attemptId },\r\n        data: {\r\n          result: \"CANCELLED\",\r\n          guidanceCode: \"ATTEMPT_CANCELLED\",\r\n          eventDeliveryStatus: \"NOT_DELIVERED\",\r\n        },\r\n      }),\r\n    );\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function markStale(attemptId: string, guidanceCode: string) {\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const attempt = await tx.verificationAttempt.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: attemptId } });\r\n    let current = attempt;\r\n    const state = verificationAttemptStateSchema.parse(current.attemptState);\r\n    if (![\"ERROR\", \"CANCELLED\", \"CLOSED\"].includes(state)) current = await appendTransition(tx, current, \"ERROR\", { guidanceCode });\r\n    return tx.verificationAttempt.update({\r\n      where: { id: attemptId },\r\n      data: {\r\n        result: \"SYSTEM_ERROR\",\r\n        guidanceCode,\r\n        eventDeliveryStatus: \"REJECTED_STALE\",\r\n        staleResultRejected: true,\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function executeProgressPath(attemptId: string) {\r\n  for (const state of [\r\n    \"CAPTURING\",\r\n    \"CURATING_FRAMES\",\r\n    \"RETRIEVING\",\r\n    \"MATCHING\",\r\n    \"LOCALIZING\",\r\n    \"EVALUATING_SEQUENCE\",\r\n    \"EVALUATING_SPECIAL_RULES\",\r\n  ] as const)\r\n    await transitionVerificationAttempt(attemptId, state, { implementation: \"deterministic-development-mock\" });\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction resultSubmissionValue(result: VerificationResult) {\r\n  if (result === \"VERIFIED\") return \"match\" as const;\r\n  if (result === \"AMBIGUOUS\" || result === \"INSUFFICIENT_VISUAL_EVIDENCE\") return \"uncertain\" as const;\r\n  return \"notMatch\" as const;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function deliverMockVerificationResult(\r\n  attemptId: string,\r\n  uncheckedScenario?: unknown,\r\n  actor: { sourceType: \"mock\" | \"desktopMock\" | \"captainMock\"; sourceId?: string } = { sourceType: \"mock\" },\r\n) {\r\n  const initial = await db.verificationAttempt.findUniqueOrThrow({ where: { id: attemptId } });\r\n  const scenario = mockVisionScenarioSchema.parse(uncheckedScenario ?? initial.mockScenario ?? \"verified\");\r\n  if (initial.attemptState === \"CANCELLED\")\r\n    throw new VisionDomainError(\"ATTEMPT_CANCELLED\", \"A cancelled attempt cannot later verify.\");\r\n  if (scenario === \"cancelled\") return cancelVerificationAttempt(attemptId, actor.sourceId);\r\n  await executeProgressPath(attemptId);\r\n  if (scenario === \"delayed_verified\") await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 750));\r\n\r\n  const attempt = await db.verificationAttempt.findUniqueOrThrow({\r\n    where: { id: attemptId },\r\n    include: { session: { include: { version: true } }, verificationRequest: true },\r\n  });\r\n  if (!attempt.session || !attempt.verificationRequest || !attempt.session.version)\r\n    throw new VisionDomainError(\"ATTEMPT_CONTEXT_MISSING\", \"The attempt has no active story context.\");\r\n  const stageChanged = attempt.session.currentBlockId !== attempt.stageId || attempt.verificationRequest.status !== \"PENDING\";\r\n  if (scenario === \"stale_stage\" || stageChanged) {\r\n    const stale = await markStale(attemptId, stageChanged ? \"STORY_STAGE_CHANGED\" : \"ATTEMPT_STALE\");\r\n    logger.info(\r\n      { area: \"vision-mock\", attemptId, scenario, stageChanged },\r\n      \"Deterministic mock result rejected as stale\",\r\n    );\r\n    return publicAttempt(stale);\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const outcome = scenarioOutcome[scenario];\r\n  const evidenceDigest = createHash(\"sha256\")\r\n    .update(\r\n      JSON.stringify({\r\n        implementation: \"b1-deterministic-mock\",\r\n        attemptId,\r\n        scenario,\r\n        storyId: attempt.storyId,\r\n        stageId: attempt.stageId,\r\n        waypointVersionId: attempt.waypointVersionId,\r\n        result: outcome.result,\r\n      }),\r\n    )\r\n    .digest(\"hex\");\r\n  const messageId = randomUUID();\r\n  createVisionEnvelope({\r\n    messageType: \"runtime.scan.result\",\r\n    messageId,\r\n    requestId: attempt.protocolMessageId ?? undefined,\r\n    sessionId: attempt.session.id,\r\n    sender: { type: actor.sourceType === \"desktopMock\" ? \"desktop\" : \"server\", instanceId: \"b1-mock-verifier\" },\r\n    payload: {\r\n      attemptId,\r\n      storyId: attempt.storyId,\r\n      stageId: attempt.stageId,\r\n      waypointVersionId: attempt.waypointVersionId,\r\n      result: outcome.result,\r\n      guidanceCode: outcome.guidanceCode,\r\n      evidenceDigest,\r\n    },\r\n  });\r\n  await db.verificationAttempt.update({\r\n    where: { id: attemptId },\r\n    data: {\r\n      result: outcome.result,\r\n      guidanceCode: outcome.guidanceCode,\r\n      evidenceDigest,\r\n      protocolMessageId: messageId,\r\n    },\r\n  });\r\n  await transitionVerificationAttempt(attemptId, terminalStateForResult(outcome.result), {\r\n    result: outcome.result,\r\n    guidanceCode: outcome.guidanceCode,\r\n  });\r\n\r\n  if ([\"SYSTEM_ERROR\", \"CANCELLED\"].includes(outcome.result)) {\r\n    const terminal = await db.verificationAttempt.update({\r\n      where: { id: attemptId },\r\n      data: { eventDeliveryStatus: \"NOT_DELIVERED\" },\r\n      include: { transitions: { orderBy: { sequence: \"asc\" } } },\r\n    });\r\n    return publicAttempt(terminal);\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  let delivered = false;\r\n  let duplicateRejected = false;\r\n  try {\r\n    const submission = {\r\n      schemaVersion: 1 as const,\r\n      eventId: messageId,\r\n      idempotencyKey: `${attempt.idempotencyKey}:story`,\r\n      eventType: \"verification.result\" as const,\r\n      providerType: \"visionLocation\" as const,\r\n      providerInstanceId: actor.sourceType === \"desktopMock\" ? \"desktop-b1-mock\" : \"web-b1-mock\",\r\n      sessionId: attempt.session.id,\r\n      publishedVersionId: attempt.session.publishedVersionId!,\r\n      blockId: attempt.stageId,\r\n      verificationRequestId: attempt.verificationRequest.id,\r\n      observedAt: new Date().toISOString(),\r\n      result: resultSubmissionValue(outcome.result),\r\n      evidence: {\r\n        evidenceDigest,\r\n        waypointId: attempt.waypointId,\r\n        waypointVersionId: attempt.waypointVersionId,\r\n        mockScenario: scenario,\r\n", "type": visionWaypointDraftConfigurationSchema.parse(parseJson(version.draftConfiguration));\r\n    const packageValue = developmentVisionPackageSchema.parse({\r\n      packageSchemaVersion: 1,\r\n parsedScenario,\r\n      compatibility: { protocol: \"1.0\", minimumAppVersion: \"0.3.0-b1\" },\r\n    });\r\n    const serializedPackage = JSON.stringify(packageValue);\r\n    const packageHash = publishedAt,\r\n        packageArtifactReference: artifactReference,\r\n        compatibilityMetadata: JSON.stringify({\r\n          protocol: \"1.0\",\r\n          packageSchemaVersion: 1,\r\n string) {\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const version = await tx.visionWaypointVersion.findFirstOrThrow({\r\n      where: { id: versionId, waypoint: { ownerId: actorId }, publishedAt: { not: null } },\r\n      include: { publication: true },\r\n    });\r\n    if (!version.publication) throw new VisionDomainError(\"VERSION_NOT_PUBLISHED\", \"Only a published version can be deprecated.\");\r\n    const deprecatedAt = version.deprecatedAt ?? new Date();\r\n    const updated = await tx.visionWaypointVersion.update({\r\n      where: { id: versionId },\r\n      data: { lifecycleStatus: \"DEPRECATED\", deprecatedAt },\r\n    });\r\n    await tx.visionWaypointPublication.update({ where: { waypointVersionId: versionId }, data: { status: \"DEPRECATED\" } });\r\n    await audit(tx, {\r\n      actorId,\r\n      action: \"VISION_WAYPOINT_VERSION_DEPRECATED\",\r\n      resourceType: \"VISION_WAYPOINT_VERSION\",\r\n      resourceId: versionId,\r\n      metadata: { waypointId: version.waypointId, retainedBindings: true },\r\n    });\r\n    return { id: updated.id, deprecatedAt: deprecatedAt.toISOString(), lifecycleStatus: updated.lifecycleStatus };\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function getLatestDraft(waypointId: string, actorId: string) {\r\n  const version = await db.visionWaypointVersion.findFirst({\r\n    where: { waypointId, waypoint: { ownerId: actorId }, publishedAt: null },\r\n    orderBy: { versionNumber: \"desc\" },\r\n    include: { publication: true },\r\n  });\r\n  return version ? publicVersion(version) : null;\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function getPublishedVersions(waypointId: string, actorId: string) {\r\n  const versions = await db.visionWaypointVersion.findMany({\r\n    where: { waypointId, waypoint: { ownerId: actorId }, publishedAt: { not: null } },\r\n    orderBy: { versionNumber: \"desc\" },\r\n    include: { publication: true },\r\n  });\r\n  return versions.map(publicVersion);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function getWaypointUsage(waypointId: string, actorId: string) {\r\n  const waypoint = await db.visionWaypoint.findFirstOrThrow({ where: { id: waypointId, ownerId: actorId } });\r\n  const bindings = await db.storyWaypointBinding.findMany({\r\n    where: { waypointId: waypoint.id },\r\n    include: { story: { select: { id: true, title: true, slug: true } }, block: { select: { id: true, title: true } }, waypointVersion: true },\r\n  });\r\n  return bindings.map((binding) => ({\r\n    id: binding.id,\r\n    story: binding.story,\r\n    block: binding.block,\r\n    waypointVersion: {\r\n      id: binding.waypointVersion.id,\r\n      versionNumber: binding.waypointVersion.versionNumber,\r\n      lifecycleStatus: binding.waypointVersion.lifecycleStatus,\r\n    },\r\n  AMBIGUOUS: [\"RESULT_DISPLAYED\", \"CLOSED\"],\r\n  ERROR: [\"RESULT_DISPLAYED\", \"CLOSED\"],\r\n  CANCELLED: [\"RESULT_DISPLAYED\", \"CLOSED\"],\r\n  EVENT_DELIVERED: [\"RESULT_DISPLAYED\", \"CLOSED\"],\r\n  RESULT_DISPLAYED: [\"CLOSED\"],\r\n  CLOSED: [],\r\n};\r\n\r\nexport class VisionDomainError extends Error {\r\n  constructor(\r\n    public readonly code: string,\r\n    message: string,\r\n  ) {\r\n    super(message);\r\n  }\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function assertAttemptTransition(from: VerificationAttemptState, to: VerificationAttemptState) {\r\n  if (!allowedAttemptTransitions[from].includes(to))\r\n    throw new VisionDomainError(\"INVALID_ATTEMPT_TRANSITION\", `Attempt cannot move from ${from} to ${to}.`);\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function terminalStateForResult(result: VerificationResult): VerificationAttemptState {\r\n  if (result === \"VERIFIED\") return \"VERIFIED\";\r\n  if (result === \"INSUFFICIENT_VISUAL_EVIDENCE\") return \"INSUFFICIENT\";\r\n  if (result === \"NOT_AT_TARGET\") return \"NOT_AT_TARGET\";\r\n  if (result === \"AMBIGUOUS\") return \"AMBIGUOUS\";\r\n  if (result === \"CANCELLED\") return \"CANCELLED\";\r\n  return \"ERROR\";\r\n}\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]
