5000), 15_000));\n+  const mode = configuration.scanMode === \"TOGGLE\" ? \"TOGGLE\" : \"HOLD\";\n+\n+  function stopHold() {\n+    if (timer.current) clearInterval(timer.current);\n+    timer.current = null;\n+    setHolding(false);\n+    setHoldProgress(0);\n+  }\n+\n+  async function scan() {\n+    stopHold();\n+    setError(\"\");\n+    setResult(null);\n+    try {\n+      const completed = await adapter.beginPlayerScan({\n+        sessionId,\n+        blockId,\n+        waypointVersionId,\n+        scenario,\n+        csrfToken,\n+        onProgress: setStatus,\n+      });\n+      setResult(completed);\n+      setStatus(completed.attemptState);\n+      if (completed.result === \"VERIFIED\") await onStoryChanged();\n+    } catch (cause) {\n+      setStatus(\"ERROR\");\n+      setError(cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"Inspection failed.\");\n+    }\n+  }\n+\n+  function beginHold() {\n+    if (holding || !waypointVersionId || status === \"CAPTURING\") return;\n+    if (mode === \"TOGGLE\") {\n+      void scan();\n+      return;\n+    }\n+    setHolding(true);\n+    startedAt.current = performance.now();\n+    timer.current = setInterval(() => {\n+      const fraction = Math.min(1, (performance.now() - startedAt.current) / holdDuration);\n+      setHoldProgress(fraction);\n+      if (fraction >= 1) void scan();\n+    }, 50);\n+  }\n+\n+  useEffect(() => () => { if (timer.current) clearInterval(timer.current); }, []);\n+\n+  const busy = [\"ARMED\", \"CAPTURING\", \"CURATING_FRAMES\", \"RETRIEVING\", \"MATCHING\", \"LOCALIZING\", \"EVALUATING_SEQUENCE\", \"EVALUATING_SPECIAL_RULES\"].includes(status);\n+  return (\n+    <section className=\"vision-scan-control\" aria-labelledby=\"vision-scan-title\">\n+      <p className=\"eyebrow\">Development Vision · {adapter.getPlatformKind()}</p>\n+      <h2 id=\"vision-scan-title\">{String(configuration.prompt ?? \"Inspect Surroundings\")}</h2>\n+      <p>This Phase B-1 control uses a deterministic mock. It does not access a camera or the game.</p>\n+      {process.env.NODE_ENV !== \"production\" && (\n+        <label className=\"vision-scenario\">\n+          <span>Test scenario</span>\n+          <select value={scenario} disabled={busy || holding} onChange={(event) => setScenario(event.target.value as MockVisionScenario)}>\n+            {mockVisionScenarios.map((item) => <option key={item} value={item}>{item.replaceAll(\"_\", \" \")}</option>)}\n+          </select>\n+        </label>\n+      )}\n+      <button\n+        type=\"button\"\n+        className={`vision-hold-button ${holding ? \"holding\" : \"\"}`}\n+        disabled={busy || !waypointVersionId}\n+        style={{ \"--scan-progress\": `${holdProgress * 100}%` } as React.CSSProperties}\n+        onPointerDown={beginHold}\n+        onPointerUp={() => { if (holding && holdProgress < 1) stopHold(); }}\n+        onPointerCancel={stopHold}\n+        onPointerLeave={() => { if (holding && holdProgress < 1) stopHold(); }}\n+        onKeyDown={(event) => { if ((event.key === \" \" || event.key === \"Enter\") && !event.repeat) beginHold(); }}\n+        onKeyUp={(event) => { if ((event.key === \" \" || event.key === \"Enter\") && holding && holdProgress < 1) stopHold(); }}\n+      >\n+        {busy ? \"Inspecting…\" : mode === \"HOLD\" ? (holding ? `Keep holding · ${Math.ceil(holdProgress * 100)}%` : \"Hold to Inspect Surroundings\") : \"Start Inspecting\"}\n+      </button>\n+      <div className=\"vision-scan-status\" role=\"status\" aria-live=\"polite\">\n+        <strong>{status.replaceAll(\"_\", \" \").toLocaleLowerCase()}</strong>\n+        {result?.result && <p>{resultCopy[result.result] ?? result.guidanceCode ?? \"Inspection complete.\"}</p>}\n+        {result?.duplicateResultRejected && <p>Duplicate result safely rejected; story progress was written exactly once.</p>}\n+        {error && <p className=\"runtime-error\">{error}</p>}\n+      </div>\n+    </section>\n+  );\n+}\n*** Update File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\src\\components\\tales\\PlayerRuntime.tsx\n@@\n import type { JsonObject } from \"@/tall-tale/types\";\n+import { VisionScanControl } from \"@/components/player/VisionScanControl\";\n@@\n           ) : state.pendingVerification?.providerType === \"captainManual\" ? (\n@@\n-          ) : state.pendingVerification?.providerType === \"textAnswer\" ? (\n+          ) : state.pendingVerification?.providerType === \"visionLocation\" ? (\n+            <VisionScanControl\n+              sessionId={sessionId}\n+              blockId={state.block?.id ?? \"\"}\n+              waypointVersionId={String(state.block?.configuration.waypointVersionId ?? \"\")}\n+              csrfToken={state.csrfToken}\n+              configuration={state.block?.configuration ?? {}}\n+              onStoryChanged={load}\n+            />\n+          ) : state.pendingVerification?.providerType === \"textAnswer\" ? const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\src\\components\\captain\\VisionAttemptDiagnostics.tsx\n+\"use client\";\n+\n+import { useCallback, useEffect, useMemo, useState } from \"react\";\n+\n+type Attempt = {\n+  id: string;\n+  attemptState: string;\n+  result: string | null;\n+  guidanceCode: string | null;\n+  eventDeliveryStatus: string;\n+  duplicateResultRejected: boolean;\n+  staleResultRejected: boolean;\n+  platform: string;\n+  adapterType: string;\n+  protocolVersion: string;\n+  createdAt: string;\n+  waypoint?: { name: string };\n+  waypointVersion?: { versionNumber: number; lifecycleStatus: string };\n+  transitions?: Array<{ sequence: number; toState: string; createdAt: string }>;\n+};\n+\n+export function VisionAttemptDiagnostics({ sessionId }: { sessionId: string }) {\n+  const [attempts, setAttempts] = useState<Attempt[]>([]);\n+  const [message, setMessage] = useState(\"\");\n+  const load = useCallback(async () => {\n+    const response = await fetch(`/api/verification-attempts?sessionId=${encodeURIComponent(sessionId)}`, { cache: \"no-store\" });\n+    if (!response.ok) return;\n+    const body = (await response.json()) as { attempts: Attempt[] };\n+    setAttempts(body.attempts);\n+  }, [sessionId]);\n+  useEffect(() => {\n+    queueMicrotask(() => void load());\n+    const timer = setInterval(() => void load(), 2000);\n+    return () => clearInterval(timer);\n+  }, [load]);\n+  const diagnostic = useMemo(\n+    () => JSON.stringify({\n+      generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),\n+      appVersion: \"0.3.0-b1\",\n+      protocolVersion: \"1.0\",\n+      packageSchemaVersion: 1,\n+      implementation: \"deterministic-development-mock\",\n+      sessionId,\n+      attempts,\n+    }, null, 2),\n+    [attempts, sessionId],\n+  );\n+  return (\n+    <section className=\"vision-diagnostics\">\n+      <p className=\"card-kicker\">Phase B-1 · deterministic Vision</p>\n+      <header>\n+        <div><h2>Vision attempt diagnostics</h2><p>No camera frames or raw evidence are stored.</p></div>\n+        <button onClick={async () => { await navigator.clipboard.writeText(diagnostic); setMessage(\"Diagnostic summary copied.\"); }}>Copy summary</button>\n+      </header>\n+      {message && <p role=\"status\">{message}</p>}\n+      {!attempts.length ? <p>No Vision attempts have been recorded for this session.</p> : (\n+        <ol className=\"vision-attempt-list\">\n+          {attempts.map((attempt) => (\n+            <li key={attempt.id}>\n+              <div><strong>{attempt.waypoint?.name ?? \"Vision Waypoint\"}</strong><code>{attempt.id.slice(0, 12)}</code></div>\n+              <dl>\n+                <div><dt>State</dt><dd>{attempt.attemptState}</dd></div>\n+                <div><dt>Result</dt><dd>{attempt.result ?? \"pending\"}</dd></div>\n+                <div><dt>Delivery</dt><dd>{attempt.eventDeliveryStatus}</dd></div>\n+                <div><dt>Runtime</dt><dd>{attempt.platform} · {attempt.adapterType}</dd></div>\n+                <div><dt>Contract</dt><dd>protocol {attempt.protocolVersion} · package v1</dd></div>\n+              </dl>\n+              {(attempt.duplicateResultRejected || attempt.staleResultRejected) && (\n+                <p className=\"diagnostic-guardrail\">{attempt.duplicateResultRejected ? \"Duplicate rejected\" : \"Stale result rejected\"}</p>\n+              )}\n+              <details><summary>State transitions</summary><ol>{attempt.transitions?.map((step) => <li key={step.sequence}>{step.sequence}. {step.toState}</li>)}</ol></details>\n+            </li>\n+          ))}\n+        </ol>\n+      )}\n+    </section>\n+  );\n+}\n*** Update File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\src\\components\\captain\\CaptainSessionControl.tsx\n@@\n import type { JsonObject } from \"@/tall-tale/types\";\n+import { VisionAttemptDiagnostics } from \"@/components/captain/VisionAttemptDiagnostics\";\n@@\n         <section className=\"event-ledger\">\n@@\n         </section>\n+        <VisionAttemptDiagnostics sessionId={sessionId} />\n*** End Patch";

.includes(query.toLocaleLowerCase()),\r\n    );\r\n    return filtered.sort((left, right) =>\r\n      sort === \"title\"\r\n        ? left.title.localeCompare(right.title)\r\n        : sort ===  <header className=\"studio-home-header\">\r\n        <div>\r\n          <p className=\"eyebrow\">Authoring waters</p>\r\n          <h1>Tall Tale Studio</h1>\r\n          <p>Draft stories, bind their assets, and publish voyages from one authoritative chart.</p>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <nav aria-label=\"Studio destinations\">\r\n          <Link href=\"/tales\">Player catalog</Link>\r\n <option value=\"title\">Title</option>\r\n            <option value=\"status\">Status</option>\r\n          </select>\r\n        </label>\r\n        <p aria-live=\"polite\">\r\n {error}\r\n        </p>\r\n      )}\r\n      {!visible.length ? \"Creating…\" : \"Create draft waypoint\"}</button>\n+        </form>\n+        <section className=\"vision-library-results\">\n+          <label><span>Search</span><input type=\"search\" value={query} onChange={(event) => setQuery(event.target.value)} placeholder=\"Name, description, or type\" /></label>\n+          {error && <p role=\"alert\" className=\"studio-error\">{error}</p>}\n+          <p>{visible.length} waypoint{visible.length === 1 ? \"\" : \"s\"}</p>\n+          <div className=\"vision-waypoint-grid\">\n+            {visible.map((item) => { const latest = item.versions[0]; return (\n+              <article key={item.id}>\n+                <p className=\"card-kicker\">{item.type.replaceAll(\"_\", \" \")} · {item.sharingScope.toLocaleLowerCase()}</p>\n+                <h2>{item.name}</h2><p>{item.description || \"No description yet.\"}</p>\n+                <dl><div><dt>Latest</dt><dd>{latest ? `v${latest.versionNumber} · ${latest.lifecycleStatus.toLocaleLowerCase()}` : \"none\"}</dd></div><div><dt>Story uses</dt><dd>{item.usageCount}</dd></div></dl>\n+                <Link className=\"brass-button\" href={`/studio/vision-waypoints/${item.id}`}>Open waypoint</Link>\n+              </article>\n+            ); })}\n+          </div>\n+        </section>\n+      </section>\n+    </main>\n+  );\n+}\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\src\\app\\studio\\vision-waypoints\\page.tsx\n+import { VisionWaypointLibrary } from \"@/components/studio/VisionWaypointLibrary\";\n+import { requireGmCapability } from \"@/lib/security\";\n+export const dynamic = \"force-dynamic\";\n+export default async function VisionWaypointsPage() {\n+  return <VisionWaypointLibrary authenticated={Boolean(await requireGmCapability(\"CREATE_TALES\"))} />;\n+}\n*** End Patch";

await tx.visionWaypointVersion.findFirstOrThrow({\r\n          where: { id: parentVersionId, waypointId, publishedAt: { not: null } },\r\n        })\r\n      : await tx.visionWaypointVersion.findFirst({\r\n          where: { waypointId, publishedAt: { not: null } },\r\n          orderBy: { versionNumber: \"desc\" },\r\n        });\r\n    const latest = await tx.visionWaypointVersion.findFirst({ where: { waypointId }, orderBy: { versionNumber: \"desc\" } });\r\n    const configuration = parent\r\n      ? visionWaypointDraftConfigurationSchema.parse(parseJson(parent.draftConfiguration))\r\n      : defaultDraftConfiguration(waypoint.type as Parameters<typeof defaultDraftConfiguration>[0]);\r\n    const created = await tx.visionWaypointVersion.create({\r\n      data: {\r\n        waypointId,\r\n        versionNumber: (latest?.versionNumber ?? 0) + 1,\r\n        parentVersionId: parent?.id ?? null,\r\n        lifecycleStatus: \"DRAFT\",\r\n        verificationProfile: parent?.verificationProfile ?? configuration.verificationProfile,\r\n        packageSchemaVersion: 1,\r\n        draftConfiguration: JSON.stringify(configuration),\r\n        compatibilityMetadata: parent?.compatibilityMetadata ?? JSON.stringify({ protocol: \"1.0\" }),\r\n        createdBy: actorId,\r\n      },\r\n    });\r\n    await audit(tx, {\r\n      actorId,\r\n      action: \"VISION_WAYPOINT_DRAFT_CREATED\",\r\n      resourceType: \"VISION_WAYPOINT_VERSION\",\r\n      resourceId: created.id,\r\n      metadata: { waypointId, parentVersionId: parent?.id ?? null, versionNumber: created.versionNumber },\r\n    });\r\n    return publicVersion(created);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function updateDraftConfiguration(versionId: string, unchecked: unknown, actorId: string) {\r\n  const configuration = visionWaypointDraftConfigurationSchema.parse(unchecked);\r\n  return db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n    const version = await tx.visionWaypointVersion.findFirstOrThrow({\r\n      where: { id: versionId, waypoint: { ownerId: actorId } },\r\n      include: { waypoint: true },\r\n    });\r\n    if (version.publishedAt || version.lifecycleStatus !== \"DRAFT\")\r\n      throw new VisionDomainError(\"PUBLISHED_VERSION_IMMUTABLE\", \"Only an unpublished draft may be edited.\");\r\n    if (configuration.waypointType !== version.waypoint.type)\r\n      throw new VisionDomainError(\"WAYPOINT_TYPE_MISMATCH\", \"Draft type must match the stable waypoint type.\");\r\n    const updated = await tx.visionWaypointVersion.update({\r\n      where: { id: versionId },\r\n      data: { draftConfiguration: JSON.stringify(configuration), verificationProfile: configuration.verificationProfile },\r\n    });\r\n    await audit(tx, {\r\n      actorId,\r\n      action: \"VISION_WAYPOINT_DRAFT_UPDATED\",\r\n      resourceType: \"VISION_WAYPOINT_VERSION\",\r\n      resourceId: versionId,\r\n      metadata: { waypointId: version.waypointId, verificationProfile: configuration.verificationProfile },\r\n    });\r\n    return publicVersion(updated);\r\n  });\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport async function publishDraftVersion(\r\n  versionId: string,\r\n  actorId: string,\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

db.platformRoleAssignment.update({ where: { id: existing.id }, data: { revokedAt: null } });\r\n    } else {\r\n      await db.platformRoleAssignment.create({\r\n        data: { accountId: userId, {} } },\r\n    include: { events: { orderBy: { sequence: \"asc\" } } },\r\n  });\r\n  for (const session of legacySessions) {\r\n    await db.$transaction(async (tx) => {\r\n      const placeholder = await tx.playerProfile.create({\r\n        data: {\r\n          displayName: session.ownerLabel?.trim() || `Legacy Player ${session.id.slice(-5)}`,\r\n          status: \"ACTIVE\",\r\n          preferences: JSON.stringify({ migratedLegacySession: session.id }),\r\n        },\r\n      });\r\n      await tx.playthroughMembership.create({\r\n        data: {\r\n          playthroughId: session.ownerLabel ?? `Legacy voyage ${session.id.slice(-5)}`,\r\n          launchedAt: session.launchedAt ?? session.startedAt,\r\n          archiveMetadata: JSON.stringify({ migratedFromLegacySession: true }),\r\n        },\r\n      });\r\n      for (const event of session.events.filter((item) => item.blockId && item.eventType === \"blockEntered\")) {\r\n        await tx.revealState.upsert({\r\n          where: {\r\n            playthroughId_contentType_contentKey: {\r\n              playthroughId: session.id,\r\n              contentType: \"BLOCK\",\r\n playerUsername, legacySessionsMigrated: legacySessions.length };\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function main() {\r\n  if (!supportedPresets.has(preset)) throw new Error(`Unknown development preset: expect(afterPreview.campaign.sequence).toBe(before.campaign.sequence);\r\n\r\n  const stale = await page.request.post(\"/api/gm/commands\", {\r\n    headers: { \"x-csrf-token\": before.csrfToken stale.json()).code).toBe(\"STALE_SEQUENCE\");\r\n\r\n  const key = crypto.randomUUID();\r\n  const request = {\r\n    command: \"REQUEST_RECONCILIATION\",\r\n    campaignSlug: page.request.post(\"/api/gm/commands\", {\r\n    headers: { \"x-csrf-token\": before.csrfToken },\r\n    data: request,\r\n  });\r\n  expect(first.ok()).toBeTruthy();\r\n  const replay = await Never use these credentials in production.\r\nDATABASE_URL=\"file:./dev.db\"\r\nSESSION_SECRET=\"$secret\"\r\nGM_USERNAME=\"kato\"\r\nGM_PASSWORD=\"development-captain-only\"\r\nPLAYER_ACCESS_CODE=\"development-moonwake\"\r\nNEXT_PUBLIC_APP_URL=\"http://127.0.0.1:3000\"\r\nLOG_LEVEL=\"info\"\r\n\"@ | Set-Content -LiteralPath $environmentPath -Encoding UTF8\r\n        Write-Host \"Created ignored local development configuration at $environmentPath\" -ForegroundColor DarkGray\r\n    }\r\n    $required = \"DATABASE_URL\", \"SESSION_SECRET\", \"GM_USERNAME\", \"GM_PASSWORD\", \"PLAYER_ACCESS_CODE\"\r\n    $contents = Get-Content -Raw -LiteralPath $environmentPath\r\n    foreach ($name in $required) {\r\n        if ($contents -notmatch \"(?m)^$name=\") { throw \".env exists but is missing $name. It was preserved; add the missing value and retry.\" }\r\n    }\r\n    return $environmentPath\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Import-ForeverEnvironment {\r\n    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Path)\r\n    foreach ($line in Get-Content -LiteralPath $Path) {\r\n        if ($line -match '^\\s*#' -or $line -notmatch '=') { continue }\r\n        $name, $value = $line -split '=', 2\r\n        $value = $value.Trim().Trim('\"').Trim(\"'\")\r\n        [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable($name.Trim(), $value, \"Process\")\r\n    }\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Sync-ForeverRuntime {\r\n    param([ValidateSet(\"development\", \"validation\")][string]$Mode = \"development\")\r\n    $isNetworkPath = $script:ProjectRoot.StartsWith(\"\\\\\")\r\n    if (-not $isNetworkPath -and $Mode -eq \"development\") { return $script:ProjectRoot }\r\n    $runtimeRoot = Join-Path $script:RuntimeBase $Mode\r\n    if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $runtimeRoot)) { New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $runtimeRoot -Force | Out-Null }\r\n    $resolvedBase = (Resolve-Path $script:RuntimeBase).ProviderPath\r\n    $resolvedRuntime = (Resolve-Path $runtimeRoot).ProviderPath\r\n    if (-not $resolvedRuntime.StartsWith($resolvedBase, [StringComparison]::OrdinalIgnoreCase)) { throw \"Unsafe runtime mirror path.\" }\r\n    & robocopy $script:ProjectRoot $runtimeRoot /MIR /XD .git .forever node_modules node_modules.failed .next artifacts coverage test-results playwright-report /XF *.db *.db-journal *.log .forever-dev.json .forever-lock.sha | Out-Null\r\n    if ($LASTEXITCODE -gt 7) { throw \"Unable to synchronize the local runtime mirror (robocopy exit $LASTEXITCODE).\" }\r\n    return $runtimeRoot\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Install-ForeverDependencies {\r\n    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$RuntimeRoot)\r\n    $lock = Join-Path $RuntimeRoot \"package-lock.json\"\r\n    $hash = (Get-FileHash -Algorithm SHA256 -LiteralPath $lock).Hash\r\n    $marker = Join-Path $RuntimeRoot \".forever-lock.sha\"\r\n    $installed = Test-Path (Join-Path $RuntimeRoot \"node_modules\\next\\package.json\")\r\n    $currentHash = if (Test-Path $marker) { (Get-Content -Raw $marker).Trim() } else { \"\" }\r\n    if (-not $installed -or $currentHash -ne $hash) {\r\n        Write-Host \"Installing pinned dependencies...\" -ForegroundColor Cyan\r\n        Invoke-ForeverNpm -WorkingDirectory $RuntimeRoot -Arguments @(\"ci\", \"--no-audit\", \"--no-fund\")\r\n        Set-Content -LiteralPath $marker -Value $hash -Encoding ASCII\r\n    } else { Write-Host \"Dependencies are current.\" -ForegroundColor DarkGray }\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Invoke-ForeverNode {\r\n    param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$WorkingDirectory, [Parameter(ValueFromRemainingArguments)][string[]]$Arguments)\r\n    Push-Location $WorkingDirectory\r\n    try {\r\n        & (Get-ForeverNode) @Arguments | Out-Host\r\n        if ($LASTEXITCODE -ne 0) { throw \"Node command failed with exit code $LASTEXITCODE.\" }\r\n    } finally { Pop-Location }\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction Initialize-ForeverRuntime {\r\n    param([ValidateSet(\"development\", \"validation\")][string]$Mode = \"development\", [switch]$ResetDatabase)\r\n    $environmentPath = Ensure-ForeverEnvironment\r\n    $runtimeRoot = Sync-ForeverRuntime -Mode $Mode\r\n    Install-ForeverDependencies -RuntimeRoot $runtimeRoot\r\n    $runtimeEnvironment = Join-Path $runtimeRoot \".env\"\r\n    Import-ForeverEnvironment -Path $runtimeEnvironment\r\n    if ($Mode -eq \"validation\") { $env:DATABASE_URL = \"file:./validation.db\" }\r\n    $databaseName = if ($Mode -eq \"validation\") { \"validation.db\" } else { \"dev.db\" }\r\n    $databasePath = Join-Path $runtimeRoot \"prisma\\$databaseName\"\r\n    if ($ResetDatabase -and (Test-Path $databasePath)) { Remove-Item -LiteralPath $databasePath -Force }\r\n    if (-not (Test-Path $databasePath)) { New-Item -ItemType File -Path $databasePath | Out-Null }\r\n    Write-Host \"Generating the database client...\" -ForegroundColor Cyan\r\n    Invoke-ForeverNode -WorkingDirectory $runtimeRoot -Arguments @(\"node_modules/prisma/build/index.js\", \"generate\", \"--schema\", \"prisma/schema.sqlite.prisma\")\r\n    Write-Host \"Applying database migrations...\" -ForegroundColor Cyan\r\n    Invoke-ForeverNode -WorkingDirectory $runtimeRoot -Arguments @(\"node_modules/prisma/build/index.js\", \"migrate\", \"deploy\", \"--schema\", \"prisma/schema.sqlite.prisma\")\r\n    if ($Mode -eq \"development\") {\r\n        Write-Host \"Ensuring development seed data without resetting voyage progress...\" -ForegroundColor Cyan\r\n        Invoke-ForeverNode -WorkingDirectory $runtimeRoot -Arguments @(\"node_modules/tsx/dist/cli.mjs\", \"prisma/seed.ts\", \"--ensure\")\r\n    } else {\r\n        Write-Host \"Verifying development seed data...\" -ForegroundColor Cyan\r\n param([Parameter(Mandatory)][string]$Url, [int]$Seconds = 60)\r\n    $deadline = (Get-Date).AddSeconds($Seconds)\r\n    do {\r\n        try { $response = Invoke-WebRequest -UseBasicParsing -Uri $Url -TimeoutSec 2; if ($response.StatusCode -lt 500) { return } } catch { Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500 }\r\n    } while ((Get-Date) -lt $deadline)\r\n    throw \"The application did not become ready at expect(worker).toContain(`\\\"${prefix}\\\"`);\n+    expect(worker).toContain('fetch(request, { cache: \"no-store\" })');\n+  });\n+  it(\"limits the install shell to an offline truth page, manifest, Work Package B1.18: Documentation\r\n\r\nCreate or update:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/\r\n  AR/\r\n    Phase_B1/\r\n      B1_Repository_Audit.md\r\n      B1_Architecture.md\r\n      B1_Domain_Model.md\r\n      B1_Protocol.md\r\n      B1_Desktop_Development.md\r\n      B1_PWA.md\r\n      B1_Test_Plan.md\r\n      B1_Demonstration.md\r\n      B1_Completion_Report.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nAlso update:\r\n\r\n* root developer setup;\r\n* architecture overview;\r\n* environment variables;\r\n* database migration instructions;\r\n* web development commands;\r\n* desktop development commands;\r\n* production build commands;\r\n* test commands;\r\n* feature-flag documentation;\r\n* generated API documentation.\r\n\r\nDocumentation must match actual commands and paths.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 25. Mandatory Phase Demonstration\r\n\r\nName                   Length\r\n----                   ------\r\nB1_Repository_Audit.md  10600\r\n# Phase B-1 Repository Audit\r\n\r\nStatus: complete before implementation  \r\nAudit date: 2026-07-18  \r\nCanonical repository: `https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT.git`  \r\nCanonical integration branch: `main`  \r\nImplementation branch: `codex/phase-b1-foundation`  \r\nStarting commit: `0192a166699c7cb75d63d46d7acfc00ef6640ac6`\r\n\r\n## Governing sources reviewed\r\n\r\nThe implementation is governed, in order, by:\r\n\r\n1. `TT-VISION-GOV-001` version 1.0, Governing Baseline, 127 pages.\r\n2. `TT-VISION-PHASE-B-001` version 1.0, Authorized Implementation Baseline, 147 pages.\r\n3. Existing project architecture and security documentation.\r\n4. Existing repository conventions.\r\n5. The Phase B-1 implementation prompt supplied on 2026-07-18.\r\n\r\nThe two governing PDFs were verified with PDF metadata, extracted text, and rendered-page inspection. Canonical copies are stored under `Development_Docs/AR/Governing_Specification` and `Development_Docs/AR/Phase_B_Roadmap`. The source copies in the original checkout were not modified.\r\n\r\n## Git and concurrent-work safety\r\n\r\nThe original checkout was on `main`, exactly synchronized with `origin/main` at the starting commit after `git fetch --prune origin` and a `0 0` upstream comparison. It contained two untracked governing PDFs. During this audit, a separate Tall Tale UI task also added an untracked `Development_Docs/Canonical_Player_Journal_Implementation_Record.md` in that checkout.\r\n\r\nTo avoid overwriting or interleaving the parallel UI work, B-1 uses the isolated worktree `C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1` on `codex/phase-b1-foundation`. No file in the original checkout was changed, staged, removed, or renamed. Before final integration, B-1 must fetch again, inspect the other task's final tree, and merge or rebase without discarding either change set.\r\n\r\n## Current platform inventory\r\n\r\n| Area | Audited implementation |\r\n| --- | --- |\r\n| Frontend | Next.js 16.2.10 App Router, React 19.2.4, strict TypeScript 5 |\r\n| Backend | Next.js route handlers and server modules in the same application |\r\n| Package manager | npm 11.9.0 with an exact `package-lock.json`; Node 24 is the documented runtime |\r\n| Repository shape | Single-package application, not a monorepo; `@/*` maps to `src/*` |\r\n| Database | Prisma 6.19.3; SQLite is canonical for local/test and MySQL 8 has a maintained parallel schema |\r\n| Migrations | Versioned SQLite migrations under `prisma/migrations`; forward-only MySQL SQL under `prisma/mysql-migrations` |\r\n| Authentication | Database-backed Game Master sessions with bcrypt and CSRF; durable Player identity sessions; legacy opaque playthrough cookies retained for compatibility |\r\n| Authorization | Staff capabilities (`CAPTAIN`, `CREATE_TALES`, `PUBLISH_TALES`, `ADMIN`, and related scopes), Player membership checks, Creator ownership, and API-local resource checks |\r\n| Runtime validation | Zod at route, draft, block, and verification boundaries |\r\n| Logging and audit | Pino structured logging with redaction; `PlatformAuditEvent` for correlated resource actions; ordered session events for runtime truth |\r\n| Testing | Vitest 4.1.10, Testing Library, Playwright 1.56.1, axe accessibility checks, database invariant scripts, production build/restart proof |\r\n| Deployment | Debian/Node/MySQL/NGINX/systemd documentation; no CI workflow is currently committed |\r\n| PWA | No manifest, service worker, install flow, offline shell, or update-state implementation exists |\r\n| Desktop/native | No desktop shell, native bridge, Electron, Tauri, or packaged frontend implementation exists; Rust is not installed in the audited Windows environment |\r\n| API documentation | Route conventions and prose documents exist, but no generated OpenAPI description exists |\r\n\r\n## Existing product and route structure\r\n\r\nThe existing application already satisfies the most important shared-product premise: Player, Captain, and Studio are one Next.js source tree and share the same domain, API, design tokens, animation director, and published story snapshots.\r\n\r\n- Player families: `/player/*`, `/play/[taleSlug]/*`, and the original `/tale/[campaignSlug]` compatibility experience.\r\n- Captain families: `/captain/*` plus `/quartermaster` and its Command Center workspaces.\r\n- Studio families: `/studio/library`, `/studio/tales/*`, settings, assets, locations, artifacts, and version history.\r\n- Dynamic catalog: `/tales`.\r\n\r\nThe desktop shell should therefore package the existing application rather than extract or copy three new applications.\r\n\r\n## Story and persistence architecture\r\n\r\n`TallTale` is the stable authored identity. `TaleDraft`, `TaleChapter`, `StoryBlock`, and `BlockConnection` form the editable graph. `PublishedTaleVersion` freezes a checksummed complete snapshot. `TaleSession` is a version-pinned playthrough aggregate with current chapter/block pointers, concurrency state, ordered events, inventory, variables, and membership.\r\n\r\n`src/tall-tale/progression.ts` is the authoritative runtime engine. `TaleSessionEvent.idempotencyKey` and per-session sequence constraints protect progression. `TaleVerificationRequest` and `TaleVerificationEvent` already provide a versioned provider seam that checks session, published version, current block, provider, request state, observation time, and duplicate idempotency before completion. This seam currently marks `visionLocation` as future-only; B-1 can activate it behind the new server-enforced feature flag instead of building a second progression engine.\r\n\r\nThe Studio editor registry already owns block identity, defaults, fields, runtime Zod schemas, and provider selection. The B-1 story block should extend that registry and preserve its graph/publishing rules. Because Studio autosave replaces chapter/block rows transactionally, `StoryWaypointBinding` synchronization must occur inside that same save transaction so real bindings cannot disappear or point to drafts.\r\n\r\n## Existing event and verification boundaries\r\n\r\nThe system already has the correct truth boundary for B-1:\r\n\r\n```text\r\npublished block activates\r\n  -> TaleVerificationRequest is persisted\r\n  -> a provider submits versioned evidence\r\n  -> current session/version/block/request are rechecked\r\n  -> TaleVerificationEvent is persisted\r\n  -> an ordered TaleSessionEvent is appended\r\n  -> the same completion engine advances exactly once\r\n```\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

## Tool Result: call_yyVeTiZnlKjLAH2cbBz9V6uT

[{"text": "Script completed\nWall time 0.4 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.3 seconds\nOutput:\n# The Forever Treasure Companion\r\n\r\nProduction-oriented foundation and fully automated local demo for a private nautical-fantasy date-night Tall Tale. All committed story material is generic development seed content; no final surprise content belongs in this public repository.\r\n\r\n## Current phase\r\n\r\nThe application is now a unified Tall Tale platform. The cinematic gateway opens distinct Player, Captain, and Creator workspaces without treating role choice as authorization. Players have durable identities, invitation acceptance, a multi-voyage library, live waiting/runtime state, and exact-version archives. Captains can create version-pinned crew voyages, deliver individually hashed link/QR/code/PIN invitations, manage invitation lifecycle, preview the Player-safe surface, and launch accepted crews. Creator Studio retains immutable publishing and now adds structured version comparison, restore-to-draft, and fork provenance.\r\n\r\nThe original companion and expanded Game Master Command Center remain integrated compatibility surfaces. A shared animation director coordinates the harbor, journal, chart, artifact, gateway, and finale experiences without allowing presentation timing to outrun server truth. All committed content remains fictional development seed material.\r\n\r\n## Stack\r\n\r\n- Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, strict TypeScript\r\n- Prisma 6.19.3 with SQLite for local/test and an equivalent MySQL production schema\r\n- GSAP 3.15 for orchestrated scenes and SVG/text work; Motion 12 for React interaction and presence\r\n- StPageFlip for the journal; local Rive and Lottie runtimes with explicit static fallbacks\r\n- bcrypt password/access-code hashing, server-side database sessions, CSRF tokens, and rate limiting\r\n- Vitest and Playwright\r\n\r\n## Reproducible setup\r\n\r\nRequirements: Windows PowerShell 5.1+, Node.js 24, npm 11, and Git. MySQL 8 is only required for production parity; the supported local/demo/test strategy is SQLite.\r\n\r\n### Fastest Windows demo\r\n\r\nAfter cloning, double-click `Start Forever Treasure Dev.cmd`, or run:\r\n\r\n```powershell\r\nnpm run dev:full\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe launcher creates an ignored `.env` when absent, installs the exact lockfile, generates Prisma, applies migrations, creates generic development data only when the voyage is missing, starts the app, checks health, and prints the URLs and credentials. Existing campaign progress is preserved across normal stop/start cycles. Network/UNC workspaces are mirrored to an ignored local runtime under `%LOCALAPPDATA%` because Node and SQLite are unreliable on network shares. Stop it with `npm run dev:stop`.\r\n\r\n- Role gateway: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/`\r\n- Player: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\r\n- Player library: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/player`\r\n- Tall Tale catalog: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tales`\r\n- Player phrase: `development-moonwake`\r\n- Returning Player development login: `sera` / the configured `PLAYER_PASSWORD` (or `PLAYER_ACCESS_CODE` fallback)\r\n- GM: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\r\n- Studio: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/studio`\r\n- Captain: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/captain`\r\n- GM development[REDACTED: LOGIN CREDENTIAL]\r\n\r\nThese values are disposable local defaults, never production credentials. To opt into LAN access, run `powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start-dev.ps1 -Lan`; Windows Firewall and network policy still determine whether another device can connect.\r\n\r\n### Portable manual setup\r\n\r\n```bash\r\ngit clone https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT.git\r\ncd treasurehuntSoT\r\nnpm ci\r\ncp .env.example .env\r\nnpm run db:generate\r\nnpm run db:migrate\r\nnpm run db:seed\r\nnpm run dev\r\n```\r\n\r\nSet unique local values in `.env`; the seedâ€™s fallback values are development-only and must never be used outside an isolated development database. Player path: `/tale/development-forever-treasure`. GM path: `/quartermaster`.\r\n\r\n## Commands\r\n\r\n| Command                                   | Purpose                                                               |\r\n| ----------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |\r\n| `npm run dev:full`                        | Idempotent setup, migration, progress-safe seed check, and server     |\r\n| `npm run dev:stop`                        | Safely stop only the recorded development process                     |\r\n| `npm run validate`                        | Fresh DB, format/lint/types/unit/E2E/a11y/build/restart proof         |\r\n| `npm run dev`                             | Next.js only; assumes setup is already complete                       |\r\n| `npm run build`                           | Production build                                                      |\r\n| `npm start`                               | Start production build                                                |\r\n| `npm test`                                | Domain, command-center, Studio, animation, and component unit tests   |\r\n| `npm run assets:validate`                 | Validate local Lottie/Rive contracts and fallback paths               |\r\n| `npm run test:e2e`                        | Cross-browser player, Command Center, Studio, and animation workflows |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate`                      | Apply local SQLite migrations                                         |\r\n| `npm run db:seed`                         | Load replaceable development content                                  |\r\n| `npm run db:preset -- mid-voyage`         | Reset to a repeatable development-only companion preset               |\r\n| `npm run db:generate:mysql`               | Generate production MySQL client                                      |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:command-center` | Apply the Phase 3 production MySQL migration                          |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:studio`         | Apply the production-parity Tall Tale Studio migration                |\r\n| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:platform`       | Apply the unified Tall Tale Platform migration                        |\r\n\r\nFull setup, validation stages, output locations, clean-clone instructions, and troubleshooting are in [local development](docs/local-development.md) and [testing](docs/testing.md). Unified identity, invitations, libraries, authorization, history, and migration are in [Tall Tale Platform](docs/tall-tale-platform.md); Studio architecture and operations are in [Tall Tale Studio](docs/tall-tale-studio.md). The future recognition seam is in [vision helper boundary](docs/future-vision-helper.md). Animation ownership, scene contracts, assets, performance, and the development lab are indexed from [animation architecture](docs/animation/architecture.md).\r\n\r\n## Repository workflow\r\n\r\n`main` is canonical. At task start: fetch, inspect status/remotes, and pull with rebase when clean. At task end: validate, review the complete diff, commit intentionally, fetch/rebase if needed, push without force, and verify local `HEAD` equals `origin/main`. See [Codex handoff](docs/codex-handoff.md).\r\n\r\n## Important limitations\r\n\r\nThe full story, final Rive artwork/audio, horizontal scale-out for SSE, and production deployment are later work. The current sound is restrained and procedural. The sole third-party visual sample is MIT-licensed, local, documented, and development-only; production uses original SVG/CSS fallbacks. The repository is public, so only generic development content is permitted. See [known limitations](docs/known-limitations.md).\r\n\r\n## Next steps\r\n\r\nAdd CI for the complete validation gate, exercise the parallel MySQL schema in an integration environment, and replace process-local SSE fan-out before multi-instance deployment. Real surprise content remains blocked until repository visibility is verified private.\r\n\r\nName                                                      Mode  \r\n----                                                      ----  \r\nAR                                                        d-----\r\n.gitignore                                                -a----\r\nForever_Treasure_Cinematic_Animation_Codex_Prompt.pdf     -a----\r\nREADME.md                                                 -a----\r\nTall_Tale_Platform_Codex_Implementation_Specification.pdf -a----\r\nTall_Tale_Platform_Implementation_Record.md               -a----\r\nTall_Tale_Studio_Phase_1_Codex_Implementation_Prompt.pdf  -a----\r\nREADME.md:29:npm run dev:full\r\nREADME.md:32:The launcher creates an ignored `.env` when absent, installs the exact lockfile, generates Prisma, applies migrations, creates generic development data only when the voyage is missing, starts the app, checks health, and prints the URLs and credentials. Existing campaign progress is preserved across normal stop/start cycles. Network/UNC workspaces are mirrored to an ignored local runtime under `%LOCALAPPDATA%` because Node and SQLite are unreliable on network shares. Stop it with `npm run dev:stop`.\r\nREADME.md:54:npm run db:generate\r\nREADME.md:55:npm run db:migrate\r\nREADME.md:56:npm run db:seed\r\nREADME.md:57:npm run dev\r\nREADME.md:66:| `npm run dev:full`                        | Idempotent setup, migration, progress-safe seed check, and server     |\r\nREADME.md:67:| `npm run dev:stop`                        | Safely stop only the recorded development process                     |\r\nREADME.md:68:| `npm run validate`                        | Fresh DB, format/lint/types/unit/E2E/a11y/build/restart proof         |\r\nREADME.md:69:| `npm run dev`                             | Next.js only; const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\README.md\n@@\n-The application is now a unified Tall Tale platform. The cinematic gateway opens distinct Player, Captain, and Creator workspaces without treating role choice as authorization. Players have durable identities, invitation acceptance, a multi-voyage library, live waiting/runtime state, and exact-version archives. Captains can create version-pinned crew voyages, deliver individually hashed link/QR/code/PIN invitations, manage invitation lifecycle, preview the Player-safe surface, and launch accepted crews. Creator Studio retains immutable publishing and now adds structured version comparison, restore-to-draft, and fork provenance.\n+The application is now a unified Tall Tale platform. The cinematic gateway opens distinct Player, Captain, and Creator workspaces without treating role choice as authorization. Phase B-1 adds persisted, immutable-versioned Vision Waypoints, a strict protocol, platform adapters, an installable PWA shell, and a restricted Electron Windows shell without creating separate Player, Captain, or Studio applications. The authorized B-1 verifier is explicitly deterministic development-only: it does not capture a camera, inspect the game, or perform real vision inference.\n@@\n - Studio: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/studio`\n+- Vision Waypoint Studio: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/studio/vision-waypoints`\n@@\n | `npm run db:migrate:mysql:platform`       | Apply the unified Tall Tale Platform migration                        |\n+| `npm run db:migrate:mysql:vision`         | Apply the additive Phase B-1 MySQL migration                          |\n+| `npm run desktop:dev`                     | Start the shared Next.js UI in the restricted Electron shell          |\n+| `npm run desktop:test`                    | Test the desktop command allowlist and input validation                |\n+| `npm run desktop:build`                   | Build Next standalone and a Windows NSIS installer                     |\n@@\n-Full setup, validation stages, output locations, clean-clone instructions, and troubleshooting are in [local development](docs/local-development.md) and [testing](docs/testing.md). Unified identity, invitations, libraries, authorization, history, and migration are in [Tall Tale Platform](docs/tall-tale-platform.md); Studio architecture and operations are in [Tall Tale Studio](docs/tall-tale-studio.md). The future recognition seam is in [vision helper boundary](docs/future-vision-helper.md). Animation ownership, scene contracts, assets, performance, and the development lab are indexed from [animation architecture](docs/animation/architecture.md).\n+Full setup, validation stages, output locations, clean-clone instructions, and troubleshooting are in [local development](docs/local-development.md) and [testing](docs/testing.md). Phase B-1 architecture, flags, desktop, PWA, protocol, API, security, test, and demonstration instructions are indexed from [the B-1 architecture](Development_Docs/AR/Phase_B1/B1_Architecture.md). Unified identity, invitations, libraries, authorization, history, and migration are in [Tall Tale Platform](docs/tall-tale-platform.md); Studio architecture and operations are in [Tall Tale Studio](docs/tall-tale-studio.md).\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\Phase_B1\\B1_Architecture.md\n+# Phase B-1 Architecture\n+\n+Status: implemented on `codex/phase-b1-foundation`  \n+Application version: `0.3.0-b1`  \n+Vision protocol: `1.0`  \n+Package schema: `1`\n+\n+## Shared product boundary\n+\n+B-1 retains one Next.js application. Browser, installed PWA, and Electron load the same routes, React components, Studio registry, progression service, Prisma models, Zod contracts, and API clients. Electron is a packaging and capability boundary, not another Player, Captain, or Studio implementation.\n+\n+```text\n+shared Next.js UI and story engine\n+  -> VisionPlatformAdapter\n+     -> MockVisionPlatformAdapter (authorized B-1 implementation)\n+     -> WebCompanionPlatformAdapter (truthful unavailable boundary)\n+     -> DesktopPlatformAdapter (restricted preload bridge + same server API)\n+  -> versioned /api/vision-* and /api/verification-attempts routes\n+  -> Prisma SQLite/MySQL persistence\n+  -> existing TaleVerificationRequest / submitVerification seam\n+  -> TaleSessionEvent idempotency and canonical progression\n+```\n+\n+Platform-specific code is confined to `src/vision/platform-adapters.ts` and `apps/desktop`. Shared UI never imports Electron, Node, localhost Companion assumptions, or generic native commands.\n+\n+## Runtime vertical slice\n+\n+1. Studio persists a `VisionWaypoint` and editable `VisionWaypointVersion` draft.\n+2. Publication validates the version, creates an immutable `VisionWaypointPublication`, SHA-256 package hash, and development `VisionBuildArtifact`.\n+3. A `visionWaypoint` story block stores the exact published version ID. Studio autosave recreates `StoryWaypointBinding` inside the same transaction as the story graph.\n+4. Entering the block creates the existing authoritative `TaleVerificationRequest` with provider `visionLocation`.\n+5. Player UI obtains a platform adapter and creates a persisted `VerificationAttempt`.\n+6. The deterministic mock records the governed progress states, result, evidence digest, and protocol message identifiers.\n+7. The result passes through `submitVerification`, which rechecks session, published tale version, current stage, provider, request, time, and idempotency key.\n+8. Only a verified current result appends canonical progression. Duplicate delivery returns the prior event and stale-stage delivery is rejected.\n+9. Captain diagnostics reads the persisted attempt and transitions; it does not invent client-only state.\n+\n+## Ownership map\n+\n+| Concern | Implementation |\n+| --- | --- |\n+| Domain and state machine | `src/vision/domain.ts` |\n+| Strict protocol 1.0 | `src/vision/protocol.ts` |\n+| Lifecycle/versioning/bindings | `src/vision/lifecycle.ts` |\n+| Attempts and story delivery | `src/vision/attempts.ts` |\n+| Adapters and selection | `src/vision/platform-adapters.ts` |\n+| Server-enforced flags | `src/vision/feature-flags.ts` |\n+| Permissions | `src/vision/permissions.ts` |\n+| Studio UI | `src/components/studio/VisionWaypoint*.tsx` |\n+| Player UI | `src/components/player/VisionScanControl.tsx` |\n+| Captain UI | `src/components/captain/VisionAttemptDiagnostics.tsx` |\n+| PWA | `public/manifest.webmanifest`, `public/sw.js`, `src/components/pwa` |\n+| Windows desktop | `apps/desktop`, `scripts/prepare-desktop.mjs` |\n+\n+## Feature flags\n+\n+Flags are typed and resolved server-side. Development/test defaults enable `vision_waypoints`, `vision_waypoint_library`, and `player_hold_to_scan`; production defaults disable the Vision path unless operators explicitly enable it. `creator_capture`, `vision_build_engine`, `shadow_verification`, `automatic_progression`, `automatic_vision_progression`, and `live_external_ar` default false. See `B1_Domain_Model.md` and `B1_Security_and_Privacy.md` for evolution boundaries.\n+\n+## Deployment and rollback\n+\n+SQLite migration `20260718050000_vision_waypoint_b1` and MySQL migration `0005_vision_waypoint_b1` are additive. Published versions and story bindings are retained when deprecated. Production rollback is: disable B-1 flags, roll back the application, and leave additive tables intact. Destructive database rollback requires a verified pre-migration backup and is not automated.\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\Phase_B1\\B1_Domain_Model.md\n+# Phase B-1 Domain Model\n+\n+## Stable authoring records\n+\n+`VisionWaypoint` is the stable identity: owner, name, description, model-agnostic type, tags, sharing scope, archive timestamp, and audit timestamps. Supported types are area arrival, exact landmark, viewpoint, object inspection, item pickup, sequence, and advanced. Sharing values already include private, crew, Tall Tale, community, and official without enabling later publication channels.\n+\n+`VisionWaypointVersion` is an explicit numbered version with parent lineage, lifecycle state, verification profile, versioned draft configuration, package schema, compatibility metadata, creator/publisher, and lifecycle timestamps. A published version cannot be edited. Later changes require a new draft; deprecation preserves existing story bindings.\n+\n+`VisionWaypointPublication` seals the version with a SHA-256 package hash, schema version, publisher, compatibility range, and status. B-1 artifacts use the `development-package://sha256/...` reference and never claim to contain a real model.\n+\n+`StoryWaypointBinding` pins one story block to one immutable version. It carries runtime mode, scan interaction, success event, retry/failure copy, Captain fallback policy, and offline behavior. B-1 permits only `DEVELOPMENT_MOCK`.\n+\n+## Attempts and supporting foundation\n+\n+`VerificationAttempt` persists story/stage/player/session/request/version identity, platform and adapter, scenario, protocol/message/idempotency identifiers, result/guidance/evidence digest, event-delivery state, Captain action, stale/duplicate guardrail outcomes, and timestamps. `VerificationAttemptTransition` is an ordered append-only state history.\n+\n+The state graph is:\n+\n+```text\n+IDLE -> ARMED -> CAPTURING -> CURATING_FRAMES -> RETRIEVING -> MATCHING\n+  -> LOCALIZING -> EVALUATING_SEQUENCE -> EVALUATING_SPECIAL_RULES\n+  -> VERIFIED | INSUFFICIENT | NOT_AT_TARGET | AMBIGUOUS | ERROR | CANCELLED\n+VERIFIED -> EVENT_DELIVERED -> RESULT_DISPLAYED -> CLOSED\n+```\n+\n+Invalid transitions throw `INVALID_ATTEMPT_TRANSITION`. Current-stage validation occurs again at delivery, not only when the attempt is created.\n+\n+Additional persistent entities establish future-compatible boundaries without implementing capture or inference: capture sessions, recording assets, regions, pose regions, hard-negative sets, build jobs/artifacts, certification runs, waypoint test runs, verification profiles, evidence bundles with retention/deletion fields, companion devices, and expiring pairing sessions whose secrets are hash-only.\n+\n+## B-1 fixture\n+\n+`prisma/seed.ts` idempotently ensures `B-1 Painted Lantern Waypoint` and the published tale `b1-vision-waypoint-demo`. Running `npx tsx prisma/seed.ts --ensure` never duplicates them and preserves accepted campaign progress.\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\Phase_B1\\B1_Protocol.md\n+# Phase B-1 Vision Protocol\n+\n+Protocol version `1.0` is implemented in `src/vision/protocol.ts` with strict Zod schemas. Every envelope contains `protocolVersion`, `messageType`, `messageId`, optional `requestId`, `sessionId`, ISO timestamp, governed sender identity, and a message-specific strict payload.\n+\n+The 27 messages cover Companion hello/capabilities/pairing/status; capture target and creator recording lifecycle; runtime scan start/progress/cancel/result/failure; build lifecycle; pause/resume; and diagnostic bundle request/availability. Capability negotiation describes capture, hotkeys, local inference, cloud build, offline packages, tray, diagnostic retention, hardware providers, supported protocol versions, and package schema versions.\n+\n+Governed error codes distinguish unavailable/pairing/origin/capture/package/model/hardware/stale-stage/validation/mock/internal failures. Payloads reject unknown fields. The serializer parses before emitting, and the deserializer validates both envelope and the payload selected by `messageType`.\n+\n+B-1 does not place raw frames or a model in protocol evidence. Runtime result evidence is a stable SHA-256 digest over deterministic, non-sensitive identifiers and the configured scenario. Contract tests round-trip every message family and reject invalid versions, malformed payloads, and extra properties.\n+\n+Future compatibility is explicit: a Companion must negotiate protocol and package schema versions; a package mismatch is an incompatibility, not a silent fallback. The future localhost service must validate paired origins and expiring hash-only credentials before accepting capture commands.\n*** End Patch";
text(await tools.apply_patch(patch));

const patch = "*** Begin Patch\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\Phase_B1\\B1_Desktop_Development.md\n+# Phase B-1 Windows Desktop Development\n+\n+## Choice and boundary\n+\n+Electron 41 packages the existing Next.js standalone application. This choice is recorded in ADR 0002. No route, React component, story engine, or domain model is forked for desktop.\n+\n+Install dependencies and run:\n+\n+```powershell\n+npm ci\n+npm run db:generate\n+npm run db:migrate\n+npx tsx prisma/seed.ts --ensure\n+npm run desktop:dev\n+```\n+\n+The development command runs Next at `http://127.0.0.1:3000`, waits for it, and opens Electron. The Navigate menu opens the same `/player`, `/captain`, and `/studio` routes used by browsers.\n+\n+## Restricted native bridge\n+\n+`contextIsolation`, renderer sandboxing, `nodeIntegration: false`, `webSecurity`, external-window denial, and same-origin navigation are enabled. `preload.cjs` exposes only one frozen method. `commands.cjs` allows exactly:\n+\n+- `vision.getCapabilities`\n+- `vision.prepareMockScan`\n+- `app.getDiagnostics`\n+\n+The bridge has no generic shell, process, filesystem, registry, network-proxy, or arbitrary IPC command. Identifiers and mock scenarios are allowlisted. The Desktop adapter uses this bridge only for governed desktop capability/preparation, then uses the same authenticated server attempt APIs as web/PWA.\n+\n+Run boundary tests with `npm run desktop:test`.\n+\n+## Production package\n+\n+```powershell\n+npm run desktop:build\n+```\n+\n+The command runs `next build` with `output: standalone`, stages only the standalone server, static assets, and public assets under `.desktop-bundle`, and invokes Electron Builder for an NSIS installer. The packaged main process forks that bundled server on loopback port 32178 and loads it after a bounded health wait. `.desktop-bundle` and installer output are generated artifacts and must not be committed.\n+\n+B-1 does not sign the installer, access a camera, register global hotkeys, add a tray agent, or install a localhost Companion. Those capabilities remain false and belong to later phases.\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\Phase_B1\\B1_PWA.md\n+# Phase B-1 PWA\n+\n+The shared application advertises `/manifest.webmanifest`, standalone display, theme colors, scalable icons, and Player/Captain/Studio shortcuts. `PwaRegistration` registers `/sw.js` and truthfully distinguishes online, offline, and waiting-update states.\n+\n+## Cache policy\n+\n+The service worker precaches only the offline truth page, manifest, and icons. Hashed `/_next/static/` assets may be cache-first. Navigation remains network-first and falls back only to `/offline`.\n+\n+The following are always fetched with `cache: no-store` and never placed in the application cache:\n+\n+- every `/api/` response;\n+- Studio and Captain pages;\n+- Player identity and live story pages;\n+- `/play/` sessions;\n+- Quartermaster/authentication and invitation routes.\n+\n+The offline page explicitly states that mutable story state, authentication, Studio data, Captain controls, and Vision verification are unavailable. It never presents cached mutable content as current. The update banner applies a waiting service worker only after user action.\n+\n+## Development verification\n+\n+Run `npm run build`, serve the production build, open browser application tools, confirm the manifest is installable, and inspect Cache Storage. `npm test -- pwa-policy` statically verifies sensitive route exclusions. Browser tests verify manifest/service-worker headers and offline truth behavior.\n+\n+The service worker version is `forever-treasure-b1-v1`. Change it only when the cached application-shell contract changes.\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\Phase_B1\\B1_Test_Plan.md\n+# Phase B-1 Test Plan\n+\n+## Automated layers\n+\n+| Layer | Coverage | Command |\n+| --- | --- | --- |\n+| Domain | configuration, scenarios, result mapping, transition graph | `npm test` |\n+| Protocol | all 27 messages, strict payloads, invalid versions | `npm test` |\n+| Flags/permissions | environment defaults and capability mapping | `npm test` |\n+| Adapters | deterministic HTTP path and truthful unavailable Companion | `npm test` |\n+| Story contracts | registry/provider and existing exact-once progression tests | `npm test` |\n+| PWA | sensitive cache exclusions and shell allowlist | `npm test` |\n+| Desktop bridge | command allowlist and input validation | `npm run desktop:test` |\n+| Migration | all SQLite migration SQL on a fresh database, fixture creation and re-ensure | `npm run validate` plus the B-1 migration proof |\n+| UI/E2E | Studio waypoint lifecycle, Player scan, duplicate/stale guards, Captain diagnostics, manifest/SW | `npm run test:e2e` |\n+| Regression | format, lint, strict TypeScript, asset validation, DB invariants, all E2E, production build/restarts | `npm run validate` |\n+\n+Shared-database mutations run in Chromium only. WebKit remains in the suite for read-only/accessibility/responsive checks. The deterministic duplicate scenario must show one accepted progression event and `duplicateResultRejected: true`; delayed/stale results must leave the current stage unchanged.\n+\n+## Migration proof\n+\n+The additive SQLite SQL can be applied in order to an empty disposable database with `prisma db execute`. The seed is run twice with `--ensure`; expected counts remain one waypoint, one demo tale, one binding, and one immutable publication. MySQL SQL is generated from the exact pre-B-1 schema and current MySQL schema, syntax reviewed, and applied only in a backed-up MySQL 8 environment.\n+\n+## Exit evidence\n+\n+`B1_Demonstration.md` records the mandatory browser, desktop-adapter, PWA, duplicate, stale, and Captain evidence. `B1_Completion_Report.md` records exact command results and any unverified operator-only item; no unchecked item is described as passed.\n*** Add File: C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt_Phase_B1\\Development_Docs\\AR\\Phase_B1\\B1_Security_and_Privacy.md\n+# Phase B-1 Security and Privacy\n+\n+## Implemented controls\n+\n+- Strict Zod schemas at lifecycle, binding, attempt, and protocol boundaries.\n+- Database-backed GM/Player sessions and existing CSRF checks on every mutation.\n+- Creator ownership checks for waypoint edits; publisher capability for publish/deprecate; Captain capability for diagnostics; session membership for Player attempts.\n+- Published-version immutability and exact version IDs in story bindings.\n+- Cryptographically random IDs, SHA-256 package/evidence digests, and hash-only future pairing secrets.\n+- Structured audit events for creation, edit, publication, deprecation, binding changes, attempts, and manual Captain actions.\n+- `Cache-Control: no-store` on APIs and a service-worker deny boundary for every sensitive/mutable route.\n+- CSP, clickjacking denial, MIME sniffing denial, same-origin referrer policy, and camera/microphone/geolocation/USB/serial denial.\n+- Restricted desktop command allowlist with sandboxed renderer; no generic shell or filesystem bridge.\n+- Evidence-bundle expiry/deletion fields; B-1 stores no frames, thumbnails, camera media, game data, or pairing secrets.\n+\n+## Threat assumptions\n+\n+| Threat | B-1 treatment |\n+| --- | --- |\n+| Malicious webpage calls future Companion | Web adapter is unavailable; future contract requires paired allowed origin and expiry |\n+| Modified package | Publication stores SHA-256 hash, schema, and compatibility metadata; later runtime must verify it |\n+| Duplicate result | Stable story delivery idempotency key returns prior event; duplicate outcome is persisted |\n+| Delayed/stale result | Current session stage and pending request are rechecked before delivery |\n+| Unauthorized mutation | Capability, ownership, CSRF, and server flag checks precede writes |\n+| Desktop command abuse | Only three validated commands cross the context-isolated preload |\n+| Service-worker leakage | API/auth/Studio/Captain/Player/play routes are network-only/no-store |\n+\n+## Privacy truth\n+\n+The phrase “Inspect Surroundings” is a story interaction, not a claim of real capture. The UI identifies the deterministic mock. `capture: false` is returned by web and desktop capabilities. Real capture, model inference, OCR, object detection, game process inspection, memory reading, packet inspection, overlays, anti-cheat interaction, and automated gameplay are out of scope and absent.\n*** End Patch";
text(await tools.apply_patch(patch));

\"#/components/schemas/CreateWaypoint\" } } } }\n+      responses: { \"201\": { description: Created }, \"403\": { $ref: \"#/components/responses/Forbidden\" } }\n+ description: Waypoint detail }, \"404\": { description: Not found } } }\n+    patch:\n+      summary: Update stable metadata\n+      security: [{ cookieSession: [], csrf: [] }]\n+      requestBody: { required: true, content: { application/json: { schema: { $ref: \"#/components/schemas/WaypointMetadata\" } } } }\n+      responses: { \"200\": { description: Updated }, \"403\": { $ref: \"#/components/responses/Forbidden\" } }\n+  /api/vision-waypoints/{waypointId}/archive:\n+    post: { summary: Archive without deleting versions or bindings, parameters: [{ $ref: \"#/components/parameters/WaypointId\" }], security: [{ cookieSession: [], csrf: [] }], responses: { \"200\": { description: Archived } } }\n+  /api/vision-waypoints/{waypointId}/versions:\n+    parameters: [{ $ref: \"#/components/parameters/WaypointId\" }]\n+    get: { summary: List latest draft and published versions, responses: { \"200\": { description: Version list } } }\n+    post:\n+      summary: Create a draft, optionally from a published parent\n+      security: [{ cookieSession: [], csrf: [] }]\n+      requestBody: { content: { application/json: { schema: { type: object, additionalProperties: false, properties: { parentVersionId: { type: string } } } } } }\n+      responses: { \"201\": { description: Draft created }, \"409\": { description: Draft already exists } }\n+  /api/vision-waypoints/{waypointId}/usage:\n+    get: { summary: List immutable story bindings, parameters: [{ $ref: \"#/components/parameters/WaypointId\" }], responses: { \"200\": { description: Usage list } } }\n+  /api/vision-waypoint-versions/{versionId}:\n+    get: { summary: Get an owned version, parameters: [{ $ref: \"#/components/parameters/VersionId\" }], responses: { \"200\": { description: Version detail } } }\n+  /api/vision-waypoint-versions/{versionId}/draft:\n+    patch:\n+      summary: Replace strict configuration on an unpublished draft\n+      parameters: [{ $ref: \"#/components/parameters/VersionId\" }]\n+      security: [{ cookieSession: [], csrf: [] }]\n+      requestBody: { required: true, content: { application/json: { schema: { $ref: \"#/components/schemas/DraftConfiguration\" } } } }\n+      responses: { \"200\": { description: Updated }, \"409\": { description: Published version is immutable } }\n+  /api/vision-waypoint-versions/{versionId}/publish:\n+    post:\n+      summary: Seal a deterministic development package\n+      parameters: [{ $ref: \"#/components/parameters/VersionId\" }]\n+      security: [{ cookieSession: [], csrf: [] }]\n+      requestBody: { content: { application/json: { schema: { type: object, additionalProperties: false, properties: { scenario: { $ref: \"#/components/schemas/MockScenario\" } } } } } }\n+      responses: { \"200\": { description: Immutable publication with SHA-256 hash }, \"409\": { description: Already sealed } }\n+  /api/vision-waypoint-versions/{versionId}/deprecate:\n+    post: { summary: Deprecate while retaining bindings, parameters: [{ $ref: \"#/components/parameters/VersionId\" }], security: [{ cookieSession: [], csrf: [] }], responses: { \"200\": { description: Deprecated } } }\n+  /api/story-waypoint-bindings:\n+    post: { summary: Bind an exact published version to an owned story block, /api/verification-attempts:\n+    post:\n+      summary: Player creates a persisted deterministic attempt\n+      security: [{ playerSession: [], csrf: [] }]\n+      requestBody: { required: true, } }\n+    get:\n+      summary: Captain lists session attempt diagnostics\n+      parameters: [{ name: sessionId, in: query, required: true, schema: { type: string } }]\n+      responses: { \"200\": { description: Persisted attempt list } }\n+  /api/verification-attempts/{attemptId}:\n+    get: { summary: Read an authorized attempt and transition history, parameters: [{ $ref: \"#/components/parameters/AttemptId\" }], responses: { \"200\": { description: Attempt detail } } }\n+  /api/verification-attempts/{attemptId}/cancel:\n+    post: { summary: Player cancels a nonterminal attempt, parameters: [{ $ref: \"#/components/parameters/AttemptId\" }], security: [{ playerSession: [], csrf: [] }], responses: { \"200\": { description: Cancelled } } }\n+  /api/verification-attempts/{attemptId}/mock-result:\n+    post:\n+      summary: Run the authorized deterministic B-1 verifier and deliver through story idempotency\n+      parameters: [{ $ref: \"#/components/parameters/AttemptId\" }]\n+      security: [{ playerSession: [], csrf: [] }]\n+      requestBody: { content: { application/json: { schema: { type: object, additionalProperties: false, properties: { scenario: { $ref: \"#/components/schemas/MockScenario\" } } } } } }\n+      responses: { \"200\": { description: Terminal result and delivery state }, \"409\": { description: Cancelled or stale } }\n+components:\n+  securitySchemes:\n+    cookieSession: { type: apiKey, in: cookie, name: forever_gm }\n+    playerSession: { type: apiKey, in: cookie, name: tall_tale_player }\n+ in: path, required: true, schema: { type: string } }\n+    AttemptId: { name: attemptId, in: path, required: true, schema: { type: string } }\n+  responses:\n+    Unauthorized: { description: Authenticated session required }\n+    Forbidden: { description: Capability, ownership, feature flag, or CSRF check failed }\n+  schemas:\n+    CreateWaypoint:\n+      allOf:\n+        - { $ref: \"#/components/schemas/WaypointMetadata\" }\n+        - type: object\n+          required: [name, description, type, locationTags, sharingScope, verificationProfile]\n+          properties: { verificationProfile: { enum: [BALANCED, STRICT, STORY_CRITICAL, CUSTOM] } }\n+    WaypointMetadata:\n+      type: object\n+      additionalProperties: false\n+      properties:\n+        name: { type: string, minLength: 2, maxLength: 160 }\n+        description: { type: string, maxLength: 4000 }\n+        type: { enum: [AREA_ARRIVAL, EXACT_LANDMARK, VIEWPOINT, OBJECT_INSPECTION, ITEM_PICKUP, SEQUENCE, ADVANCED] }\n+        locationTags: { type: array, maxItems: 64, items: { type: string, maxLength: 80 } }\n+        sharingScope: { enum: [PRIVATE, CREW, TALL_TALE, COMMUNITY, OFFICIAL] }\n+    DraftConfiguration:\n+      type: object\n+      additionalProperties: false\n+      required: [schemaVersion, waypointType, verificationProfile, scanInteraction, creatorGuidancePreferences, captainFallbackPolicy, acceptedPoseConfiguration, stableRegionConfiguration, hardNegativeRequirement, storyPurposeMetadata, buildPreference]\n+      properties:\n+        schemaVersion: { const: 1 }\n+        waypointType: { enum: [AREA_ARRIVAL, EXACT_LANDMARK, VIEWPOINT, OBJECT_INSPECTION, ITEM_PICKUP, SEQUENCE, ADVANCED] }\n+        verificationProfile: { enum: [BALANCED, STRICT, STORY_CRITICAL, CUSTOM] }\n+        scanInteraction: { type: object }\n+        creatorGuidancePreferences: { type: object }\n+        captainFallbackPolicy: { type: object }\n+        acceptedPoseConfiguration: { type: object }\n+        stableRegionConfiguration: { type: object }\n+        hardNegativeRequirement: { type: object }\n+        storyPurposeMetadata: { type: object }\n+        buildPreference: { enum: [LOCAL, CLOUD_ASSISTED, UNDECIDED] }\n+    CreateAttempt:\n+      type: object\n+      additionalProperties: false\n+      required: [sessionId, blockId, waypointVersionId, scenario, platform, adapterType]\n+      properties:\n+        sessionId: { type: string }\n+        blockId: { type: string }\n+        waypointVersionId: { type: string }\n+        scenario: { $ref: \"#/components/schemas/MockScenario\" }\n+        platform: { enum: [WEB, PWA, DESKTOP] }\n+        adapterType: { enum: [MOCK, WEB_COMPANION, DESKTOP] }\n+    MockScenario:\n+      enum: [verified, insufficient, not_at_target, ambiguous, system_error, delayed_verified, cancelled, stale_stage, duplicate_result_delivery]\n*** End Patch";

 expect(await retryAccept.json()).toMatchObject({ idempotent: true, playthroughId: created.playthroughId });\n\n  const revocable = await captain.post(\"/api/captain/playthroughs\", {\n    headers: { \"x-csrf-token\": captainCsrf },\n    data: {\n      taleId: fork.id,\n      versionId: versionTwo.id,\n      voyageName: `Revocation Proof ${crypto.randomUUID().slice(0, 8)}`,\n      captainMode: \"HYBRID\",\n      hints: \"DISABLED\",\n      sideQuests: false,\n      accessibilityDefaults: {},\n      expiresInHours: 24,\n      accountRequired: false,\n      maxRedemptions: 1,\n      players: [{ displayName: \"Revocation Mariner\", crewRole: \"Lookout\" }],\n    },\n  });\n  expect(revocable.status(), await revocable.text()).toBe(201);\n  const revocableBody = (await revocable.json()) as {\n    invitations: Array<{ id: string; link: string }>;\n  };\n  const original = revocableBody.invitations[0];\n  const replace = await captain.post(`/api/captain/invitations/${original.id}`, {\n    headers: { \"x-csrf-token\": captainCsrf },\n    data: { action: \"replace\", extendHours: 24 },\n  });\n  await expectOk(replace);\n  const replacement = (await replace.json()) as { replacement: { id: string; link: string } };\n  const staleContext = await browser.newContext();\n  const stalePage = await staleContext.newPage();\n  await stalePage.goto(original.link);\n  await expect(stalePage).toHaveURL(/\\/player\\/invitation\\?state=invalid$/);\n  await expect(stalePage.getByRole(\"heading\", { name: \"The seal will not open\" })).toBeVisible();\n  await staleContext.close();\n\n  const revoke = await captain.post(`/api/captain/invitations/${replacement.replacement.id}`, {\n    headers: { \"x-csrf-token\": captainCsrf },\n    data: { action: \"revoke\" },\n  });\n  await expectOk(revoke);\n  const revokedContext = await browser.newContext();\n  const revokedPage = await revokedContext.newPage();\n  await revokedPage.goto(replacement.replacement.link);\n  await expect(revokedPage).toHaveURL(/\\/player\\/invitation\\?state=invalid$/);\n  await expect(revokedPage.getByRole(\"heading\", { name: \"The seal will not open\" })).toBeVisible();\n  const lifecycleLibrary = await captain.get(\"/api/captain/library\");\n  await expectOk(lifecycleLibrary);\n  const lifecycleBody = (await lifecycleLibrary.json()) as {\n    invitations: Array<{ id: string; status: string; replacementId: string | null }>;\n  };\n  expect(lifecycleBody.invitations).toContainEqual(\n    expect.objectContaining({ id: original.id, status: \"REPLACED\", replacementId: replacement.replacement.id }),\n  );\n  expect(lifecycleBody.invitations).toContainEqual(\n    expect.objectContaining({ id: replacement.replacement.id, status: \"REVOKED\" }),\n  );\n  await revokedContext.close();\n\n  await captainContext.close();\n  await playerContext.close();\n  await strangerContext.close();\n});\n\r\nimport { expect, test, type APIRequestContext, type Page } from \"@playwright/test\";\r\nimport sharp from \"sharp\";\r\n\r\ntest.skip(({ browserName }) => browserName !== \"chromium\", \"The version-pinned mutation workflow runs once.\");\r\n\r\nconst unique = (label: string) => `${label}-${crypto.randomUUID()}`;\r\n\r\nasync function current(request: APIRequestContext, sessionId: string) {\r\n  const response = await request.get(`/api/play/sessions/${sessionId}`);\r\n  expect(response.ok()).toBeTruthy();\r\n  return response.json() as Promise<{\r\n    session: { status: string; versionId: string };\r\n    block: { id: string; blockType: string; title: string } | null;\r\n    pendingVerification: { id: string; providerType: string } | null;\r\n    inventory: string[];\r\n  }>;\r\n}\r\n\r\nasync function dragLibraryBlock(page: Page, name: string) {\r\n  const source = page.locator(\".block-library article\").filter({ has: page.getByText(name, { exact: true }) });\r\n  const target = page.locator(\".timeline-drop\").last();\r\n  await target.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded();\r\n  await source.scrollIntoViewIfNeeded();\r\n  const [sourceBox, targetBox] = await Promise.all([source.boundingBox(), target.boundingBox()]);\r\n  expect(sourceBox).toBeTruthy();\r\n  expect(targetBox).toBeTruthy();\r\n  const before = await page.locator(\".timeline-block\").count();\r\n  await page.mouse.move(sourceBox!.x + sourceBox!.width / 2, sourceBox!.y + sourceBox!.height / 2);\r\n  await page.mouse.down();\r\n  await page.mouse.move(targetBox!.x + targetBox!.width / 2, targetBox!.y + targetBox!.height / 2, { steps: 12 });\r\n  await page.mouse.up();\r\n  await expect(page.locator(\".timeline-block\")).toHaveCount(before + 1);\r\n}\r\n\r\ntest(\"published Studio tale completes through player, Captain, and helper contracts\", async ({ browser }) => {\r\n  const playerContext = await browser.newContext();\r\n  const captainContext = await browser.newContext();\r\n  const strangerContext = await browser.newContext();\r\n  const player = playerContext.request;\r\n  const captain = captainContext.request;\r\n\r\n  const catalogResponse = await player.get(\"/api/tales\");\r\n  expect(catalogResponse.ok()).toBeTruthy();\r\n  const catalog = (await catalogResponse.json()) as {\r\n    tales: Array<{ slug: string; title: string; version: string }>;\r\n  };\r\n  expect(catalog.tales).toContainEqual(expect.objectContaining({ slug: \"development-studio-voyage\", version: \"1.0\" }));\r\n\r\n  const startResponse = await player.post(\"/api/tales/development-studio-voyage/start\", {\r\n    data: { ownerLabel: \"Playwright Crew\" },\r\n  });\r\n  expect(startResponse.status()).toBe(201);\r\n  const started = (await startResponse.json()) as { sessionId: string };\r\n  const sessionId = started.sessionId;\r\n  expect((await strangerContext.request.get(`/api/play/sessions/${sessionId}`)).status()).toBe(401);\r\n\r\n  let state = await current(player, sessionId);\r\n  const pinnedVersionId = state.session.versionId;\r\n  expect(state.block).toMatchObject({ blockType: \"narrative\", title: \"The Lantern Wakes\" });\r\n\r\n  let response = await player.post(`/api/play/sessions/${sessionId}`, {\r\n    data: { action: \"continue\", idempotencyKey: unique(\"narrative\") },\r\n  });\r\n  expect(response.ok()).toBeTruthy();\r\n  state = await current(player, sessionId);\r\n  expect(state.pendingVerification?.providerType).toBe(\"textAnswer\");\r\n\r\n  response = await player.post(`/api/play/sessions/${sessionId}`, {\r\n    data: { action: \"answer\", answer: \"anchor\", idempotencyKey: unique(\"wrong-answer\") },\r\n  });\r\n  expect(response.ok()).toBeTruthy();\r\n  expect((await response.json()).accepted).toBe(false);\r\n  expect((await current(player, sessionId)).block?.blockType).toBe(\"riddle\");\r\n\r\n  response = await player.post(`/api/play/sessions/${sessionId}`, {\r\n    data: { action: \"answer\", answer: \"  LANTERN  \", idempotencyKey: unique(\"right-answer\") },\r\n  });\r\n  expect(response.ok()).toBeTruthy();\r\n  state = await current(player, sessionId);\r\n  expect(state.block?.blockType).toBe(\"captainApproval\");\r\n  expect(state.pendingVerification?.providerType).toBe(\"captainManual\");\r\n\r\n  const loginResponse = await captain.post(\"/api/gm/login\", {\r\n    data: { username: process.env.GM_USERNAME, [REDACTED: SECRET ASSIGNMENT] },\r\n  });\r\n  expect(loginResponse.ok()).toBeTruthy();\r\n  const { csrfToken } = (await loginResponse.json()) as { csrfToken: string };\r\n\r\n  const pairResponse = await captain.post(\"/api/helper/pair\", {\r\n    headers: { \"x-csrf-token\": csrfToken },\r\n    data: { sessionId, deviceId: \"playwright-helper\" },\r\n  });\r\n  expect(pairResponse.ok()).toBeTruthy();\r\n  const { token, pairingId } = (await pairResponse.json()) as { token: string; pairingId: string };\r\n  const helperStatus = await captain.get(\"/api/helper/status\", { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } });\r\n  expect(helperStatus.ok()).toBeTruthy();\r\n  expect(await helperStatus.json()).toMatchObject({\r\n    pairing: { id: pairingId, status: \"ACTIVE\" },\r\n    scope: { sessionId, publishedVersionId: pinnedVersionId, currentBlockId: state.block!.id },\r\n  });\r\n  const pending = state.pendingVerification!;\r\n  const wrongVersionResponse = await captain.post(\"/api/helper/verification\", {\r\n    headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` },\r\n    data: {\r\n      schemaVersion: 1,\r\n      eventId: unique(\"helper-event\"),\r\n      idempotencyKey: unique(\"helper-idempotency\"),\r\n      eventType: \"verification.observation\",\r\n      providerType: \"captainManual\",\r\n      providerInstanceId: \"playwright-helper\",\r\n      sessionId,\r\n      publishedVersionId: `${pinnedVersionId}-wrong`,\r\n      blockId: state.block!.id,\r\n      verificationRequestId: pending.id,\r\n      observedAt: new Date().toISOString(),\r\n      result: \"match\",\r\n      confidence: 0.99,\r\n      evidence: { test: true },\r\n    },\r\n  });\r\n  expect(wrongVersionResponse.status()).toBe(409);\r\n  expect((await wrongVersionResponse.json()).code).toBe(\"wrongVersion\");\r\n\r\n  const approval = await unique(\"captain-approve\") },\r\n  });\r\n  expect(approval.ok()).toBeTruthy();\r\n  state = await current(player, sessionId);\r\n  expect(state.block?.blockType).toBe(\"chapterComplete\");\r\n
